A struggle ensues as others try to protect the survivors, and return them to their original times and places.

sent0: Jack and Hank are two friends who teach literature at the local university. They regularly meet for dinner as a quartet with their respective wives, Terry (married to Jack) and Edith (married to Hank). Edith and Jack embark on an affair, which Terry starts to become suspicious about but doesn’t outright confront her husband either. Instead, she gets closer to Hank, who is himself a womanizer. When Jack learns his wife has slept with Hank, he does not erupt in anger as Terry expected, and his indifferent reaction upsets Terry. The two couples must contend with the entanglements of their deceits and betrayals.
sent1: In the heart of Paris, Madame Vernier is shaken to her core when she uncovers the truth about her husband, who has been entangled in a passionate affair with the enchanting dancer Lilette. Heartbroken yet determined, she seeks solace and distraction, turning to her friend Eva for advice. Inspired by Eva's encouragement to reclaim her own autonomy, Madame Vernier embarks on a flirtation of her own, meeting Marcel Douzet, a strikingly handsome yet impoverished bookbinder. To safeguard her identity, she adopts a false name and masquerades as a maid, immersing herself in an elaborate charade. This deception leads her to a comical yet revealing encounter where she must pretend to be her own servant, blurring the lines between reality and pretense. However, as her feelings for Marcel deepen, Madame Vernier grapples with her sense of loyalty and the implications of her actions. Ultimately, after a tumultuous internal struggle, she chooses to return to her husband, realizing that the familiar, despite its betrayals, still holds an undeniable grip on her heart, illustrating the complexity of love, loyalty, and the human experience of forgiveness.
hard_neg: Albrecht Froben, a devoted husband to Octavia, finds himself ensnared in a forbidden love for his neighbor, Äls Flodéen, whose health is deteriorating due to a vicious illness. As a deadly epidemic sweeps through the town, Albrecht takes it upon himself to rescue Äls's daughter from the impending danger. His noble intentions, however, lead him to contract typhoid, forcing him into quarantine at a local hospital. Meanwhile, Octavia, sensing the growing bond between her husband and Äls, faces a heart-wrenching dilemma. In a profound act of selflessness, she disguises herself as Albrecht and rides past Äls’s home daily, speaking words of comfort and hope to the ailing woman, whose bed is positioned by the window. Through this act of love and sacrifice, Octavia seeks to bolster Äls's spirit. Upon Albrecht’s return from the hospital, a poignant dream visits Äls, where she converses with a projection of Albrecht, leading her to a heartbreaking revelation: she cannot accept a love fueled by another's betrayal and, with a heavy heart, chooses to embrace death over enduring life entangled in such complex emotions. In the end, Albrecht is left to reconcile with Octavia, their bond mending in the wake of loss, illustrating the intricate dance of love, sacrifice, and the painful acceptance of fate.

In a profound act of selflessness, she disguises herself as Albrecht and rides past Äls’s home daily, speaking words of comfort and hope to the ailing woman, whose bed is positioned by the window. Through this act of love and sacrifice, Octavia seeks to bolster Äls's spirit. Upon Albrecht’s return from the hospital, a poignant dream visits Äls, where she converses with a projection of Albrecht, leading her to a heartbreaking revelation: she cannot accept a love fueled by another's betrayal and, with a heavy heart, chooses to embrace death over enduring life entangled in such complex emotions. In the end, Albrecht is left to reconcile with Octavia, their bond mending in the wake of loss, illustrating the intricate dance of love, sacrifice, and the painful acceptance of fate.

sent0: Jack and Hank are two friends who teach literature at the local university. They regularly meet for dinner as a quartet with their respective wives, Terry (married to Jack) and Edith (married to Hank). Edith and Jack embark on an affair, which Terry starts to become suspicious about but doesn’t outright confront her husband either. Instead, she gets closer to Hank, who is himself a womanizer. When Jack learns his wife has slept with Hank, he does not erupt in anger as Terry expected, and his indifferent reaction upsets Terry. The two couples must contend with the entanglements of their deceits and betrayals.
sent1: In Paris  Madame Vernier discovers that her husband has been having an affair with the dancer Lilette. On the advice of her friend Eva, she decides to have a flirtation of her own and encounters  Marcel Douzet, a handsome but poor bookbinder. To conceal her identity from him, she uses a false name and pretends to be a maid. This leads to an encounter where she has to pretend to be her own servant. Ultimately, she chooses to return to her husband.
hard_neg: Albrecht Froben, though married to Octavia, falls in love with his neighbor, Äls Flodéen.  She, however, is slowly dying from a debilitating disease.  During an epidemic, Albrecht goes to bring her daughter to safety but he catches typhoid and is quarantined in hospital. Octavia, realising the love match, and hearing that Äls is now bedridden and dying, dresses up as him and rides by her gates every day to keep her spirits up—her bed is next to the window.  Albrecht returns.  Äls has a dream in which she talks to her projection of Albrecht and concludes that she does not wish to take part in this union and accepts death.  Albrecht is reconciled with his wife.

sent0: In the bustling underbelly of the city, two street gangsters, Armand and Marie, find themselves entwined in a passionate affair that fuels their reckless ambitions. Marie, with her fiery spirit and cunning mind, convinces Armand and their criminal associates to embark on a daring heist at the lavish residence of Pierre Marcel, a brilliant yet aloof scientist whose wealth is as notable as his reputation. As the gang storms the opulent home, their plans unravel when the police unexpectedly arrive, forcing Pierre to make a split-second decision that could change everything; he hides Armand, who bravely intervenes to protect him from the clutches of a fellow gang member wielding a knife, sustaining a grave wound in the process. During Armand's arduous recovery, Pierre, feeling an odd sense of gratitude and perhaps intrigue, introduces Armand to the enchanting Jean D'Arcy, a woman whose beauty and charm seem to heal more than just physical scars, and to Armand’s surprise, he finds himself captivated by her. This unexpected bond sows the seeds of jealousy in Marie’s heart, igniting her desire for revenge against Pierre, whom she blames for the shift in Armand’s affections. As the plot thickens, Marie and Pierre’s paths cross in a fateful encounter, and a complicated romance burgeons, leading to their hasty marriage while Armand is away in London pursuing a fresh start. On the night of their wedding, with a mixture of triumph and vengeance, Marie reveals her true identity as an Apache, claiming that her revenge is complete, as she dashes back into Armand's waiting arms. The celebration is abruptly shattered when Marie's treacherous gang members, having aided her in her deceit, turn on her and fire shots in a chaotic scene. In the aftermath, Marie is left fighting for her life, and as she recuperates, Pierre makes the heart-wrenching decision to leave for America, seeking a divorce to allow her the freedom to reunite with the man she truly loves, Armand.
sent1: In the competitive world of finance, Howard Brubaker, a Wall Street broker, finds himself trapped in a loveless marriage with his wife Phyllis, who harbors no affection for him. As fate would have it, he crosses paths with Catherine, the stunning wife of his boss Ted Gunther, who is notorious for his infidelity and emotional neglect towards her. One fateful evening after Ted unexpectedly promotes Howard, he invites him to a lavish house party teeming with available women, goading Howard to seize the opportunity for adventure. Hesitant but compelled by circumstance, Howard approaches Catherine, who surprisingly reciprocates his interest, and together they escape the confines of the party to embark on a night of thrilling escapades throughout the city. As they share stories and laughter, both Howard and Catherine begin to unearth the hollowness of their respective marriages, realizing that they have long been unfulfilled and yearning for genuine connection. With a spark igniting between them, they make a pact to leave their old lives behind and plan a daring escape to Paris the following evening. However, the thrilling twist comes when Ted, oblivious to Howard's intentions, remains immersed in his pursuits at the party, unaware that the man destined to whisk away his wife is none other than his newest protégé.
hard_neg: Regina Lambert, a British newlywed, has just returned to her Paris home with dreams of a happy life alongside her husband Charles. However, upon arriving, her excitement quickly turns to horror as she discovers their once vibrant apartment is now stripped bare, and the chilling presence of French police fills the space, turning her world upside down. The shocking news hits hard: Charles has been murdered, leaving Regina to grapple with the tragic reality that he had liquidated their possessions, amounting to $1.8 million, and vanished with the money. As the investigation unfolds, Regina encounters Joshua, a mysterious stranger she briefly met during her recent vacation, who becomes an unexpected ally in her quest for answers. Together, they delve into the murky depths of Charles's life, piecing together clues about his deceitful dealings, all while evading three menacing figures who appear to have sinister intentions. The stakes rise as Regina realizes that uncovering the truth about her husband may not only expose the dark secrets he kept but also put her own life in jeopardy, blurring the lines between trust and betrayal in her quest for justice.

sent0: Twenty-five-year-old Lucile is the beautiful mistress to Charles, a wealthy, kind-hearted businessman who provides for all her material needs, but for whom she has no true love. When she meets a charming young man her own age, Antoine, she falls in love. He finds her a menial job in a publishing firm, but she can not or will not hold it down. Soon she becomes pregnant with his child. But Charles helps her through her crisis by funding her abortion – against the wishes of Antoine, who nevertheless accepts, even though he planned on moving out of his bachelor flat, the three of them into a soulless concrete block, money being short. In the aftermath, her feelings for the younger Antoine fade. Eventually, she returns to the good-hearted businessman who has patiently waited for her.
sent1: In a bustling city, a wealthy heiress named Genevieve finds herself captivated by the charm of a local carpenter named Thomas, whose humble ways and romantic spirit stand in stark contrast to her affluent lifestyle. Realizing the gulf between their worlds, her father resorts to hiring a hypnotist, intending to manipulate her heart towards an aristocratic match that he deems more suitable. However, the spell is broken when Genevieve and Thomas, fueled by their deep connection, elope to a forgotten countryside where they dream of a life free from societal constraints, attempting to reconnect with simpler, more authentic living. Their idyllic escape, however, is short-lived, as the realities of survival drive them back into the city's embrace, where they struggle to make ends meet in a modest apartment. When their funds dwindle, they find themselves scraping by as laborers in the bustling market, which further strains their relationship. In a surprising twist, Genevieve's former betrothed orchestrates a plan to restore her to her former life of comfort, leaving Genevieve to confront her feelings and choices, ultimately guiding the couple back to a middle-class existence, albeit with a newfound understanding of love and sacrifice.
hard_neg: Reisiger, a dedicated journalist with a relentless pursuit for stories, roams various corners of the world, driven by his ambition and the thrill of uncovering the unknown. During one of his assignments, he meets a captivating woman named Franziska, and their whirlwind romance culminates in marriage after she unexpectedly becomes pregnant. Despite their initial happiness, Reisiger's insatiable wanderlust drives him to leave Franziska time and again, prioritizing his career over their family. One fateful moment arrives when a dying colleague urges him to return home, leading him to contemplate his choices. However, as World War II ignites across Europe, Reisiger is compelled to depart once more, leaving Franziska behind clutching their child, who must now wait for an uncertain future, embodying the painful struggle between personal ambition and the ties of love and loyalty, while she grapples with the profound isolation of waiting in a world turned upside down.

Finally, their issues are resolved through the machinations of her spurned would-be suitor, and they resume a middle-class lifestyle.

sent0: Horacia Somorostro (Charo Santos-Concio) was released in 1997 after being imprisoned for a crime she did not commit. Although Somorostro reunites with her daughter, she learns that her husband is now deceased and her son is missing. She realized that a thing remains unchanged - the power and privilege of the elite. This belief was cemented when Somorostro later found out that her former rich lover, Rodrigo Trinidad (Michael de Mesa) was the one who framed her for a crime. She learns that Trinidad is forced to stay within his house like his friends due to kidnapping incidents targeting the rich. To the ruling class, the kidnappings are the most serious problem in the country's history. Somorostro begins to plot her revenge amidst the crisis.
sent1: Julian (Kim Joohyuk) has lived a life cultivated through the allure of wealth, charming rich women to finance his extravagant lifestyle. Yet, as the consequences of his past choices catch up with him, he finds himself drowning in debts incurred from a reckless expansion of his enterprise, leaving him vulnerable to violent repercussions if he fails to settle his accounts within a month. In a desperate gamble for survival, he concocts a nefarious scheme to impersonate the long-lost sibling of a wealthy heiress, all in hopes of securing her vast inheritance through nefarious means. As he ensnares Min (Moon Geun-young), the blind yet iron-willed heiress, he discovers an unexpected bond blossoming between them, transforming his cold intentions into genuine affection. However, as the weight of his deceit becomes unbearable, compounded by the re-emergence of Min's life-threatening illness, Julian grapples with a profound guilt that threatens to consume him, forcing him to confront the very nature of love, responsibility, and redemption in a world where morality is often sacrificed for survival.
hard_neg: Paul Exben is a remarkable success story: a partner in one of Paris's most prestigious law firms, boasting a substantial salary, a spacious home, a glamorous wife, and two sons who could easily grace the pages of a high-end fashion catalog. However, his world shatters when he uncovers his wife Sarah's affair with Greg Kremer, a local photographer. In a moment of passionate fury, Paul commits a fatal mistake. As he stands over the lifeless body of his wife's lover, Paul comprehends that his once-perfect life is irreversibly shattered. But rather than succumb to his grim circumstances, Paul decides to seize a radical opportunity. sent0: This third novel of Updike's Rabbit series examines the life of Harry "Rabbit" Angstrom, a one-time high school basketball star, who has reached a paunchy middle-age without relocating from Brewer, Pennsylvania, the poor, fictional city of his birth. Harry and Janice, his wife of 22 years, live comfortably, having inherited her late father's Toyota dealership. He is indeed rich, but Harry's persistent problems—his wife's drinking, his troubled son's schemes, his libido, and spectres from his past—complicate life. Having achieved an opulent lifestyle that would have embarrassed his working-class parents, Harry is not greedy, but neither is he ever quite satisfied. Harry has grown smitten with a country-club friend's young wife. He worries about Nelson, his indecisive son, a student at Kent State University. Throughout the book, Harry wonders whether his former lover, Ruth, ever gave birth to their illegitimate daughter.
sent1: Bill Babbitt supported the death penalty, until it came knocking at his door. Bill fondly recalls early life with his brother Manny, a relationship filled with love and adventure that was abruptly altered by a tragic car accident, irrevocably changing Manny's life. The innocence of their childhood gives way to the harsh realities of war when Manny enlists, only to return from Vietnam burdened by deep-seated mental health issues. The toll of the conflict manifests itself in bouts of paranoia, pushing Manny to the fringes of society where he struggles with his demons. Concerned for his brother's well-being, Bill extends an invitation for Manny to live with his family in Sacramento, hoping to provide a safe haven. A sudden and shocking turn of events thrusts Bill into a moral quandary when he discovers that Manny, in a moment of desperation, has committed a crime. Bill is faced with an excruciating decision that tests his love for his brother against his ethical beliefs, each choice weighted with the heavy consequences of familial loyalty and societal judgment. Through this turmoil, the narrative explores the complexities of brotherhood, the ramifications of mental illness, and the haunting question of what is truly right when love and law collide.
hard_neg: In a wealthy Chicago suburb during the early 1960s, middle-class Jimmy Reardon navigates the complexities of youth while feeling the weight of societal expectations. He spends his days in the company of his affluent best friend Fred Roberts, who represents the life Jimmy feels he should aspire to, but instead finds himself entangled in a relationship with Fred's snobby girlfriend, Denise Hunter. While wrestling with his own identity, Jimmy dabbles in writing poetry, seeking solace in words while grappling with the discontent brewing within him about his future. His parents, envisioning a path for him that mirrors his father's business education, impose their will, leaving Jimmy longing for independence, especially from a life he feels suffocated by. As he dreams of escaping to Hawaii with his wealthy yet chaste girlfriend, Lisa Bentwright, the tide of his reality shifts when he is tasked with driving home his divorced mother's friend, Joyce Fickett, who unexpectedly seduces him. This betrayal leads to unforeseen consequences as Jimmy's delayed return causes a rift with Lisa, who seeks comfort in the arms of another, Matthew Hollander. The collision of these relationships culminates in a crash—both literal and metaphorical—when Jimmy, overwhelmed by his choices, crashes the family car and faces a moment of reckoning with his father. This narrative weaves a tale of youthful rebellion, the search for an authentic self, and the bittersweet realization of growing up amidst the pressures of expectation and desire.

hard_neg: In a wealthy Chicago suburb during the early 1960s, middle-class Jimmy Reardon hangs out with his upper-class best friend, Fred Roberts, and sleeps with Fred's snobby girlfriend, Denise Hunter. His parents won't help him pay for tuition unless he attends the same business college as his father did, but Jimmy doesn't want to follow that path. Instead, he focuses on coming up with enough money for a plane ticket to go to Hawaii with his wealthy yet chaste girlfriend, Lisa Bentwright. On the night of a big party, Jimmy is given the task of driving home his mother's divorced friend, Joyce Fickett, who conveniently seduces him. Since he is late picking up Lisa, she goes to the dance with the rich Matthew Hollander, instead. Jimmy crashes the family car and then shares a rapprochement with his father.

sent0: The East Is Red depicts the history of the Chinese Communist Party under Mao Zedong from its founding in July 1921 to the establishment of 'New China' in 1949. Detailed in the musical are several key events in CCP history such as the Northern Expedition (taken up by the Kuomintang (KMT) National Revolutionary Army with Chinese Communist and Soviet support), the KMT-led Shanghai massacre of 1927, the Nanchang Uprising and the formation of the People's Liberation Army, the Long March, guerrilla warfare of the PLA during the Second United Front (during the War of Resistance Against Japanese Aggression), the subsequent overthrow of the National Government of the Republic of China on Mainland China by the PLA in the decisive phase of the Chinese Civil War, and the founding of the People's Republic on October 1, 1949. The East Is Red is divided into the following named stages: The prelude 'Sunflowers Face the Sun', followed by 'Dawn of the East', 'A Spark Ignites a Prairie Fire', 'Ten Thousand Crags and Torrents', 'Beacon of Anti-Japanese Resistance', 'Bury the Chiang Family Dynasty', and 'The Chinese People Have Stood Up'. Two additional scenes from the end of the play, 'The Motherland Moves Forward' and 'The World Moves Forward', were omitted from the film adaptation during filming under Mao's suggestion.
sent1: In 1919, following a brief and tumultuous period of communist governance, the Hungarian Republic of Councils met its demise due to the relentless advance of a nationalist counter-revolution. Under the command of Admiral Horthy, the nationalists reclaimed authority, establishing a regency that would tighten its grip on Hungary. Amidst this turbulent backdrop, former soldiers of the now-dissolved Hungarian Red Army became the targets of a merciless persecution led by the secret police and the Hungarian Royal Gendarmerie. One such soldier, István Cserzi, found himself fleeing to the vast expanses of the Great Hungarian Plain, where he sought refuge on a farm owned by two women. Their kindness provided him a temporary sanctuary, aided further by the support of a childhood friend who served as a commandant within the local Gendarmerie, allowing István to exist in a state of relative safety as long as he remained hidden from prying eyes. However, his precarious situation took a dark turn when he uncovered a disturbing secret: the farm's owners were covertly administering poison to the husband of one of the women and his ailing mother. Faced with a harrowing decision, István grappled with the moral implications of potentially betraying them to the authorities, fully aware that such an action could spell his own doom, thus encapsulating the complex intertwining of self-preservation and ethical dilemmas in a time of brutal political upheaval.
hard_neg: In the year of 1917, Mexico was engulfed in a fierce conflict as the new government initiated a crusade against the Catholic Church, leading to the systematic arrest and execution of priests and the burning of churches, effectively outlawing religion. In this chaotic environment, a jovial and carefree priest found himself caught in the crossfire. Although the initial turmoil had forced him into hiding, his intrinsic calling compelled him to return to his homeland, driven by an unwavering commitment to fulfill his priestly duties. As he navigated this perilous landscape, facing the constant threat of capture and execution, he embodied the spirit of resilience and faith, finding solace in clandestine gatherings of the faithful. His journey was not merely one of survival but a powerful testament to the enduring nature of belief amid adversity. Ultimately, the priest's bravery reinvigorated the community, igniting a flicker of hope in a nation shadowed by despair, thus illustrating how the power of conviction can transcend even the darkest of times.

Two additional scenes from the end of the play, 'The Motherland Moves Forward' and 'The World Moves Forward', were omitted from the film adaptation during filming under Mao's suggestion.
sent1: In 1919, following a brief and tumultuous period of communist governance, the Hungarian Republic of Councils met its demise due to the relentless advance of a nationalist counter-revolution. Under the command of Admiral Horthy, the nationalists reclaimed authority, establishing a regency that would tighten its grip on Hungary. Amidst this turbulent backdrop, former soldiers of the now-dissolved Hungarian Red Army became the targets of a merciless persecution led by the secret police and the Hungarian Royal Gendarmerie. One such soldier, István Cserzi, found himself fleeing to the vast expanses of the Great Hungarian Plain, where he sought refuge on a farm owned by two women. Their kindness provided him a temporary sanctuary, aided further by the support of a childhood friend who served as a commandant within the local Gendarmerie, allowing István to exist in a state of relative safety as long as he remained hidden from prying eyes. However, his precarious situation took a dark turn when he uncovered a disturbing secret: the farm's owners were covertly administering poison to the husband of one of the women and his ailing mother. Faced with a harrowing decision, István grappled with the moral implications of potentially betraying them to the authorities, fully aware that such an action could spell his own doom, thus encapsulating the complex intertwining of self-preservation and ethical dilemmas in a time of brutal political upheaval.
hard_neg: In 1917 Mexico, the new government has commenced a war against the Church. Priests are rounded up and executed, churches burned down and religion outlawed. A carefree happy priest has to go on the run but returns to his nation to perform his priestly duties.

sent0: The story centers on Diana 'Sugar' Hill (Bey), a photographer in Houston whose boyfriend, nightclub owner Langston (Larry D. Johnson), has been killed by mob boss Morgan (Robert Quarry) and his men when he refused to sell the club to Morgan. Sugar seeks the help of a former voodoo queen named Mama Maitresse (Zara Cully) to take revenge on Morgan and his thugs. Mama summons the voodoo lord of the dead, Baron Samedi (Don Pedro Colley), who enlists his army of zombies to destroy the men who killed Langston and now want the club. Investigating the killings is Sugar's former boyfriend, police Lt. Valentine (Richard Lawson).
sent1: In the bustling city of New Orleans, photographer Diana 'Sugar' Hill (Bey) finds herself engulfed in a whirlwind of grief and vengeance after the tragic death of her boyfriend, nightclub owner Langston (Larry D. Johnson). Langston's life was brutally cut short by the notorious mob boss Morgan (Robert Quarry) when he adamantly refused to sell his beloved club. Heartbroken and fueled by a desire for retribution, Sugar turns to an unlikely ally: the enigmatic Mama Maitresse (Zara Cully), a former voodoo queen steeped in the ancient practices of her craft. With the flicker of candles and the scent of incense filling the air, Mama Maitresse calls upon the formidable Baron Samedi (Don Pedro Colley), the voodoo lord of the dead. His dark and fearsome presence transforms the atmosphere, as he commands an army of zombies, reanimated and ready to heed Sugar's call for revenge against the men who took her love and threatened her future. As the chaos unfolds and the undead warriors rise, the once-vibrant club becomes the backdrop for a terrifying yet liberating confrontation. Meanwhile, the determined Lt. Valentine (Richard Lawson), Sugar's former boyfriend and a local police officer, doggedly investigates the escalating violence, unaware that the very world he seeks to uphold has now intertwined with the supernatural forces unleashed by Sugar's pain. The outcome of this macabre alliance ultimately reshapes Sugar’s understanding of justice, love, and loss, revealing the profound and haunting ways in which grief can manifest.
hard_neg: In the vibrant streets of Marseilles, Hans, a Canadian sailor, finds himself ensnared in a complicated love affair with the alluring cabaret owner, Dolores. Their passionate romance blossoms amidst the backdrop of music and laughter, but Hans's world takes a dark turn when he is brutally robbed and left for dead in a deserted alley. Upon awakening, he discovers that Dolores has mysteriously vanished, leaving him heartbroken and desperate. Struggling to piece together the events that led to his current plight, Hans reluctantly finds work as a nightclub bouncer, a position that exposes him to the seedy underbelly of the nightlife he once adored. It is there that he meets Tania, a free-spirited gypsy girl whose vibrant energy captivates him. Their flirtation offers a temporary escape from the shadows of his past, yet Tania's charm cannot fill the void left by Dolores. As Hans navigates this new chapter, he grapples with themes of betrayal and the elusive nature of love. The outcome of his journey serves as a stark contrast to the passion he once shared with Dolores, reframing his understanding of relationships as he learns that sometimes, the pursuit of love can lead to unforeseen consequences and heartbreak.

Conman Raymond Fernandez (Jared Leto) defrauds rich women through personal ads, and meets Martha Beck (Salma Hayek) who joins Raymond in his schemes, posing as his sister.
They begin traveling the country, murdering over a dozen women who respond to their ads. 
Homicide detectives Robinson (John Travolta) and Hildebrandt (James Gandolfini) track them down and bring them to justice.

sent0: Starting with the birth of the Jesus child, the film deviates wildly from the New Testament template, with the Virgin Mary now giving birth to twins, likely to give credence to the idea that Gnostic The Gospel of Thomas was actually written by the twin brother of Jesus. However, in keeping with tradition, only the life and suffering of the One is portrayed. Throughout the entire film, Jesus is shadowed by dark agents (the Four Evangelists) who seem to be the puppet masters of the story. But this preordained life spins out of control at the Last Supper, where Jesus develops a fondness for drinking blood. On his way to Calvary, he meets Saint Veronica and surrenders to this thirst, thereby acquiring immortality as a vampire. Jesus lives.
sent1: In a chilling narrative of injustice, a young man finds himself wrongfully convicted of a heinous crime, specifically the tragic murder of two innocent children, a crime he didn't commit. This harrowing experience subjects him to the despair and darkness of a life behind bars, where hope feels like a distant memory. However, fate intervenes when a breakthrough in DNA evidence emerges, proving his innocence and leading to his release. Yet, as he steps into the world once again, he is met with a horrifying twist; a vengeful gang from within the prison system, seeking to settle scores, abducts his daughter, leaving him in a state of frantic desperation and despair. This series of events forces him to confront the profound repercussions of his wrongful imprisonment, a reflection of society's failures, and the lengths to which he will go to protect his family in a reality that has become a twisted nightmare.
hard_neg: In a dark tale of manipulation and deceit, conman Raymond Fernandez finds his niche in the world of romance scams, artfully crafting personal ads to ensnare wealthy women who are desperate for love. His life takes a turn when he meets Martha Beck, an equally twisted soul who, lured by his charm and the thrill of the con, becomes his partner in crime, masquerading as his sister to further entangle their victims in their web of betrayal. As they crisscross the country, their insatiable greed drives them to a horrific spree of violence, resulting in the brutal murders of more than a dozen women who dared to respond to their ads. Meanwhile, the dedicated detectives Robinson and Hildebrandt are hot on their trail, piecing together the clues that will ultimately lead to their capture. This relentless pursuit of justice reveals not only the darkness that can reside in human hearts but also the fragile nature of trust and the devastating consequences of unchecked ambition, culminating in a showdown that forces both the criminals and the lawmen to confront their own moral boundaries.

sent0: Pilgrim is an American former intelligence agent known as the 'Rider of the Blue' who later writes a book on forensic pathology. Pilgrim becomes involved in a case in New York City where a mysterious woman uses his book to commit untraceable murders in the aftermath of 9/11. The 'Saracen' is a Saudi who becomes radicalized by watching his father's beheading. He later trains as a doctor and fights in the Soviet–Afghan War. Pilgrim is recalled to the intelligence community who have detected a threat involving the Saracen, who has created a vaccine-resistant strain of the variola major virus.
sent1: Paul Exben stands as a testament to the heights one can achieve through hard work, pride, and a touch of ambition. As a partner in a prestigious Paris law firm, he enjoys a life many envy: a handsome salary, a beautiful wife named Sarah, and two charming sons whose smiles light up any room. Yet, this seemingly idyllic existence crumbles when Paul uncovers Sarah's affair with Greg Kremer, a local photographer. Overcome by a whirlwind of betrayal and anger, he makes a fateful choice that leads to Greg's untimely demise. In the quiet aftermath, Paul is left in a haunting silence, standing over the body of a man whose life he has taken. His once-stable world collapses into chaos, but in this destruction lies an unexpected opportunity. Seizing a chance to reinvent himself, he adopts Greg's identity and flees to a remote village along the stunning Adriatic coast. Here, amidst the serene landscapes, Paul embarks on a journey that not only allows him to escape the ghosts of his past but also compels him to confront the true essence of his existence and the intricate web of human emotion, ultimately leading to an awakening that challenges his understanding of love, loss, and redemption.
hard_neg: In a lush, remote estate, a stunning young woman named Ivanna arrives with aspirations of joining the esteemed Baron Janos Dalmar as a biochemist. Captivated by the Baron's charm and grandeur, she finds herself torn between her professional aspirations and her burgeoning attraction. In her quest to suppress these desires, Ivanna immerses herself in her scientific work, hoping to find solace in the intricate formulas and discoveries of biochemistry. However, as a series of gruesome murders begin to plague the surrounding area, she becomes increasingly suspicious of the enigmatic Baron and the secrets he harbors. sent0: When Karyn Evans discovers her arrogantly self-centered professor husband Adam is having an affair with student Lindsey Rutledge, she is out for Revenge. She retaliates by having a dalliance of her own with philosophical campus carpenter Pete Lachappelle. Adam is infuriated when he learns about his wife's new relationship, and she in turn defends her right to enjoy the same carnal pleasures he does. The four decide to share a Vermont ski house, where their efforts to behave like liberal people are tested by angst, hurt feelings, and Kasey Evans, who unexpectedly arrives to confront her parents with their outrageous behavior, who makes sure to put her parents back on the right track.

sent0: Windstorm is the story of the teenage girl Mika, who, after accidentally setting fire to her teacher's car, gets sent to her grandmother's stable over the holidays, where she is supposed to learn discipline and order. Unexpectedly, though, she learns that she's not only a naturally talented rider, but also speaks the language of the horses. Windstorm is the direct ancestor of the legendary mare Halla, who was known for carrying her hurt rider to the olympic victory. Mika finds out she is the only one with the ability to ride and tame Windstorm, who is known to be a dangerous horse, and they share an unbreakable, almost magical bond from the start. Windstorm is a movie for all people who love horses, whether young or old.
sent1: After the tragic loss of his parents, the young Prince Bajaja embarks on a quest to carve out his destiny and find his place in the world, driven by a mix of grief and determination. During his travels, he encounters a majestic, enchanted horse that has been trapped by a wicked spell; with a courageous heart, Bajaja frees the creature, earning its loyalty and discovering that it possesses the ability to bestow him with magical gifts. Disguising himself as a mute gardener, he finds himself amidst a royal court where he meets Princess Slavěna, a young woman destined for a dire fate—she is to be sacrificed to a fearsome three-headed dragon on her eighteenth birthday. Though the king, her father, is desperate to find a brave suitor to save her, the fearsome reputation of the dragon has left all potential heroes trembling in their boots; yet, Bajaja feels the weight of his own inner strength and, armed with enchanted armor gifted by the horse, steps forward when all others hesitate. In a fierce battle, he confronts the dragon, suffering wounds that test his resolve, but ultimately he triumphs and claims victory. However, in a twist of fate, an envious knight, cloaked in deceit, takes credit for Bajaja’s bravery, leaving the young prince to grapple with the injustice that not only undermines his efforts but also forces him to reflect on the true nature of valor and recognition.
hard_neg: The story centers around seven-year-old Barbro, a spirited girl with a vivid imagination who shares a secret bond with her twin sister, Ylva-li, the only person who adores her unconditionally, calling her Most Beloved Sister. Ylva-li reigns in a magical realm known as the golden hall, accessible only to those who dare to crawl through a hidden passage beneath the enchanting rose bush, Salikon. Together, they embark on whimsical adventures, exploring their dreams and the depths of their sisterly connection while riding their horses through lush, vibrant landscapes. However, the heart-wrenching moment arrives when Barbro must return to her mundane life with her parents, who feign excitement over her return, creating a hollow sense of belonging for Barbro. Ylva-li, sensing the looming fate that awaits her in the enchanted realm, warns her sister that she will perish when the roses of Salikon wither. Barbro, steeped in skepticism and the innocence of childhood, dismisses the warning and chooses to leave, believing in the permanence of their bond. Yet, the following day, reality strikes cruelly as the roses wilt and die, marking the end of her sister’s existence and leaving Barbro in a state of disbelief and sorrow, forced to confront the fragility of love and the harsh truths of life, which now overshadow her once-vibrant imagination.

Her father, the king, is looking for a suitor for the princess, but no one is prepared to fight the dragon, except Bajaja, who receives magical armor from the enchanted horse. Although hurt, Bajaja defeats the dragon, but an evil knight takes credit for the deed.
hard_neg: The story revolves around seven-year-old Barbro, who has a secret twin sister called Ylva-li, the only person in Barbro's life who likes her more than anything else, and who calls her Most Beloved Sister. Ylva-li is the queen of the golden hall which can be reached by crawling down a hole under the rose bush, Salikon. Barbro and Ylva-Li ride their horses and have adventures together. When Barbro has to return to her parents, Ylva-Li tells her that she will die when the roses on Salikon wither. Barbro refuses to believe her and returns to her parents, who pretend that they have missed her. The next day, the roses on the rose bush are all dead, and there is no longer a hole in the ground.

sent0: The son of the Countess Mensdorf runs away when he can no longer stand her relationship with the Baron Von Mallock. The son becomes the famous trapeze artist Frattani, and after many years he returns home and meets Madeleine, a young dancer. They fall in love and he wants to give up the circus and have a normal life. But when he returns to his mother he finds she doesn't recognize him. The Baron is unhappy with his return and uses criminal means to get rid of him, but in trying to kill Frattani the Baron ultimately loses his own life. The young couple look forward to a happy life together.
sent1: In the heart of Paris, a young man named Paul Exben stands at the pinnacle of success, a partner in a law firm revered for its high-profile cases and even higher salaries. He revels in the opulence of his life, surrounded by luxury, with a beautiful wife named Sarah and two charming sons who embody the glamour of the city. This façade of perfection, however, crumbles to dust when Paul discovers a heart-wrenching betrayal: his wife has been entangled in an affair with a local photographer named Greg Kremer. Consumed by rage and despair, Paul acts in a moment of madness that leads to an irreversible tragedy; he takes Greg's life, shattering his own in the process. As he gazes down at the consequences of his actions, Paul is enveloped in a suffocating sense of loss, realizing he has plunged himself into a nightmare from which he cannot awaken. Yet, in the depths of despair, he finds an unexpected spark of determination. He decides to assume the identity of the man he has killed, seeking refuge in a remote corner of former Yugoslavia, where the sun-kissed Adriatic waves promise a fresh start. It is here, away from the chaos of his past, that Paul begins to unravel the complexities of his own identity and charts a new course toward an authentic life, filled with the potential for redemption and introspection.
hard_neg: Deep within the confines of her troubled home, fourteen-year-old Jessika fights tirelessly against the mounting pressures that threaten to tear her family apart. With her father recently unemployed, the air is thick with tension and despair; her mother's attempts to maintain a semblance of normalcy only exacerbate the growing rift between them. As her parents engage in constant arguments, Jessika's older sister Caro becomes increasingly absorbed in the world of teenage romance, leaving Jessika feeling isolated and neglected. In the shadows of her family's dysfunction, Jessika's spirit begins to erode, leading her toward a horrifying event that she is forced to witness firsthand. This pivotal moment serves as a cruel awakening, revealing the harrowing depths of her family's denial and dysfunction. As despair deepens, Jessika finds herself spiraling into a dark depression, feeling trapped in a reality she cannot control. In a courageous bid for her own salvation, she resolves to break free from the weight of her family's turmoil, a decision met with resistance from her parents who remain ensnared in their delusions. Determined to reclaim her life, Jessika boldly steps into the unknown, ready to confront the challenges ahead and carve out a future that defies the darkness surrounding her.

hard_neg:
Despite a dark secret, 14-year-old Jessika (Elisa Schlott) fights to keep her broken family together. 
When Jessika's father (Michael Lott) loses his job, their family life becomes an impossible struggle. While her parents try to repair their already off-balance relationship and her sister Caro (Sina Tkotsch) becomes increasingly obsessed with boys, Jessika is left to her own devices. The lack of communication between the parents and their incapability to manage their off kilter situation, culminates in a horrifying event, that Jessika is forced to witness. In the face of her parents delusional denial, Jessica begins to fall into a deep self-destructive depression. To save herself from her family's dark abyss, and despite her parents continuous attempts to hold her back, Jessika decides to take her future into her own hands and takes a very courageous step….

sent1:
Paul Exben is a remarkable success story: a partner in one of Paris's most prestigious law firms, boasting a substantial salary, a spacious home, a glamorous wife, and two sons who could easily grace the pages of a high-end fashion catalog. However, his world shatters when he uncovers his wife Sarah's affair with Greg Kremer, a local photographer. In a moment of passionate fury, Paul commits a fatal mistake. As he stands over the lifeless body of his wife's lover, Paul comprehends that his once-perfect life is irreversibly shattered.
But rather than succumb to his grim circumstances, Paul decides to seize a radical opportunity. Assuming the identity of the deceased man, he escapes to a remote region in former Yugoslavia, nestled along the enchanting Adriatic coast. In this secluded refuge, Paul is granted a second chance at being true to himself and, finally, gaining a profound perspective on the grand tapestry of life.

sent0: Set against the backdrop of a South African hospital, the film centers on Hobday, a male nurse portrayed by the acclaimed Anthony Quinn, who is assigned to care for a foreign President named Simon Sabela, a man who narrowly escaped an assassination attempt on the very day he touched down in South Africa for an official visit. The atmosphere is charged with tension, as the leader is surrounded by a formidable security detail due to the multiple threats against his life. As Hobday navigates the complexities of his responsibilities, he makes a fateful decision to kidnap Sabela for his own personal gain, unaware that a sniper is lurking in the shadows, still intent on carrying out the assassination while the President is under his care. This precarious situation spirals into a whirlwind of unexpected plot twists, culminating in a breathtaking climax involving cable cars suspended above a high plateau ridge, where loyalties are tested, and the stakes are raised to their highest point.
sent1: In a gripping narrative that unfolds with a sense of urgency, former mercenary Yeo-hoon has turned over a new leaf, striving for a semblance of normalcy in his life. However, his past comes crashing back when he finds himself wrongfully accused of the murder of a high-profile CEO. In a desperate bid for freedom, he narrowly escapes, taking a bullet in the process and landing in a hospital bed where the stakes only escalate. Here, he encounters Tae-joon, a medical resident who, driven by compassion and a sense of justice, assists Yeo-hoon in evading his relentless pursuers. Yet, the plot thickens when Tae-joon's pregnant wife is suddenly kidnapped, thrusting both men into a whirlwind of chaos. As they embark on a perilous 36-hour chase, the narrative explores the lengths to which one will go to protect loved ones, revealing inner conflicts and forging an unlikely camaraderie amidst the turmoil.
hard_neg: In a thrilling pursuit of scientific advancement, Major Perry Rhodan leads a four-man mission aboard the rocketship Stardust, aiming to uncover a source of radioactive material more potent than uranium, with a focus on the Moon. Upon arrival, they stumble upon a stranded Arkonide spaceship where Commander Thora is valiantly trying to rescue a scientist named Crest, who is gravely ill with leukemia. The urgency of the situation escalates as the Earth crew learns that a cure exists back home. Driven by compassion and a sense of duty, Perry and his team embark on a dangerous journey back to Earth to fetch a doctor who possesses the cure. However, an unexpected betrayal looms within their ranks; one crew member has been feeding information to a crime lord who covets the radioactive material and views the Arkonide situation as a chance for even greater power. This leads to a critical moment when the crime lord orchestrates a plan to replace the medical team, resulting in the kidnapping of Thora and a race against time. Just when hope seems lost, Crest’s ingenuity offers a glimmer of salvation as he equips Rhodan and Bull with Arkonide technology, enabling them to execute a daring rescue operation that not only saves Thora but also secures the means to cure Crest. Their journey concludes with a promise to return to the Moon, armed with vital resources to aid the Arkonides in repairing their damaged spaceship, showcasing the themes of sacrifice and collaboration in the face of adversity.

With many threats against his well-being, the leader is heavily guarded around the clock. Hobday manages to kidnap his patient for strictly personal gain, unaware that a hired sniper is still attempting to take the life of the foreign leader while in Hobday's custody. hard_neg:
In a quest to find a source of radioactive material more powerful than uranium, Major Perry Rhodan leads a four-man mission to the Moon on the rocketship Stardust.
On the Moon, they find a stranded Arkonide spaceship, where Commander Thora is trying to save a scientist named Crest, along with a crew of robots.  The earthmen find that Crest is suffering from leukemia, for which there is a cure available on Earth.  Perry and others take an Arkonide shuttlecraft to Earth to bring back a doctor with the cure.
One of the Earth crewmen is a traitor, however, supplying information to a crime lord who is after the radioactive material, but who sees the encounter with the Arkonides as providing an opportunity for an even greater prize.  The crime lord arranges to replace the doctor and nurses with his own people, and upon arriving at the Arkonide ship they kidnap Thora in a bid to gain Arkonide technology.
However, Crest provides Rhodan and Bull with Arkonide technology, which helps them rescue Thora as well as the real doctor, who is able to cure Crest.  They soon leave the Moon in the Stardust, promising to return with materials the Arkonides need to repair their spaceship.

sent0: In a modest market garden in the South of France, a woman raises her seven children alone. They all work hard alongside hired labour and are exploited by the farm owner, who is her lover and the father of the children, an unfeeling and demanding man who lives on a nearby estate with his legally married wife. Through her unconditional love, the mother strives to preserve the world of her children. She would rather bring them up in the country than in the town, without resources.
sent1: In the quaint coastal town of Boulogne-sur-Mer, Sandra finds herself returning to the place of her childhood, a decision borne out of desperation as she escapes the clutches of a violent husband. The once familiar streets resonate with echoes of her past, yet the warmth of her mother's embrace welcomes her back into a world that feels foreign after fifteen years. With no financial means to sustain herself, Sandra secures a position at a local fish cannery, where she begins to forge new friendships with two fellow workers, both of whom offer her a semblance of camaraderie in her time of need. However, life takes a drastic turn when one day, during a seemingly innocuous moment at work, she finds herself in a life-or-death confrontation with one of her colleagues. The tension escalates, and in a tragic twist of fate, Sandra accidentally kills him, a turning point that shatters her fragile sense of safety and plunges her into a haunting reckoning with violence and survival. As she grapples with the realities of her actions, the harsh contrast between her hopes for a fresh start and the darkness that surrounds her amplifies, revealing the stark truths of her existence and the relentless struggle to carve out a life free from fear.
hard_neg: In 'Half Broke Horses,' author Jeannette Walls chronicles the remarkable life of her grandmother, Lily Casey Smith, capturing the essence of a woman who embodies both resilience and spirit in the face of adversity. Growing up amid the frontier of Texas, Lily learns the art of horse breaking, a skill that symbolizes both independence and the rugged determination required for survival. At just fifteen, her adventurous spirit propels her to undertake a solitary journey of five hundred miles to fulfill her dream of teaching in a one-room schoolhouse, a pivotal moment that marks her transition from a carefree girl to a responsible adult. As the narrative unfolds, we see her evolve into a formidable ranch owner in Arizona alongside her second husband and their children, navigating the complexities of rural life with an unyielding spirit. Lily's story is replete with colorful episodes that showcase her versatility; she dabbles in poker, bootlegging, and horse racing—all activities that reveal her unquenchable thirst for freedom and the challenges she faces along the way. 'Half Broke Horses' serves as a celebration of the tenacity of rural life, reflecting on the joys and struggles inherent in such a lifestyle, while also paying homage to the legacies passed down through generations, affirming that every tale, woven with hardship and hope, contributes to the rich tapestry of human experience.

sent0: Marc has inherited the house of his late aunt on the Côte d'Azur and takes the family there on for their summer holiday, leaving their home in Paris. Charly, who has never had a girlfriend, is thought to be gay by his parents and Martin, who is gay, is also staying with them. Béatrix's lover Mathieu arrives in the village and manages to sneak opportunities to be with her. When Martin goes out one night to the local gay cruising area (an old fort on a nearby hillside) Charly follows him and meets Didier. After realizing he isn't gay, he calls Didier for help when the hot water stops working. Didier then meets Marc and they realize how much they missed each other from when Marc used to visit the area in his youth. Throughout everyone eats much fruits de mer, especially sea violets. At the end everyone sings a song called 'Fruits de mer', each with their preferred partner.
sent1: The narrative unfolds with a light-hearted correspondence between a renowned author and his publisher, Ernst Rowohlt, who encourages Tucholsky to pen a whimsical love story. In response, Tucholsky presents a vibrant summer tale centered on Kurt, affectionately referred to as Peter, who recounts his adventures in Sweden alongside his dear friend Lydia, whom he often calls 'die Prinzessin' or the princess. Their journey is marked by scenic train rides and a ferry across serene waters to Gripsholm castle, where they spend a delightful three weeks. During their stay, they are joined by Kurt's childhood companion, Karlchen, and later Lydia’s close friend, Billie, offering a mix of lively encounters and heartfelt moments. Among the charming episodes, the narrative includes an unexpected erotic scene that captures the spirit of youthful exploration during the waning days of Weimar Germany. However, the story also takes a poignant turn when they encounter a little girl suffering under the harsh rule of a cruel caretaker in a children's home. Moved by her plight, they reach out to the girl's mother, residing in Switzerland, and orchestrate a heartfelt reunion for the child, intertwining themes of love and compassion throughout their summer escapade.
hard_neg: Set against the backdrop of post-World War II Australia, the film narrates the life of Edouard Pierson, a wool dealer originally from Belgium who seeks a fresh start in a foreign land. After the tragic death of his girlfriend, Edouard becomes a single father, bearing the weight of his responsibilities alone while raising a young girl whom his family remains unaware of. Returning to Belgium, he aims to assist his family with their wool business, a task that rekindles old familial tensions and unveils long-buried secrets. In this tumultuous environment, he encounters Jeanne, a captivating woman who is also navigating the challenges of single parenthood. Their relationship blossoms amidst the chaos, marked by deep connections and emotional vulnerabilities. As the plot unfolds, Edouard's interactions with his family oscillate between conflict and reconciliation, leading to revelations that shift the dynamics of their relationships. Ultimately, the film weaves a narrative that examines the complexities of love, loss, and the enduring bonds of family, culminating in a resolution that reflects on the past while hinting at the hopeful promise of new beginnings.

sent0: Pier Ullman, a man of many odd jobs, found himself entwined in a life that flickered between the mundane and the illicit, often joining forces with Kevin for their mutual associate, Rachid. The rhythm of his life took a sharp turn when he received the shocking news of his father's death, a man he hadn't heard from in years, buried under the weight of unresolved issues and estrangement. This tragic revelation ignited a spark of vengeance in Pier, compelling him to confront the past that had long haunted his family; he was determined to avenge his father, who had suffered at the hands of Pier's grandfather and uncle. With a steely resolve, he sought out his father's family, threading his way into their world, ultimately infiltrating their diamond business in Antwerp, a treacherous landscape filled with both opportunity and danger, where the glimmer of diamonds became not just a pursuit of wealth, but a path towards redemption and retribution.
sent1: Vincent Malivert, once the esteemed head of a renowned jewel brokerage on the illustrious Place Vendôme, found himself ensnared in a web of financial ruin and scandal. His life spiraled out of control due to mounting debts and an involvement in the trafficking of stolen jewels, leading him to an irreversible decision: he took his own life, leaving behind a shattered world for his wife, Marianne. Struggling with her own demons of alcoholism and recent stints in a rehabilitation clinic, Marianne stumbled upon a hidden treasure in the form of perfectly cut diamonds nestled in Vincent's safe. Aware of their likely stolen origins, a flicker of determination ignited within her—a chance to reclaim her life from the abyss that had consumed it. As she embarked on a quest to find a buyer for these illicit jewels, she inadvertently crossed paths with Battistelli, a dubious dealer whose shadowy connections linked back to the very troubles that had ensnared her in a loveless marriage, marking her journey as one of dark irony and a desperate search for liberation.
hard_neg: Sid, Russ, and Jerry, three aimless dreamers caught in the web of their dead-end lives, aspired to a world of crime in search of an easy payday that could liberate them from their mundane existence. Their misguided ambition was tested when an ill-fated jewelry store heist exposed their incompetence and served as a wake-up call. Undeterred by this failure, they set their sights on a more audacious target: an armored-truck robbery that they believed would finally turn their fortunes around. However, as they plotted this grand scheme, the chaos surrounding them only intensified, with their dysfunctional families spiraling wildly out of control. Each day brought fresh disasters at home, reflecting the disarray in their lives and revealing that their dreams of criminal success were overshadowed by the realities of their failing relationships and the inescapable grip of their pasts, turning their misguided pursuits into a darkly comedic testament to the futility of their aspirations.

Marianne, who has spent the last few years in and out of a clinic recovering from alcoholism, discovers a set of perfect cut diamonds in her husband's safe. Although she knows the diamonds are probably stolen, she decides to use this opportunity to rebuild her life and sets about trying to find a buyer for the hidden jewels. Unwittingly, she is drawn to a shady dealer named Battistelli, the very man who drove her into a disastrous and loveless marriage.

sent1: In the heart of Paris, British newlywed Regina Lambert finds herself ensnared in a web of deceit and terror upon returning home with her husband Charles after a brief vacation. Determined to end her marriage, she is met with the shocking sight of their apartment stripped of all belongings, only to discover that Charles has been murdered, leaving her reeling with confusion and fear. The French police swarm the scene, revealing that Charles had liquidated their possessions for an astonishing $1.8 million, all of which has mysteriously vanished. As she grapples with the tragedy and betrayal, Regina encounters Joshua, a mysterious stranger she had met during her holiday. With his unexpected assistance, she begins to piece together the fragmented truth about Charles’s illicit dealings, while simultaneously facing the looming threat of three menacing figures who now pursue her relentlessly. The narrative unfolds with a sense of urgency as Regina transforms from a heartbroken bride into a determined woman, fighting against the odds to reclaim her life and uncover the sinister forces at play. The outcome of her quest for justice reflects not only her resilience but also serves as a stark reminder of the dangers that lurk beneath the surface of seemingly perfect lives, revealing the darker truths that lie hidden within the shadows of love and betrayal.

sent0: The story of the painter Antonio Ligabue unfolds with rich flashbacks that delve into his childhood and his Swiss-Italian roots. Little Antonio, thrust into the care of adoptive parents, quickly begins to experience a cascade of psychophysical disorders, battling rickets and ultimately facing expulsion from school after a violent encounter with his mother. This tumultuous early life leads to numerous hospitalizations in an asylum, painting a vivid picture of his inner turmoil. Yet within this chaos, Antonio finds solace in his artistic pursuits; he passionately engages in painting and sculpture, often bringing to life exotic animals such as lions, horses, gorillas, and tigers, which he seamlessly merges with the landscapes of Emilia, the region he later calls home in Gualtieri, where he is mockingly referred to as 'El Tudesc,' or 'The German.' His artistic talent does not go unnoticed for long, as the critic Renato Marino Mazzacurati discovers him, urging him to embrace his work and participate in local art exhibitions and conferences. This encouragement marks a pivotal turning point in his life, leading to a gradual recognition of his artistry, although he still faces dismissal from certain academics who label him a naïve artist, a term that only partially captures the depth and raw emotion of his work.
sent1: The journey of Jacob, a boy grappling with a profound belief that he is a wolf, takes a dark turn when he attacks his brother, resulting in his commitment to a mental asylum. Inside the institution, Jacob forges connections with fellow patients who share his animalistic delusions, including Rufus, a patient convinced he is a German Shepherd, and Cecile, an enigmatic figure known as 'Wildcat.' Together, they navigate the oppressive atmosphere of the asylum, often roaming the hallways at night in search of freedom. However, the head of the asylum, Dr. Mann, employs brutal methods to maintain control, which deeply frustrates Jacob and culminates in a violent outburst against an orderly, leading to his confinement in a cage. In a pivotal moment, Cecile sneaks into his cell, igniting a passionate encounter that is soon shattered by the arrival of Dr. Angeli, resulting in Jacob's punishment with a cattle prod. This act of cruelty provokes a rebellion among the other patients against the oppressive regime of the Zookeeper. Disgusted by the inhumane treatment, Cecile devises a plan to rescue Jacob, ultimately aiding his escape into the forest where he can embrace the wildness he believes is his true nature, symbolizing a quest for personal freedom and identity amidst turmoil.
hard_neg: The tale of Jacques 'Jock' Boyer, a professional cyclist, unfolds as he relocates to Rwanda in 2006 with a mission to support a group of genocide survivors striving to build a national cycling team. This team is comprised of orphaned children, many of whom are still reeling from the traumatic scars of the genocide that devastated the country just a decade prior. As Jock immerses himself in the lives of these young athletes, he witnesses their struggles and the harsh realities they face: illiteracy, malnutrition, and a lack of basic amenities such as clean water and healthcare. The initial vision of 'Team Rwanda' extends beyond mere cycling; it transforms into a comprehensive initiative dedicated to fulfilling the broader needs of its members, embodying resilience and hope for a nation still healing from its past. Over time, the team becomes a beacon of strength, representing the possibility of recovery and unity in a fractured society. Their journey culminates in the remarkable achievement of qualifying for the 2012 Summer Olympics, marking a significant milestone that not only elevates the team but also serves as a testament to the power of sport in overcoming adversity and fostering a new national identity, symbolizing the triumph of the human spirit against the backdrop of history.

hard_neg:
Professional cyclist Jacques “Jock” Boyer moves to Rwanda in 2006 to help a group of struggling genocide survivors working to form a national cycling team. The team is composed of children left orphaned and traumatized by the genocide a decade earlier. Over the course of the story, both Boyer and the team "rise from the ashes" of their pasts with the help of their new achievements.
"Team Rwanda" begins as a cycling organization but evolves as organizers realize the athletes' greater needs. Many riders are illiterate and malnourished, living without water, electricity or healthcare, and most are recovering from the psychological effects of the 1994 genocide. Eventually, "Team Rwanda" is viewed as a symbol of hope for Rwanda, ambassadors for the recovering country. In subsequent years, the team expands its vision and develops a model of caring for athletes. In 2012, the team begins developing the first all-African team to match up to the Tour de France after one of the riders qualifies for the 2012 Summer Olympics.

sent0: The film opens aboard a U-boat as it returns from a mission. It then follows the crew onshore the day before they ship off for their next mission—meeting their family and sweethearts, spending a last night at a club, and so forth. Then they ship off, soon sighting and boarding a Dutch merchant ship, which they inspect for contraband. The boarding of the ship is shown being done professionally and in a non-confrontational manner. While they are aboard the Dutch ship, a Royal Navy ship spots them and tries to torpedo them, but the U-boat ends up sinking it.
sent1: In the year of 1588, a formidable pirate ship, aligned with the Spanish Armada, limps into a small, isolated village on the British coast after suffering substantial damage in a fierce naval skirmish. As the crew begins repairs, the Spanish pirates weave a tale of victory, instilling a false sense of security among the villagers, who are led to believe that the English fleet has been vanquished. The villagers, initially hesitant, slowly succumb to the pirates' manipulation, offering their cooperation with a sullen demeanor, but the air thickens with underlying tension and whispers of rebellion. As the pirates celebrate their perceived triumph, the simmering unrest culminates into a full-blown revolt, revealing the fragile nature of power and control, and forcing the pirates to confront not only the villagers’ defiance but also their own hubris in underestimating the spirit of those they sought to subjugate.
hard_neg: At a small station named Podbednya, reminiscent of many such locations throughout the Soviet Union, the echoes of fierce battles fought during the Great Patriotic War linger in the air. As the years pass, relatives of fallen soldiers come to pay their respects, reminiscing about those who reached for victory but were claimed by the chaos of war. The film "One-Two, Soldiers Were Going..." intricately weaves two parallel narratives, one set in the spring of 1944 on the battlefield, where bravery and sacrifice unfold amidst the clamor of conflict, while the other unfolds in the mid-1970s, reflecting on the memories and legacies of those who fought. As the story progresses, the two timelines converge, illustrating the enduring impact of war on families and communities, and by the end, the audience is left reflecting on the significance of remembrance, the weight of loss, and the inextricable links between past and present, all encapsulated in the solemn commemoration of that fateful day in March 1944.

sent0: The film opens aboard a U-boat as it returns from a mission. It then follows the crew onshore the day before they ship off for their next mission—meeting their family and sweethearts, spending a last night at a club, and so forth. Then they ship off, soon sighting and boarding a Dutch merchant ship, which they inspect for contraband. The boarding of the ship is shown being done professionally and in a non-confrontational manner. While they are aboard the Dutch ship, a Royal Navy ship spots them and tries to torpedo them, but the U-boat ends up sinking it.
sent1: In the year of 1588, a formidable pirate ship, aligned with the Spanish Armada, limps into a small, isolated village on the British coast after suffering substantial damage in a fierce naval skirmish. As the crew begins repairs, the Spanish pirates weave a tale of victory, instilling a false sense of security among the villagers, who are led to believe that the English fleet has been vanquished. The villagers, initially hesitant, slowly succumb to the pirates' manipulation, offering their cooperation with a sullen demeanor, but the air thickens with underlying tension and whispers of rebellion. As the pirates celebrate their perceived triumph, the simmering unrest culminates into a full-blown revolt, revealing the fragile nature of power and control, and forcing the pirates to confront not only the villagers’ defiance but also their own hubris in underestimating the spirit of those they sought to subjugate.

sent0: Steve Daggett (Cameron Mitchell) fights to protect Fidel Castro from dangerous pro-Batista counterrevolutionaries. Steve comes to Cuba to find his friend Hank Miller (Logan Field) who has been missing for a while. It turns out that he has been captured by Fernando (Eduardo Noriega), the leader of the pro-Batista forces, who needs Hank to convert their airplanes into bombers. Steve's former girlfriend Monica (Allison Hayes) is now Mrs. Hank Miller. 'Pier 5, Havana' was filmed in the Los Angeles area just after the Cuban Revolution. The Errol Flynn semi-documentary Cuban Rebel Girls and the black comedy Our Man in Havana were shot on location on the island in the same post-revolution period.
sent1: In the midst of Cold War tensions, an American intelligence officer named Jeff Larson (Ray Danton) is dispatched to San Juan, Spain, following a tip-off about suspicious Soviet activity at a naval base. Equipped with his skills and a keen sense of duty, Larson reunites with his old colleague Bob Stuart (Roger Hanin) and meets Pilar Perez (Pascale Petit), a contact with invaluable local insights. As Larson digs deeper, he uncovers a sophisticated network of remote-controlled cameras planted by the Soviets, designed to spy on American submarines. This dangerous discovery leads to a tragic incident when two Spanish sailors perish in an explosion while attempting to disarm one of these boobytraps. Undeterred, Larson dons his scuba gear to infiltrate a Soviet spy vessel, where he not only learns that the enemy is tracking U.S. naval movements but also intercepting crucial radio communications. With critical intelligence at stake, he disarms another landmine protecting a camera, turning the device into a weapon against the Soviets by converting it into a limpet mine. Throughout his perilous mission, Larson faces multiple assassination attempts but remains resolute, knowing that each action he takes could tip the balance between nations.
hard_neg: In the heart of the dense Akasava jungle in South America, British scientist Prof. Walter Forrester (Ángel Menéndez) is immersed in his research when an intriguing stone is uncovered by his assistant. This rare artifact, however, is swiftly stolen, leading to Forrester's sudden disappearance and casting a shadow of suspicion over him. As the tension escalates, a Scotland Yard detective is tragically murdered right outside Forrester's office in London, prompting the Scotland Yard chief, Sir Philipp (Siegfried Schürenberg), to assign the case to the astute Jane Morgan (Soledad Miranda), who, under the guise of the British consul's alluring young wife Irving Lambert (Alberto Dalbés), embarks on a secret mission to unravel the mystery. Her investigation leads her to South America, where she encounters Rex Forrester (Fred Williams), the professor's concerned nephew, who is equally determined to uncover the truth behind his uncle's enigmatic fate. As Jane navigates a web of deceit and danger, she must discern friend from foe, all while racing against time to solve the case before it spirals into an international incident, revealing the complex interplay between scientific discovery, personal loyalty, and the relentless pursuit of truth.

sent1: Prior to his discovery and death, an American intelligence officer working undercover at a Soviet Naval base sends proof of the Russians filming American submarines off a joint US-Spanish naval base in San Juan, on the coast of Spain. American intelligence "Super Agent" Jeff Larson (Ray Danton) is sent to San Juan to investigate where he meets up with his former colleague Bob Stuart (Roger Hanin), and his Spanish contact, Pilar Perez (Pascale Petit). Larson (code name "Jaguar"), helps the American military discover remote controlled video cameras being used by the Soviets. These cameras are boobytrapped using sophisticated landmines, killing two Spanish sailors who tried to disarm one. Larson skin dives to clandestinely board a Soviet spy ship to discover that not only are they monitoring American submarines, but they are intercepting radio transmissions from the US-Spanish naval base as well as having a mole on the base. Larson successfully disarms a landmine protecting another video camera, saves the camera for analysis and hatches a plan to convert the landmine into a limpet mine and "return [it] to sender."  Throughout the escapade Larson survives several assassination attempts.
