sent1:
During a long stay in Paris, the young Callimaco learns from his friend Cammillo Calfucci of the beauty of Lucrezia, who has been married for four years with the rich and silly notary Nicia Calfucci, from whom she cannot have children. Returning to Florence, he sees for the first time and falls in love with the woman, who tries to meet and seduce but without success. To help him in the enterprise, in addition to his servant Siro, is Ligurio, who has a great influence on Nicia; Ligurio advises Callimaco to pretend to be a doctor and to convince the notary to let his wife drink an infusion of mandragola, capable of curing her presumed sterility (in fact it is Nicia who is sterile: according to a belief then widespread, a man who was not impotent must necessarily have been able to procreate). However, this magical cure has a contraindication: whoever has the first sexual relationship with the woman will be infected with the poison of the mandragola and will die within eight days. To remedy the problem and at the same time protect Nicia's honor, all you have to do is meet her secretly with the first street "boy" who will absorb all the deadly poison.
Persuaded Nicia, all that remains is to convince Lucrezia, who will never consent given her pious and devoted character. This time also the mother Sostrata and the friar Timothy will intervene, who playing on her Christian devotion - dramaturgically important the biblical quotation of Lot and the daughters - will convince her to "cure". That night Callimaco will disguise himself as a beggar and will be carried by the husband himself into the arms of his wife, who will not be satisfied with this fleeting encounter but will want to reiterate it in the time to come.

sent0: The film begins by showing Antonio López García as a very meticulous painter. He drives in pegs to mark his stance, hangs a weight and uses strings to determine the symmetry and center of his painting. His first attempt starts out peacefully but he soon encounters problems due to the weather and the size of his canvas. As López and a friend discuss Michelangelo's The Last Judgement, painted when Michelangelo was in his 60s, which López is fast approaching, the film's subject takes shape as the relationship between the artist's work and his own mortality. López's future attempts are much more rushed and frantic as he struggles to compete with the weather, the fleeting sun and the rotting and weighed down fruit in maintaining his vision.
sent1: As the story unfolds, we meet Sonny Wexler, a once-renowned film producer who now feels the weight of his fading fame and the personal turmoil brought on by his wife's battle with addiction. Once celebrated for his Oscar-nominated film, Sonny finds himself overshadowed by a younger generation of filmmakers, particularly the ambitious studio executive Damon Black, who represents a new era that has little regard for veterans like him. Realizing that his cinematic legacy is slipping through his fingers, Sonny is driven by a fierce determination to reclaim his place in Hollywood. The turning point arrives when he discovers an extraordinary screenplay penned by an up-and-coming writer, reigniting his hope for a comeback. However, in a cruel twist, Black sabotages his efforts to secure the script, leaving Sonny in a race against time with only seventy-two hours to gather the funds necessary to buy the story. In a moment of desperation, he turns to the mafia for a loan of $50,000, marking a drastic shift in his character as he grapples with the lengths one must go to for redemption, ultimately reflecting on the themes of ambition, legacy, and the haunting specter of mortality.
hard_neg: In a poignant tale, we delve into the lives of Marina, a prostitute whose heart holds immense love, and Alexander, an unsuccessful artist crippled by a terminal illness that blinds him and saps his strength. Their brief time together in Italy is steeped in a bittersweet joy, as they cling to the warmth of their fleeting romance amidst the chaos of life. Despite Marina's unwavering support and affection, Alexander's condition deteriorates rapidly, shrouding their moments in a sense of impending loss. As they confront the unbearable reality of his illness, a profound bond forms, yet it also leads them to a tragic decision to end their lives together, engulfed in a sorrowful embrace. This heartbreaking outcome challenges the notion of love and the lengths individuals will go to in order to escape suffering, ultimately framing their story as a haunting exploration of love intertwined with despair, highlighting the fragility of life and the often-painful choices arising from deep emotional connections.

In a moment of desperation, he turns to the mafia for a loan of $50,000, marking a drastic shift in his character as he grapples with the lengths one must go to for redemption, ultimately reflecting on the themes of ambition, legacy, and the haunting specter of mortality.
hard_neg: It is a love story between the prostitute Marina and the unsuccessful artist Alexander, who suffers from a cancer which makes him blind and ill. They are happy for a short period in Italy. Despite Marina's loving care, Alexanders health gets worse and they together commit suicide in the end.

sent0: The film begins by showing Antonio López García as a very meticulous painter. He drives in pegs to mark his stance, hangs a weight and uses strings to determine the symmetry and center of his painting. His first attempt starts out peacefully but he soon encounters problems due to the weather and the size of his canvas. As López and a friend discuss Michelangelo's The Last Judgement, painted when Michelangelo was in his 60s, which López is fast approaching, the film's subject takes shape as the relationship between the artist's work and his own mortality. López's future attempts are much more rushed and frantic as he struggles to compete with the weather, the fleeting sun and the rotting and weighed down fruit in maintaining his vision.
sent1:
The story revolves around Sonny Wexler, an aging and washed-up veteran film producer, who is burdened with a wife struggling with pill addiction. While Sonny had once produced an Oscar-nominated film during his prime, he now grapples with being a "has-been" in a Hollywood industry dominated by a younger generation, exemplified by the studio executive Damon Black and foreign investors.
Aware that his time in the limelight is dwindling and fearing he will be forgotten, Sonny decides to make one last bid for relevance by creating a memorable movie. His opportunity emerges when he comes across a remarkable screenplay from a promising young writer. However, Black interferes with the deal and edges Sonny out, leaving Sonny with just seventy-two hours to secure enough funds to acquire the script himself.
Facing desperation, Sonny resorts to seeking assistance from the mafia to borrow the necessary $50,000 he needs to make his dream a reality.
hard_neg: In a poignant tale, we delve into the lives of Marina, a prostitute whose heart holds immense love, and Alexander, an unsuccessful artist crippled by a terminal illness that blinds him and saps his strength. Their brief time together in Italy is steeped in a bittersweet joy, as they cling to the warmth of their fleeting romance amidst the chaos of life. Despite Marina's unwavering support and affection, Alexander's condition deteriorates rapidly, shrouding their moments in a sense of impending loss. As they confront the unbearable reality of his illness, a profound bond forms, yet it also leads them to a tragic decision to end their lives together, engulfed in a sorrowful embrace. This heartbreaking outcome challenges the notion of love and the lengths individuals will go to in order to escape suffering, ultimately framing their story as a haunting exploration of love intertwined with despair, highlighting the fragility of life and the often-painful choices arising from deep emotional connections.

sent0: On one of those rare days when all is quiet at the Quai des Orfèvres, Maigret receives a visit from the mild-mannered toy salesman (at the Magasins du Louvre) Xavier Marton. Marton thinks that either his wife is trying to poison him or he is just crazy. Then Mme. Marton also shows up, leaving Maigret rather bemused. Worried about his own wife's health and musing over his marriage, Maigret decides to investigate despite an order from the public prosecutor not to. When a body finally shows up, the reality is a surprise to everyone.
sent1: British newlywed Regina Lambert lives in Paris with her husband Charles. She returns home following a short vacation, determined to divorce Charles only to discover their apartment has been stripped bare and that her husband has been murdered. The French police are in her apartment. Charles had liquidated their possessions for $1.8M and the money is missing. Regina is soon reunited with a mysterious stranger Joshua (Mark Wahlberg) she met on her holiday. He helps her piece together the truth about the deceased Charlie and deal with three menacing people who are now following her.
hard_neg: Forced to leave France, Dédée and her bullying pimp Marco have reached Antwerp, where she is one of the girls in René's bar and Marco is the doorman, doing drug deals on the side. Taking a stroll by the docks in the early evening, Dédée meets Francesco, sympathetic Italian captain of a cargo ship, who knows René. When he comes later to the bar, he discusses some secret deal with René and then takes Dédée to a hotel for the night. The two have fallen for each other and he would like to take her away with him, but this would need the agreement of René and of Marco. René is happy to do a favour to Francesco, happy to free Dédée from the obnoxious Marco, who he throws out into the street, and says he is happy to drive Dédée to Francesco's ship once he has closed the bar for the night. While Francesco is waiting on the jetty for Dédée to appear, Marco shoots him dead, drops his gun, and disappears. When René and Dédée arrive to find the body, they comb the nightspots of the city in search of Marco, eventually catching him at the railway station. At gunpoint they take him to a lonely spot where René, after knocking him out, runs the car over him.

sent0: May, a single mom, wants to become an actress. Her next-door neighbor August, a bodybuilder, wants to become a worthy successor to his hero, Arnold Schwarzenegger. They live in a district of Los Angeles known as Echo Park, not far from Dodger Stadium, and dream of a better life. Jonathan, a pizza delivery boy, arrives at May's door and is immediately smitten with her. As he entertains her young son, Henry, she goes out to pursue an acting opportunity that has come along, only to discover that it involves disrobing in private residences, delivering 'strip-o-grams.' May gives the strip-o-grams a try and August tries to meet his idol at a reception at the Austrian embassy, while Jonathan worries that the two are more than mere neighbors.
sent1: In the bustling heart of Los Angeles, Harold Hall, a young man with dreams bigger than his talent, finds himself yearning for the silver screen. With little acting ability to speak of, he stumbles into an unexpected opportunity when a mix-up with his application photograph lands him a coveted screen-test. As he arrives at the illustrious Hollywood studio, the excitement of the moment quickly turns to chaos; everything he attempts goes awry, leading to a series of comical mishaps that earn him the ire of the studio staff. Nevertheless, amidst the turmoil, he captures the attention of a stunning actress, whose charm and wit stir something deep within him. With her support and a sense of ridiculousness that seems to endear him to everyone, Harold’s antics begin to reshape how the studio views him. Eventually, the studio owner, recognizing the accidental brilliance in Harold's blunders, comes to see him not as a liability but as a potential comic genius, reshaping his fate and illuminating the idea that sometimes, the path to success is paved with failure and laughter.
hard_neg: On a family farm, Jane Falbury leads a practical life, grounded in hard work and responsibility, until the arrival of her sister Abigail, a glamorous actress who brings her theater troupe along for a much-needed rehearsal space. At first, Jane and her steadfast housekeeper, Esme, are hesitant to open their barn for this colorful band of performers; however, their hospitality soon earns them unexpected support in the form of eager helping hands that lighten the burdens of farm life. As the troupe begins to settle in, Jane finds herself entangled in a complex web of emotions when Joe Ross, the troupe's director, starts to show interest in her. Though initially engaged to Orville, an equally grounded man, Jane's feelings for Joe grow, especially after Abigail, caught in a whirlwind of her own frustrations, unleashes her anger and storms off, leaving Joe vulnerable and more drawn to Jane than ever before. This unexpected love triangle forces Jane to confront her own aspirations and desires, ultimately leading her to question the life she has always known and whether the allure of the stage—and love—might beckon her to a different path, one that reflects the tensions between ambition and loyalty, the known and the unknown.

The actors and actresses, including the director, Joe Ross (Gene Kelly), repay her hospitality by doing chores around the farm. Although Joe is engaged to Abigail, he begins to fall in love with Jane after Abigail leaves him in an angry fit. Similarly, although Jane is engaged to Orville (Eddie Bracken), she falls in love with Joe.

sent0: In his work, Heinrich Heine describes his journey as a student from Göttingen, where he had attended the Georgia Augusta University in 1820/21, through the Harz range and over its highest mountain, the Brocken summit, to the small town of Ilsenburg. During the trip he meets well known and unknown contemporaries, who he sometimes describes in detail and compares with other people, sometimes historical protagonists. For example, he meets in Göttingen the physician and (like Heine) Jewish Burschenschaft member, Karl Friedrich Heinrich Marx and recounts their exchange about medicine as well as Marx' treatise, Goettingen in medicinischer, physischer und historischer Hinsicht ('Göttingen From a Medical, Physical and Historic Perspective'). Nature is also a subject of this travel account: In the work, he mentions all the rest points and overnight stops made by this group of travelling poets: The route, which took Heinrich Heine about four weeks, has become a trail that tourists can follow in and which, as the Heinrich Heine Way (Heinrich-Heine-Weg) is described in several travel guides. A duel, which was illegal at the time, between students is also a subject of his writings. Heine himself had to leave Göttingen University due to a duel affair after antisemitic hostilities; the Göttingen chapter reflects his bad experiences.
sent1: In the quietude of a small town, Roland reminisces about his formative years as a student, recounting the tumultuous journey that led him from a state of poverty to the dusty halls of university. As he reflects on his past, he vividly remembers the streets of Berlin, where his youthful exuberance often led him to drunken escapades, much to the chagrin of his father, who, concerned for his son’s future, shipped him off to the countryside for an education. The narrative unfolds as Roland finds himself captivated by the enigmatic aura of his college professor, a man whose intellect both inspires and isolates him. Torn between admiration and a profound fear of exposure, Roland seeks solace in the professor’s secluded home, where the societal constructs of love—Philia, Èros, and Agápe—interweave in a nuanced tapestry of relationships. Although these concepts remain unspoken, their essence pulses through the pages, marking a turning point in Roland's life, as he grapples with the weight of his emotions and the complexity of human connections, ultimately questioning whether he can ever truly belong in such a labyrinthine world.
hard_neg: Reisiger, a fervent seeker of stories, wanders through diverse landscapes and cultures, driven by an insatiable curiosity to unearth the tales of humanity. His life takes a pivotal turn when he meets and marries Franziska, a woman who embodies the warmth and stability he often lacks due to his relentless pursuit of adventure. However, as their lives unfold and Franziska becomes pregnant, Reisiger finds himself torn between domesticity and the call of distant lands, culminating in a painful cycle of departure and return. A haunting moment arises when a dying colleague implores him to return home, prompting a momentary reckoning with his choices. Yet, when the shadows of World War II loom over the horizon, Reisiger must once again heed the call to arms, leaving Franziska in a state of uncertainty and longing. Their story, fraught with love and sacrifice, reflects the broader themes of commitment and the struggle between personal ambition and familial duty, emphasizing the poignant reality that some journeys may never lead back home, leaving behind echoes of unfulfilled promises and yearning hearts.

sent0: In a small Peruvian village, Kansas Hopper, a seasoned stunt coordinator, finds himself in charge of the horses for a western film. The tranquility of the village is shattered when a tragic accident occurs on set, resulting in the death of an actor during a dangerous stunt. This harrowing experience prompts Kansas to reassess his life and, seeking solace, he decides to leave the tumult of the film industry behind and embrace a new beginning with a local woman, believing he has discovered paradise. However, his idyllic retreat is disrupted when he is unexpectedly drawn into a peculiar situation: the indigenous villagers are staging their own version of a western film, using makeshift cameras crafted from sticks, and reenacting scenes of violence from the movies without grasping the concept of cinematic illusion. This bizarre intertwining of reality and performance challenges Kansas’s understanding of his craft and forces him to confront the consequences of storytelling, reshaping his perceptions of what it means to create art.
sent1: In a bustling town, Grandma Tina arrives with a heart full of joy, ready to celebrate the wedding of her beloved nephew Alberto and his fiancée Ileana. However, her excitement is quickly overshadowed by the realization that a cherished medallion, a precious keepsake from her late husband, has been stolen. Determined to reclaim her lost treasure, Tina heads to the local police station to report the theft, but the bureaucratic delays leave her feeling frustrated and powerless. Unable to wait any longer, she takes matters into her own hands, embarking on a wild and messy quest to track down the thief herself. Through a series of comical mishaps and unexpected encounters, she stumbles upon the thief living at the same hotel, inadvertently uncovering a larger criminal operation in the process. In a dramatic twist of fate, she manages to help apprehend the entire gang, and as a result of her bravery and quick thinking, her grandson Alberto is able to marry Ileana. The community recognizes Tina's extraordinary efforts, and she is honored with the title of an honorary colonel in the police force, an outcome that not only validates her relentless pursuit but also redefines her role within the family and the community.
hard_neg: In a dark and twisted tale, conman Raymond Fernandez operates under the ruse of romance, using personal ads to lure wealthy women into his trap. His life takes a fateful turn when he meets Martha Beck, a woman equally entangled in deceit, who masquerades as his sister as they embark on a spree of manipulation. Together, they traverse the country, engaging in a chilling series of murders that leave a trail of devastated families in their wake. As their sinister partnership deepens, they are relentlessly pursued by determined homicide detectives Robinson and Hildebrandt, who piece together the fragmented clues left behind. This cat-and-mouse chase culminates in their eventual capture, bringing an end to their reign of terror. The outcome serves as a stark reminder of the darkness that can lurk beneath the surface of desire and ambition, revealing how the pursuit of greed can lead to unimaginable tragedy and the loss of humanity.

sent0: In a grand mansion perched on the outskirts of a village, a wealthy widower named Victor grapples with the haunting memories of his wife’s tragic descent into madness. In an effort to shield his two grown children from a similar fate, he locks them away, believing that their intense bond threatens their sanity. To oversee their mental well-being, he enlists the help of a dubious doctor known for his unorthodox methods, convinced that he alone can cure their unnatural affections for one another. As the days unfold, murder lurks in the shadows of the nearby village, instilling fear within the community, while a travelling priest appears, armed with sacred rites to confront the local demons that seem to plague the villagers. The intertwining of these lives reveals a complex tapestry of love, fear, and madness, leading to a climax where Victor must confront the consequences of his actions, ultimately discovering that isolation breeds more insanity than the very affections he sought to suppress.
sent1: Stefano, an aspiring artist, finds himself in a remote village nestled in the Valli di Comacchio, where he has been tasked with restoring a dilapidated fresco that depicts the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian. This fresco, painted on a crumbling wall of the local church, is the work of the enigmatic Legnani, a long-deceased artist whose life was shrouded in rumors of madness. As Stefano settles into the home once belonging to Legnani's sisters, he begins to develop a romantic relationship with Francesca, the town’s charming schoolteacher. However, as he digs deeper into the town's history and learns from the locals about Legnani’s dark past—filled with tales of his gruesome inspirations drawn from real-life tortures—Stefano feels a sense of dread grow within him. Murders erupt in the village, including the brutal killing of his employer, and he soon realizes that someone is intent on preventing him from uncovering the truth of Legnani’s legacy. The dance between love and horror becomes palpable as the community's quiet facade crumbles, forcing Stefano to confront the chilling reality of the artist's tormented genius and the madness that once enveloped the village.
hard_neg: In the picturesque setting of Brittany, Barbara, a devoted wife and mother, struggles with the emotional and ethical implications of her life with her husband, the esteemed professor of medicine, Georg Bertram, who once heroically saved her father’s life. Their union is tested when they have a child with severe mental disabilities, revealing Georg's cold, clinical nature that surfaces in his chilling proposal of euthanasia for their innocent child. Overwhelmed by his harshness, Barbara takes a bold step to protect her child by fleeing to Brittany, hoping that the tranquil surroundings and compassionate care will foster a semblance of improvement in their lives. Here, she encounters a kind-hearted doctor who embraces a more humane approach to medicine, providing a stark contrast to her husband’s detachment. Through her journey, Barbara grapples with the themes of maternal love, the sanctity of life, and the search for understanding in a society that often devalues those who are different. The transformative experience in Brittany ultimately leads Barbara to redefine her identity and challenge the oppressive expectations placed upon her, setting her on a path of empowerment and resilience.

While temporarily taking up residence in the house that had been previously owned by the two sisters of the deceased painter, Stefano begins a romance with a new, beautiful schoolteacher, Francesca (Francesca Marciano), meanwhile learning from various townspeople that the painter had been a madman who had derived his art from real life. Specifically, Stefano learns that the artist — assisted by his two equally-insane sisters — had been a killer who brutally tortured people to death as inspiration for his horrific paintings — a practice that had likely been used for the very painting he is in process of restoring. As Stefano is discouraged for his task throughout the town, some of the villagers are brutally killed — including his employer — and he comes to suspect that their murderer is trying to deter him from discovering the full truth behind the artist and his ominous legacy within the sleepy community.

sent0: A wealthy widower locks up his two grown-up children, afraid that they will go mad, as did his wife. He then invites a doctor of dubious reputation to supervise their mental health and cure them of the unnatural attraction they have for each other. Meanwhile, in the vicinity of the mansion, murders are happening in the local village and a travelling priest arrives to help drive out any local demons.
sent1: Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a village of the Valli di Comacchio area where he has been employed to restore a fresco depicting what appears to be the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, which has been painted on a rotting wall of the local church by a mysterious, long-dead artist named Legnani. While temporarily taking up residence in the house that had been previously owned by the two sisters of the deceased painter, Stefano begins a romance with a new, beautiful schoolteacher, Francesca (Francesca Marciano), meanwhile learning from various townspeople that the painter had been a madman who had derived his art from real life. Specifically, Stefano learns that the artist — assisted by his two equally-insane sisters — had been a killer who brutally tortured people to death as inspiration for his horrific paintings — a practice that had likely been used for the very painting he is in process of restoring. As Stefano is discouraged for his task throughout the town, some of the villagers are brutally killed — including his employer — and he comes to suspect that their murderer is trying to deter him from discovering the full truth behind the artist and his ominous legacy within the sleepy community.
hard_neg: Barbara is married to the distinguished professor of medicine Georg Bertram who once saved her father's life. When they have a mentally handicapped child together his clinical coldness comes to the fore and he wants to commit euthanasia on the child. She stops him and takes the child away to Brittany in the hope that a change of location and nursing will help them to improve. While there she encounters a much more sympathetic doctor.

sent0: In New York City, a hospital worker is found to have been devouring bodies in the morgue. Morgue assistant and anthropology expert Lori discovers he was from the Asian Molucca islands where she grew up. Dr. Peter Chandler investigates, and he and Lori discover that similar corpse mutilations have occurred in other city hospitals, where immigrants from this region are working. Peter leads an expedition to the islands to investigate, where he liaises with Doctor Obrero. Included are his assistant George, George's eager journalist girlfriend Susan, Lori, local boatsman Molotto assigned by Obrero, and three guides. The crew is hunted by cannibals and zombies, the latter created by the sinister Doctor Obrero, who is experimenting with corpses. Lori is accepted as queen of the cannibals and sends them off against the mad scientist and his zombie army.
sent1: In the early 1970s, while the majority of Americans were focused on events in Vietnam, the United States Army was secretly developing a way to resurrect and control dead bodies. Their intention was to have the dead fight instead of the living, but the experiments were shut down when the reanimated corpses were unable to control their hunger for human flesh. Thirty years later, the army decides to reopen the project. Grover City, because of its remote location, would be the home of their main testing facilities. Without warning, the Grover City experiments go horribly wrong and the reanimated corpses go on a rampage, eating everyone in sight. With the town overtaken by zombies, a group of High School seniors takes it upon themselves to fight back and find a cure for the disease.
hard_neg: Pine Cove suffers a major crisis when the town psychiatrist, Val Riordan — who has been haphazardly issuing prescriptions instead of dealing with the real mental problems of her patients — suffers a sudden bout of guilt and substitutes all of her patients' anti-depressants with placebos. At this same time, by coincidence, human-generated environmental activity stirs a prehistoric sea-beast from its underwater keep to come ashore. In addition to its ability to change form, the beast exudes a pheromone that inspires uncontrollable lust among the residents of Pine Cove and also lures some of them as prey. After mistakenly trying to mate with a fuel truck (causing an explosion), the beast hides in a trailer park, attracting the curiosity of local crazy lady and former B-movie star Molly Michon, who builds a rapport with the injured beast. Meanwhile, Theophilus Crowe, the town constable, investigates a strange suicide, the activities of his corrupt boss, and his adversely affected marijuana habit. When the beast (whom Molly has named "Steve") starts eating residents of Pine Cove and interfering with Theo's boss's methamphetamine business, Molly (who has become romantically involved with the beast) and Theo band together to make possible the beast's safe escape and to take down the boss at the same time.

sent0: Marcel Marx, formerly both a bohemian and struggling author, has given up his literary ambitions and relocated to the port city of Le Havre. He leads a simple life based around his wife, Arletty, his favourite bar and his low income profession as a shoeshiner. As Arletty suddenly becomes seriously ill, Marcel's path crosses with that of an underage illegal immigrant from Africa. Marcel and friendly neighbors and other townspeople help to hide him from the police. The police inspector may, or may not, be hot on their heels.
sent1: In the heart of Belgium, Edouard Pierson, a wool dealer who had fled to Australia following the devastation of World War II, finds himself back in his homeland amidst the familiar yet changed landscapes of his youth. Following the death of his beloved partner, Edouard must navigate the complexities of single parenthood, leaving behind his young daughter in Australia as he returns to assist his family with their wool business. The journey back is not merely physical; it stirs memories and emotions long buried, particularly as he reconnects with his estranged family, where old resentments and secrets bubble to the surface. In the midst of familial tensions, he encounters Jeanne, a spirited single mother with her own challenges, and a deep bond forms between them, one that forces Edouard to confront his past and his responsibilities. As their relationship intensifies, Edouard learns that love can be both a refuge and a burden, leading him to reevaluate his priorities as truths about his family emerge, ultimately redefining his understanding of connection and sacrifice.
hard_neg: Bruno, a 35-year-old architect who has crafted a beautiful life complete with a loving wife and child, unexpectedly finds himself grappling with an unsettling emptiness despite his apparent success. In a sudden decision driven by an internal crisis, he leaves behind the comfort of his home and family, seeking solitude and clarity. This journey leads him to the vibrant city of Santiago, where opportunity knocks in the form of a prestigious project that could mark his career. Accompanied by Fer, a captivating young history professor, Bruno embarks on a quest not only to design an iconic building but also to rediscover his own identity and purpose. As they explore the layers of the city's rich heritage, Bruno's relationship with Fer deepens, offering him new perspectives on life and love that starkly contrast with his previous existence. Ultimately, the project serves as a turning point, pushing Bruno to confront his deepest fears and desires, leading him down a path that is both liberating and fraught with unexpected challenges.

hard_neg: Bruno (Celhay) is a 35-year-old architect who leads a seemingly perfect life. He resides in a beautiful house with his wife and child and also owns a thriving architecture office. However, despite his comfortable lifestyle, he experiences a profound sense of unease. As a result, he decides to leave everything behind and move out to live alone, coincidentally when a businessman approaches him to design an icon for the city of Santiago. With newfound motivation, he embarks on a quest to search for heritage traces, accompanied by Fer (Emilio Edwards), a 29-year-old, energetic, and captivating gay history professor.

sent0: In the midst of a chaotic life marked by misdeeds and a thirst for quick gains, two swindlers find themselves imprisoned for stealing alcohol. However, upon their release, they seize an unexpected opportunity to work in a laboratory focused on developing a groundbreaking device aimed at regenerating old cows. The lab's owner, eager to use this innovative technology for rejuvenating his aging livestock, unwittingly sets the stage for a twist of fate. As the experimental process unfolds, something goes horrifically awry, leading to an unforeseen and bizarre consequence: the swindlers are unexpectedly rejuvenated into children, forcing them to confront the innocence they lost and the lives they must now navigate anew.
sent1: After a lifetime marked by poor choices and a criminal past, Ben Garvey finds himself in a precarious position when he loses his job due to the stigma surrounding his background. In an unexpected reunion, his brother Ricky, freshly released from prison, entices him with a reckless scheme to rob a laboratory of its valuable gold dust. However, their plans crumble spectacularly when the heist goes disastrously wrong, resulting in the tragic deaths of Ricky and his accomplices. Facing a death sentence, Ben shares a heart-wrenching farewell with his wife and daughter, conveying to his young girl that he will not return to them. As he prepares for his execution, an inexplicable twist occurs; instead of facing death, Ben finds himself hitching a ride with a stranger who informs him of a second chance at life as the new groundskeeper for a psychiatric hospital in a remote Oregon town. Although he wishes to return to his former life, the doors are closed. As Ben grapples with his new reality and the haunting memories of his family, he begins to question whether he has truly escaped death or if he has become embroiled in a more sinister scientific experiment involving himself and the other residents of the hospital.
hard_neg: Vera Kardina stands at the threshold of her family's estate, a vast expanse of the Donetsk steppe stretching before her, now orphaned and under the care of her aunt. As she approaches the grand yet desolate house, she is struck by the profound silence surrounding the place, which soon gives way to the gnawing sensation that this life might be unbearably monotonous. As days turn into weeks, her fears are confirmed; Aunt Dasha, who rules the household with an iron fist, reveals herself to be a pretentious provincial figure, while her grandfather, gluttonous and rude, adds to the pervasive discomfort. Moreover, the local doctor Neshchapov, who has allegedly taken an interest in Vera, presents as an insipid and bland character, leaving her increasingly frustrated. The activities that once offered her solace, such as hosting guests and visiting the church, grow increasingly tedious. With time, her dreams of finding love and making a difference in the community dissipate into the harsh reality of her surroundings. In a moment of misplaced anger towards her dull maid Alyona, an unexpected outburst echoes from her grandfather's old book, leading Vera to confront the unsettling realization that she has succumbed to the very environment she sought to escape. Ultimately, feeling trapped, she contemplates marrying the doctor she despises, believing it to be her only means of breaking free from the stifling atmosphere that now defines her life.

As he prepares for his execution, an inexplicable twist occurs; instead of facing death, Ben finds himself hitching a ride with a stranger who informs him of a second chance at life as the new groundskeeper for a psychiatric hospital in a remote Oregon town. Although he wishes to return to his former life, the doors are closed. As Ben grapples with his new reality and the haunting memories of his family, he begins to question whether he has truly escaped death or if he has become embroiled in a more sinister scientific experiment involving himself and the other residents of the hospital.
hard_neg:
Both of her parents now dead, the young Vera Kardina returns to her estate, run now by her aunt.
Approaching the house, lost in a vast Donetsk steppe, she enjoys the quietness of the place, but has the uneasy feeling that life here might be unbearably dull.
Things do not get better as time goes by. Aunt Dasha turns out to be somewhat vulgar provincial grand dame, insincere and occasionally obnoxious, the decrepit grandfather is rude and gluttonous, the local doctor Neshchapov who'd "fell in love with [Vera’s] photo portrait" (if the aunt is to be believed) seems vacuous and bland. Her two main distractions, entreating the guests and visiting the church, become more and more unbearable.
Soon Vera starts to realize that the dreams she'd cherished, of "finding true love here" and "helping the poor" were vain fantasies. At one point, getting mad with her rather slow-witted maid Alyona, she shouts at her and is instantly taken aback, realizing that the phrase she's just used ("Twenty five lashes for her!") is taken straight from her grandfather's old book. She eventually succumbs to the idea of marrying the doctor whom she intensely dislikes, just to get out of the house, and because this seems to be the only course to pursue.

sent0: In the midst of a chaotic life marked by misdeeds and a thirst for quick gains, two swindlers find themselves imprisoned for stealing alcohol. However, upon their release, they seize an unexpected opportunity to work in a laboratory focused on developing a groundbreaking device aimed at regenerating old cows. The lab's owner, eager to use this innovative technology for rejuvenating his aging livestock, unwittingly sets the stage for a twist of fate. As the experimental process unfolds, something goes horrifically awry, leading to an unforeseen and bizarre consequence: the swindlers are unexpectedly rejuvenated into children, forcing them to confront the innocence they lost and the lives they must now navigate anew.

As she approaches the grand yet desolate house, she is struck by the profound silence surrounding the place, which soon gives way to the gnawing sensation that this life might be unbearably monotonous. The activities that once offered her solace, such as hosting guests and visiting the church, grow increasingly tedious. With time, her dreams of finding love and making a difference in the community dissipate into the harsh reality of her surroundings. In a moment of misplaced anger towards her dull maid Alyona, an unexpected outburst echoes from her grandfather's old book, leading Vera to confront the unsettling realization that she has succumbed to the very environment she sought to escape. Ultimately, feeling trapped, she contemplates marrying the doctor she despises, believing it to be her only means of breaking free from the stifling atmosphere that now defines her life.

sent0: In a gripping tale of intrigue and courage, Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson emerge from the comfortable shadows of retirement, compelled by the specter of a mysterious murder that has haunted the quiet streets of London. Their investigation plunges them into a dark underworld where they uncover that a notorious American criminal organization known as The Scowrers has enlisted the nefarious genius of Professor Moriarty to exact a chilling vengeance on John Douglas, the informant whose testimony had previously incarcerated them. As Holmes employs his unparalleled deductive reasoning, he unravels a web of deceit and betrayal, ultimately outsmarting Moriarty, solving the murder, and ensuring that justice prevails against the sinister machinations of evil. The story unfolds against a rich backdrop of the 1930s, weaving in a flashback that explores a dangerous infiltration reminiscent of James McParland's historical endeavor against the Molly Maguires, creating a compelling narrative that not only entertains but also challenges the boundaries of time and truth.
sent1: In the twilight of his career, Simon, a retired police inspector, finds himself reluctantly confined to a retirement home, a decision he deeply resents. However, a spark of camaraderie ignites when he forms an unexpected friendship with Alfred, a fellow resident who possesses a wealth of stories from his own colorful past. The tranquility of their newfound friendship is shattered when Alfred meets an untimely demise under suspicious circumstances, instantly raising Simon's suspicions. Despite the management’s reassurances and the gendarmerie’s dismissal of the event as mere accident, Simon's instincts scream foul play. Fueled by a determination to unearth the truth, he enlists the help of Francky, a rebellious rocker with a penchant for mischief, and together they embark on a thrilling investigation that leads them through a maze of secrets hidden within the seemingly mundane walls of the retirement home. As they unravel clues, the duo faces several turning points, each revealing the darker motivations behind their fellow residents, and ultimately, Simon's quest for justice not only redefines his purpose but also uncovers a network of deceit that transcends age and circumstance.
hard_neg: In a narrative that unfolds with tension and grit, Frank Warren, a seasoned treasury agent, is assigned the daunting task of dismantling the operations of a formidable mob crime boss whose influence stretches deep into the fabric of society. As he delves into the underbelly of organized crime, Warren encounters roadblocks at every turn, from terrified witnesses who refuse to speak to a police force riddled with corruption and complicity. The threats become palpable as he discovers that many informants end up silenced, their fates a grim reminder of the stakes at play. Yet, in a surprising twist, Warren finds an unexpected ally in a shadowy figure within the criminal world, who offers critical information that could shift the balance of power. This unlikely alliance forces him to navigate a treacherous landscape where trust is a rare commodity, and as he weighs his options, the story culminates in a thrilling confrontation that reshapes his understanding of justice and morality. Ultimately, Warren's journey not only challenges his professional resolve but also reveals the complexities of human nature amidst the fierce battle between right and wrong.

sent0:
Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson come out of retirement to investigate a mysterious murder. They find that an American criminal organisation called The Scowrers has asked evil mastermind Professor Moriarty to wreak vengeance on John Douglas, the informant who sent them to prison. Holmes outsmarts Moriarty, solves the murder and brings Moriarty to justice.
Like all the films featuring Wontner as Holmes, this one has a contemporary 1930s setting, making the flashback sequence pitting undercover detective Douglas against the Scowrers somewhat problematical since, historically, the real-life incident on which this sequence is based, Pinkerton operative James McParland's infiltration of the Molly Maguires, occurred in the 1870s, a full half-century earlier.

sent0: Marie-Louise, portrayed by the talented Anne Girardot, is a woman whose emotional attachment to her ex-husband, Alexandre, played by the charismatic Jean Rochefort, refuses to fade, even after their separation. The couple's love story took a tumultuous turn when Alexandre, disillusioned by Marie-Louise's participation in leftist marches and demonstrations, chose to leave her behind. Despite the passage of time and the changes in her life, including her daughter Laura's burgeoning romance with Marc, a charming young man embodied by Bernard Fresson, Marie-Louise clings to the hope that Alexandre will eventually return to her. Laura, seeing her mother's unwavering devotion to a love long lost, supports Marie-Louise in her quest, urging her to fight for what she believes is a worthy cause. However, Marie-Louise's fixation on this dream blinds her to the reality around her. When her son, played by Bernard Le Coq, finally confronts her with the truth—that Alexandre is not returning—the emotional weight of this revelation proves too much to bear, leading to dire consequences that cast a long shadow over her life. Jean Rochefort's portrayal marks a significant moment in his career, as he steps into the role of a father to adult children, while the vibrant cast around him, including the youthful Claude Jade and the equally talented Bernard Fresson, brings a rich texture to this complex narrative of love, loss, and the human spirit's resilience in the face of heartache. This film, which features Rochefort distinctly sporting his iconic moustache, not only showcases the intricacies of familial relationships but also delves into the depths of how unyielding love can lead to a profound personal crisis.
sent1: In the vibrant working-class neighborhood of Marseille, Marius and Jeannette find themselves living in the same modest apartment complex, navigating the struggles of their day-to-day lives in close proximity to one another. Marius, a security guard at an abandoned cement plant, faces challenges not only from his physical limitations due to a disability but also from the looming threat of the plant's demolition, which forces him to squat in the old building to save on living expenses. Meanwhile, Jeannette, a dedicated single mother, juggles the demands of raising her two children on her limited paycheck earned as a supermarket checkout operator. Their lives intersect dramatically one fateful day when Jeannette attempts to steal two cans of paint from the cement lot, only to be caught by Marius, who instinctively chases after her. The following day, in an unexpected turn, Marius approaches Jeannette's door with an apology and the very cans of paint she had tried to pilfer, setting the stage for a tentative relationship to blossom. As they begin to navigate their new connection, both Marius and Jeannette grapple with the emotional scars left by their past relationships, making them hesitant to fully commit to one another. The dichotomy between Jeannette’s romantic fantasies and Marius’ grounded, practical approach to life creates a tension that they must confront if their budding love is to flourish. Ultimately, their journey becomes a poignant exploration of love's transformative power, demonstrating that healing and acceptance are possible, even for those who have suffered greatly.
hard_neg: The narrative of Australia centers around Edouard Pierson, a Belgian-born wool dealer who embarks on a new chapter in life after World War II by emigrating to Australia. The story adopts a poignant twist as Edouard returns to his native Belgium to aid his family with their wool business, but he carries with him the burden of being a single father, having lost his girlfriend under tragic circumstances. This loss has left him to raise his young daughter alone, a fact that his family is blissfully unaware of, adding layers of complexity to his visit. Upon his return, Edouard encounters Jeanne, a captivating woman who, like him, is navigating the challenges of single parenthood. Their initial meeting sparks a deep connection, leading to an intense romance that flourishes under the shadow of their shared struggles. As the narrative unfolds, Edouard's relationship with his family fluctuates, filled with ups and downs as long-buried secrets and unresolved tensions come to light. The exploration of these familial dynamics serves as a backdrop to the central love story, revealing the intricate ways in which the past can shape the present. Ultimately, the conclusion ties together the strands of Edouard's life, highlighting the impact of love, loss, and reconciliation, and leaving the audience to reflect on how the complexities of relationships can redefine one's sense of home and belonging.

To be older, he had a moustache, since this film his trademark, which he had removed only once (1996 in "Ridicule").

This loss has left him to raise his young daughter alone, a fact that his family is blissfully unaware of, adding layers of complexity to his visit. Upon his return, Edouard encounters Jeanne, a captivating woman who, like him, is navigating the challenges of single parenthood. Their initial meeting sparks a deep connection, leading to an intense romance that flourishes under the shadow of their shared struggles. As the narrative unfolds, Edouard's relationship with his family fluctuates, filled with ups and downs as long-buried secrets and unresolved tensions come to light. The exploration of these familial dynamics serves as a backdrop to the central love story, revealing the intricate ways in which the past can shape the present. Ultimately, the conclusion ties together the strands of Edouard's life, highlighting the impact of love, loss, and reconciliation, and leaving the audience to reflect on how the complexities of relationships can redefine one's sense of home and belonging.

sent0:
Marie-Louise (Anne Girardot) is a woman whose love for her ex-husband will not die. The Lawyer Alexandre (Jean Rochefort) left her, because she attended a few leftist marches and demonstrations. While her daughter Laura (Claude Jade) falls in love with Marc (Bernard Fresson), Marie-Louise keeps hoping that Alexandre will come back to her. Laura helps her to fight for love and Marie-Louise is so attached to this idea that when her son (Bernard Le Coq) finally convinces her that he will never return, the realization has dire consequences.
Jean Rochefort played his first big major role. In this film he played with 41 years a family father of adult children (the young Claude Jade was already 23 and Bernard Fresson who has played Jade's fiancé one year younger than Rochefort). To be older, he had a moustache, since this film his trademark, which he had removed only once (1996 in "Ridicule").
sent1: Marius and Jeannette live in the same working-class apartment complex in Marseille, in close proximity to their neighbors. The lame Marius is a security guard at an abandoned cement works, and since the company has gone out of business and the plant will soon be demolished, he is squatting in order to save money. Jeannette is a single mother raising her two children on her own on a meagre supermarket checkout operator salary. They meet when Jeannette tries to steal two cans of paint from the cement lot, and Marius catches her and tries to chase her. The following day Marius comes to her door to apologize and brings her the two cans of paint. A relationship soon develops between them, but as both have been wounded by marital difficulties and life in general, they are hesitant to become committed. It does not help that Jeannette's romantic fantasy notions are different from Marius' practical ideas. The two must learn how to love again in order for their relationship to blossom.
hard_neg: Australia is about Edouard Pierson, a Belgian-born wool dealer who emigrated to Australia after World War II. The movie actual takes place in Belgium as he returns to his homeland to assist his family with their wool business. Edouard was left a single father after his girlfriend died and when he goes to Belgium he leaves behind this young girl, whom his family don't know about. He meets a beautiful woman, Jeanne, another single parent, and an intense relationship develops. Edouard's relationship with his family has its ups and downs and many secrets are revealed before the movie's conclusion ties everything together.

sent0: Antonio, a brazen, individualistic ETA terrorist, travels with two fellow cell members, Carlos and Lourdes to Madrid, where they intend to carry out a terrorist attack on a police station. Just like Lourdes, with whom he shares a complex romantic liaison, Antonio is caught in a downward spiral of disenchantment and despondency with respect to the organization and the life he has led so far. He moves into the area under the guise of an unassuming photographer for the press, and finds himself falling for his neighbor, Charo, a naive prostitute with an impending drug problem who is unaware of Antonio's activities. She reciprocates, and Antonio uses her whimsical desire to have their first tryst in Granada as an excuse to flee Madrid right after he shoots a police officer. Meanwhile, matters become complicated when Antonio's identity as a terrorist is made public and Charo's sleazy, drug-addicted acquaintance Lisardo, incidentally an informant, gives Antonio's identity away to corrupt police officer Rafa. The film ends on a tragical note as the car bomb (containing 100 kg worth of explosives) and the police car carrying Charo haplessly converge in front of the police station. Fuelled by his love, a self-destructive streak, or both, Antonio follows the car to the station gate right as Carlos presses the detonator.
sent1: In the heart of Paris, the anarchist group known as 'Nada' becomes fixated on an audacious plan to kidnap the United States Ambassador to France, intending to leverage his life for a hefty ransom. While the majority of the members buzz with excitement over the scheme, it is teacher Treuffais who stands as a lone dissenting voice, adamant in his refusal to partake in the morally dubious operation. Their plot unfolds in a brothel frequented by the Ambassador, where the atmosphere grows tense and dangerous. Tragically, things spiral out of control when a police officer and an undercover agent are killed in the chaos of the kidnapping. The Minister of the Interior, seeing opportunity amidst the horror, instructs Commissioner Goémond to track down the group's hideout, insinuating that the untimely death of the hostage could serve to sway public sentiment against the Left. In a whirlwind of violence, the police storm the group's refuge, and one by one, the members fall until only Diaz remains. In a desperate act, Diaz executes his hostage before fleeing into the night. Meanwhile, Goémond, having arrested and mercilessly interrogated Treuffais, lies in wait in Treuffais' apartment, convinced that the desperate Diaz will lead him to answers. The story culminates in a deadly confrontation, where both Goémond and Diaz perish in a hail of gunfire, leaving Treuffais to call a newspaper to reveal the harrowing tale of the Nada group, a testament to the chaos and moral decay wrought by their acts.
hard_neg: Marie, a young girl orphaned at a tender age, finds herself taken in by a bar-owner and his wife in the bustling port of Marseille, but her new life turns grim as she is thrust into the role of an exploited servant. The indifference of her adoptive parents weighs heavily upon her, and although she becomes the object of desire for Petit Paul, a brutish figure who is more interested in her body than her spirit, her heart belongs to Jean, a hardworking dockworker whose kindness offers a glimmer of hope in her dark world. However, circumstances force Marie into a reluctant life with Petit Paul, leading Jean to pursue them to a nearby fairground, igniting a fierce confrontation between the two men. In the ensuing chaos, a policeman is tragically stabbed, resulting in Jean's arrest while Petit Paul escapes unscathed. Time passes, and a year later, Jean unexpectedly finds Marie again, only to discover her living in a squalid existence with a sick baby, her resources drained by Petit Paul's incessant drinking. Determined to help, Jean enlists the support of a compassionate neighbor, a crippled woman who offers comfort and assistance, yet their budding camaraderie draws the ire of Petit Paul, who, fueled by suspicion and jealousy, returns with a gun for a violent showdown. In a dramatic and unexpected twist, it is the crippled woman who seizes the opportunity to fight back, obtaining the weapon and fatally shooting Petit Paul. The story reaches its bittersweet conclusion as Jean and Marie finally grasp the chance to be together, their lives forever altered by hardship and loss, yet finding solace in the understanding that love can emerge from ruin, even if it bears the scars of their painful past.

hard_neg: Marie (Gina Manès) was an orphan adopted by a bar-owner and his wife in the port of Marseille, and now she is harshly exploited by them as a servant in the bar. She is desired by Petit Paul (Edmond van Daële), a thuggish layabout, but is secretly in love with Jean (Léon Mathot), a dockworker.  Marie is forced to leave with Petit Paul, but Jean follows them to a fairground where the two men fight.  In the brawl a policeman is stabbed and, while Petit Paul escapes, Jean is arrested and gaoled.  A year later, Jean rediscovers Marie, now with a sick baby and living with Petit Paul, who spends all their money on drink.  Jean tries to support Marie, aided by a crippled woman (Marie Epstein, credited as "Mlle Marice"), who lives next door; but Petit Paul, warned by gossiping neighbours that Jean is seeing Marie, returns for a violent confrontation, this time armed with a gun. In the ensuing struggle, the crippled woman obtains the gun and kills Petit Paul.  In an epilogue, we see Jean and Marie finally free to love each other, though their faces suggest that experience has taken its toll on their lives.

sent1: The anarchist group "Nada" decides to kidnap the United States Ambassador to France and demand a ransom for his release. Although some group members are reluctant to the plan, teacher Treuffais alone refuses to participate in the venture. During the operation, carried out in a brothel which the Ambassador regularly visits, a police officer and an undercover agent are killed. The Minister of the Interior orders Commissioner Goémond to find the hideout of the group, implying that the death of the hostage could be useful to the state as it would turn the public's opinion against the Left. During the attack on the group's refuge, all members except Diaz are killed, who executes his hostage before he flees. Goémond, who had arrested and violently interrogated Treuffais, waits for Diaz in Treuffais' apartment, convinced that Diaz will show up sooner or later. During the final shootout, both Goémond and Diaz are killed. Treuffais rings up a newspaper, offering to tell the full story of the Nada group.

sent0: After losing their jobs as security guards, best friends Ivan (John Cusack) and Josh (Tim Robbins) start a music video production company called "Video Aces". When they meet their childhood heroes, 1970s soul duo Swanky Modes (Sam Moore and Junior Walker), Ivan and Josh concoct a scheme to give them a new audience by hijacking a Menudo concert, getting them to perform in Menudo's place, and broadcasting it live across the country on a television satellite hook-up. The movie also features a fake ad spot for a real Los Angeles restaurant, Roscoe's House of Chicken 'n Waffles. Notable appearances in the film include: Mary Crosby, of the soap opera Dallas; character actors Clu Gulager and Doug McClure; football player Lyle Alzado; 1960s actress Connie Stevens; Soul Train host Don Cornelius; singer Courtney Love; Navasota singer King Cotton; original "Human Beat-Box" Doug E. Fresh; ska-punk band Fishbone (who also perform the incidental score) as "Ranchbone"; The Dead Boys and The Lords of the New Church singer Stiv Bators; Ted Nugent; "Weird Al" Yankovic; and Dead Kennedys singer Jello Biafra, in a cameo as an FBI agent.
sent1: In a small town, six friends, driven by their shared aspiration for fame, embark on an ambitious and utterly naive project to create what they claim will be the most innocent adult film the world has ever seen. Despite their glaring lack of experience and knowledge, they refuse to be deterred, pouring their hearts and souls into every aspect of the production, from casting to directing, even as chaos unfolds around them. Their determination ultimately culminates in a screening night at their local bar, where excitement fills the air, only to be shattered when the brother of an actress, infuriated by her minimal involvement, storms in, seizing the film reel and dousing it with whiskey before igniting it in a fiery act of revenge. This shocking moment leads to the harrowing realization that the original print of their hard work has been incinerated, leaving nothing but ashes behind. Yet, in an unexpected twist of fate, the crew's cinematographer had been diligently documenting the entire journey on mini DV tapes, capturing not just the film-making process but the emotional highs and lows. With a rush of creativity born from disaster, he pieces together a heartwarming and humorous documentary, stripped of the originally intended content but rich with the spirit of their endeavors. This new project, aptly titled The Amateurs, resonates with audiences everywhere, transforming their misfortune into a celebrated success on the independent film circuit.
hard_neg: At the tender age of 13, Lymon burst onto the music scene with the infectious sound of Frankie Lymon & the Teenagers, captivating fans with their 1956 hit "Why Do Fools Fall in Love?" and shining brightly in the movie Rock, Rock, Rock, only to find his life spiraling out of control as he transitioned from the exuberance of youth to the sobering realities of adulthood. Following a promising start with Mr. Rock and Roll, his solo singing career quickly faltered, and by the age of 18, he found himself grappling with the devastating aftermath of fame, ultimately succumbing to a heroin overdose just seven years later. The narrative weaves through his tragic life, juxtaposing the exhilarating highs of his musical achievements with the painful lows as three women—Zola Taylor of The Platters, petty thief Elizabeth Waters from Philadelphia, and schoolteacher Emira Eagle—navigate the legal battles over Lymon's royalties in a courtroom drama that reveals the profound impact of his life and legacy. The courtroom scenes serve as haunting flashbacks that illustrate both the fleeting nature of fame and the personal demons that plagued Lymon, culminating in a poignant conclusion where Emira emerges victorious, reclaiming Frankie's estate amidst a backdrop of nostalgia and sorrow. As viewers watch the credits roll, they are left with the bittersweet echo of Lymon's voice singing "Goody Goody," a reminder of his once-great talent and the tragic fate that followed.

Lymon was 13 years old when the teenage group Frankie Lymon & the Teenagers erupted from radios and jukeboxes with their 1956 hit "Why Do Fools Fall in Love?" and appeared in the movie Rock, Rock, Rock (1956).
After Mr. Rock and Roll (1957), Lymon started a solo singing career, but it all fell apart. Lymon's career was over by the time he was 18 years old, and he died of a heroin overdose seven years later.
Jumping from the 1950s to the 1960s, the film traces the rise and fall of Lymon (Larenz Tate) in a series of flashbacks as courtroom claims on Lymon's royalties are outlined by three women: Zola Taylor (Halle Berry) of the R&B group The Platters; Elizabeth Waters (Vivica A. Fox), a petty thief from Philadelphia; and schoolteacher Emira Eagle (Lela Rochon). Ending credits show the real Frankie Lymon singing his song "Goody Goody".
Little Richard also makes a courtroom appearance, while Miguel A. Nunez Jr. portrays Little Richard in scenes set in the 1950s.
The film ends with Emira winning Frankie's estate.

sent0: After losing their jobs as security guards, best friends Ivan (John Cusack) and Josh (Tim Robbins) start a music video production company called "Video Aces". When they meet their childhood heroes, 1970s soul duo Swanky Modes (Sam Moore and Junior Walker), Ivan and Josh concoct a scheme to give them a new audience by hijacking a Menudo concert, getting them to perform in Menudo's place, and broadcasting it live across the country on a television satellite hook-up. The movie also features a fake ad spot for a real Los Angeles restaurant, Roscoe's House of Chicken 'n Waffles. Notable appearances in the film include: Mary Crosby, of the soap opera Dallas; character actors Clu Gulager and Doug McClure; football player Lyle Alzado; 1960s actress Connie Stevens; Soul Train host Don Cornelius; singer Courtney Love; Navasota singer King Cotton; original "Human Beat-Box" Doug E. Fresh; ska-punk band Fishbone (who also perform the incidental score) as "Ranchbone"; The Dead Boys and The Lords of the New Church singer Stiv Bators; Ted Nugent; "Weird Al" Yankovic; and Dead Kennedys singer Jello Biafra, in a cameo as an FBI agent.
sent1:
Six friends come together to try to find fame and fortune by making the world's most innocent adult movie ever made. Although they have literally no idea what they're doing, they keep at it and are determined to accomplish their goal...no matter what gets in the way.
After the clueless crew finally completes the film, they screen it for the first time at the local bar. The brother of a girl whose scene ultimately was not used (besides some brief shots of her face) barges in, takes the reel off the projector, covers it in whiskey and sets it aflame.
It is revealed that this was the only print they had and that all their work has been lost. However, the cinematographer had been videotaping the entire production process on mini DV tapes. He then edits these down into a completely innocent documentary, minus the nudity and sex scenes, about the trials and tribulations of making (and then losing) their original film. This new version is released as the titular The Amateurs which becomes a critical and financial success on the independent film circuit.
hard_neg: At the tender age of 13, Lymon burst onto the music scene with the infectious sound of Frankie Lymon & the Teenagers, captivating fans with their 1956 hit "Why Do Fools Fall in Love?" and shining brightly in the movie Rock, Rock, Rock, only to find his life spiraling out of control as he transitioned from the exuberance of youth to the sobering realities of adulthood. Following a promising start with Mr. Rock and Roll, his solo singing career quickly faltered, and by the age of 18, he found himself grappling with the devastating aftermath of fame, ultimately succumbing to a heroin overdose just seven years later. The narrative weaves through his tragic life, juxtaposing the exhilarating highs of his musical achievements with the painful lows as three women—Zola Taylor of The Platters, petty thief Elizabeth Waters from Philadelphia, and schoolteacher Emira Eagle—navigate the legal battles over Lymon's royalties in a courtroom drama that reveals the profound impact of his life and legacy. The courtroom scenes serve as haunting flashbacks that illustrate both the fleeting nature of fame and the personal demons that plagued Lymon, culminating in a poignant conclusion where Emira emerges victorious, reclaiming Frankie's estate amidst a backdrop of nostalgia and sorrow. As viewers watch the credits roll, they are left with the bittersweet echo of Lymon's voice singing "Goody Goody," a reminder of his once-great talent and the tragic fate that followed.

sent0: Set in the enigmatic Briar Patch of the Tikrit Expanse, which is an area rich in both mystery and danger, the narrative unfolds aboard Deep Space 12, a bustling space station orbiting the tranquil planet Ba'ku. This station serves as a home and operational hub for a diverse array of officers who navigate the complexities of interstellar diplomacy and conflict. As the series progresses, the crew becomes embroiled in an ongoing struggle against the technologically advanced Grey, an enigmatic species whose motives are as elusive as their capabilities, marking them as the central antagonists whose threat looms large over the crew's daily endeavors. Alongside the Grey, the narrative introduces a colorful array of characters, including a cunning Andorian trader and the formidable Tholians, all of whom add layers of tension and intrigue to the story. The crew's journey is further complicated by the discovery of mysterious ancient alien artifacts that captivate their curiosity and serve as a pivotal narrative device, guiding their actions and decisions as they interact with familiar species from the rich Star Trek universe, including the Andorians, Ba'ku, Klingons, Romulans, Son'a, and Tzenkethi, all of whom contribute to the unfolding drama in unexpected ways.
sent1: In the gripping year of 1943, a clandestine project known as the Philadelphia Experiment led to the US Navy ship USS Eldridge vanishing under mysterious circumstances, thrusting it into the annals of conspiracy and intrigue. Fast forward to the contemporary world of 2012, a group of ambitious researchers embarks on a dangerous quest to replicate the enigmatic experiment, believing they can unlock its secrets. However, their reckless ambitions lead to unintended consequences, as the Eldridge re-emerges, seemingly having traveled through time and space, carrying with it the last surviving sailor of the original crew. This survivor, now aged and weathered, unexpectedly reunites with his estranged granddaughter, who is both fascinated and horrified by the stories of his past. Meanwhile, the ship continues to drift through the corridors of time, ensnaring others within its ghostly hull and forcing them to confront their fates. As the research company learns of the chaos, they resolve to eliminate all witnesses to the incident, prompting a fierce struggle between those who wish to protect the survivors and the merciless operatives intent on covering up the truth. This conflict serves as a powerful reflection of not just the dangers of meddling with time, but also the lengths one will go to for family and justice, as they fight against overwhelming odds to restore the past and set things right.
hard_neg: Upon receiving a significant promotion that places him at the helm of the Statistical Section, the secretive headquarters of French military intelligence, Georges Picquart finds himself engulfed in a web of deception that challenges his understanding of justice and loyalty. Initially tasked with overseeing operations, Picquart's investigation into the infamous case of Alfred Dreyfus, a man wrongfully convicted of espionage and sentenced to life on the notorious Devil's Island, reveals cracks in the carefully constructed edifice of military righteousness. As he delves deeper, he uncovers a chilling reality: the evidence that condemned Dreyfus is not only flimsy but has been deliberately manipulated by military officials desperate to hide their failures. The harrowing truth intensifies as Picquart learns that the actual spy, the one who had betrayed France, remains free and actively undermining national security. Despite being warned off by his superiors to cease his inquiries, Picquart's sense of duty compels him to persist, putting his career and life at grave risk. His relentless pursuit of truth not only aims to exonerate an innocent man but also seeks to hold accountable those who have betrayed the very principles they vowed to uphold, embodying a powerful struggle against institutional corruption and the pursuit of justice in a world rife with moral ambiguity.

Based in the Briar Patch (Tikrit Expanse) introduced in Star Trek: Insurrection, on Deep Space 12, a space station in orbit of the planet Ba'ku. Early in the series, an advanced species, the Grey, are introduced and become the primary villain, with a story arc that spans the entire series. hard_neg: Upon being promoted to run the Statistical Section, the top secret headquarters of French military intelligence, Georges Picquart begins to discover that the evidence that was used to convict Alfred Dreyfus of espionage, which resulted in his imprisonment for life on Devil's Island, is flimsy at best. As he investigates further, he discovers that the military and the government doctored much of the evidence. Moreover, the spy who actually passed the information Dreyfus was convicted of sending to the Germans is actually still operating. Warned off the investigation by his superiors, Picquart persists, risking his career and his life, to free an innocent man from unjust imprisonment and to stop a spy operating within the military, who has gone unpunished.

sent1: In 1943, the US Navy ship USS Eldridge disappears, due to the Philadelphia Experiment.  In the present day (2012), researchers try to recreate the experiment, which has the unintended consequence of making the Eldridge reappear, apparently having traveled through time.  The sole survivor of the original experiment is aboard, and meets up with his now-adult granddaughter.  The Eldridge continues to drift through time and space, trapping others aboard it.  The research company attempts to cover up the incident by trying to kill everyone involved.  A struggle ensues as others try to protect the survivors, and return them to their original times and places.

sent0: In the troubled backdrop of 1760s France, a young girl named Suzanne Simonin finds her life irrevocably altered when her parents force her into a convent, believing that as an illegitimate child, she must atone for her mother’s perceived sins. Initially, she is met with kindness from the abbess, who provides a semblance of comfort in an otherwise harsh environment. However, the death of this nurturing figure leads to the arrival of a new abbess, whose oppressive demeanor and strict rules turn Suzanne’s sanctuary into a prison of despair. As the maltreatment and physical abuse escalate, Suzanne grapples with the dire consequences of her vows and the crushing weight of her suffering, which strips away her sense of self and autonomy. It is not until a glimmer of hope arrives through a friend’s words, suggesting that liberation from her vows may be possible, that Suzanne begins to envision a life beyond the confines of the convent, stirring a fierce determination to reclaim her identity and escape the torment that has consumed her existence.
sent1: In a dark twist of fate, a woman finds herself wrongfully accused of a crime that her husband has committed, leading to her imprisonment and the shattering of her dreams. While behind bars, she endures the heart-wrenching experience of giving birth, only to have her child taken from her and put up for adoption, deepening her anguish and despair. The flicker of hope reignites when fresh evidence emerges, exonerating her and revealing her husband's deceit. With her freedom finally restored, she embarks on a desperate journey to find her daughter, fueled by the longing to reconnect and reclaim the life that was unjustly taken from her. This quest not only symbolizes her fight against the injustices she faced but also embodies her determination to rebuild her shattered family, drawing upon her resilience and love as guiding forces through the darkest of times.
hard_neg: In the parched surroundings of N'Djamena, Chad, Amina's already precarious existence is dealt a devastating blow when she learns that her 15-year-old daughter Maria is pregnant. This revelation shatters their fragile world, as Maria, terrified and unwilling, faces a societal condemnation that leaves Amina feeling helpless. In a nation where the law and religious beliefs deny women autonomy over their own bodies, Amina is thrust into an agonizing battle against the constraints that threaten to engulf her family. Desperation gnaws at her as she navigates the harsh realities of a culture that offers no support, her heart heavy with the fear that her daughter’s future may be irreparably damaged. Amina's struggle becomes a poignant reflection of the broader societal issues that imprison women like her, igniting a fierce maternal instinct to protect her child from a fate she feels is already sealed.

sent0: Taking place in the 1760s France, a young girl named Suzanne Simonin is forced by her parents to become a nun. She learns that as an illegitimate child, she is expected to atone for her mother's sin. Her abbess treats her kindly, but when the abbess dies and another takes her place, Suzanne considers breaking her vows. Due to the maltreatment and physical abuse she undergoes, she is thrown into a world of punishment in which she suffers dehumanization. Suzanne is filled with despair and mental torment. It is not until a friend gives Suzanne some hope that she may not have to remain a nun forever and that Suzanne's punishment lifts.
