When he comes later to the bar, he discusses some secret deal with René and then takes Dédée to a hotel for the night.
The two have fallen for each other and he would like to take her away with him, but this would need the agreement of René and of Marco. René is happy to do a favour to Francesco, happy to free Dédée from the obnoxious Marco, who he throws out into the street, and says he is happy to drive Dédée to Francesco's ship once he has closed the bar for the night.
While Francesco is waiting on the jetty for Dédée to appear, Marco shoots him dead, drops his gun, and disappears. When René and Dédée arrive to find the body, they comb the nightspots of the city in search of Marco, eventually catching him at the railway station. At gunpoint they take him to a lonely spot where René, after knocking him out, runs the car over him.

sent0: Steve Randall is an independent trucker who is hired by an old friend to haul some freight. Only when Steve arrives at the warehouse does he discover he has been hired to haul away stolen goods. Steve wants no part of the plot and resists, but a cop is killed as they're committing the burglary, and all except one manage to get away. Later, after kidnapping and assaulting Steve, the criminals, led by Walt Radak, threaten to mutilate Randall's wife unless Steve confesses to the murder committed by Radak's brother, captured during the theft and sentenced to death for the cop-killing. Steve plays along with the criminals just long enough to escape. He takes his wife and leaves town, heading cross country. The couple are then pursued by both the cops and the crooks. Steve then discovers his wife is pregnant with their first child, making the stakes even higher that they get away to safety.
sent1:
Two NASCAR hopefuls, driver Larry Rayder and his mechanic Deke Sommers, successfully execute a supermarket heist to finance their jump into big-time auto racing. They extort $150,000 in cash from a supermarket manager by holding his wife and daughter hostage.
In making their escape, they are confronted by Larry's one-night stand, Mary Coombs. She coerces them to take her along for the ride in their souped-up 1966 Chevrolet Impala. The unorthodox sheriff, Captain Everett Franklin, obsessively pursues the trio in a dragnet, only to find his outmoded patrol cars unable to catch Larry, Mary, and Deke after they ditch the Impala for a 1969 Dodge Charger R/T 440 at a flea market.
As part of the escape plan, Larry's vehicle enters an expansive grid of walnut groves, where the trees provide significant cover from aerial tracking, and the many intersecting roads ("with sixty distinct and separate exits") make road blocks ineffective. The trio evades several Dodge Polara patrol cars, one of them a specially prepared high-performance police interceptor that effectively keeps up with the Charger, and even Captain Franklin himself in a Bell JetRanger helicopter. Believing they've finally beaten the police, Larry and company meet their doom when they collide with a freight train pulled by an Alco S-1 locomotive, which unexpectedly emerges from a walnut grove.
hard_neg: Dédée, a vulnerable woman caught in a web of exploitation, is forced to escape from France and finds herself in Antwerp, working at René's bar under the watchful eye of her cruel pimp, Marco, who also serves as the doorman. One fateful evening, as she wanders by the docks, she encounters Francesco, the kind-hearted Italian captain of a cargo ship who is familiar with René. What unfolds is an unexpected romance, as Francesco envisions a future free from the chains of her past life, but their plans are intricately tied to the whims of René and Marco. René, yearning to do a favor for Francesco, agrees to help Dédée break free from Marco's grasp, culminating in a moment of liberation when he forcefully ejects Marco from their lives. However, just as hope flickers on the horizon, tragedy strikes when Marco, in a fit of rage, shoots Francesco dead at the jetty. Dédée and René, devastated, must now hunt down the man who shattered their dreams. Their pursuit leads them through the city's dark corners, culminating in a confrontation at the railway station where they track Marco down. In a violent turn of events, René extracts a brutal revenge, running Marco over, while Dédée is left to grapple with the loss of both her love and her precarious freedom, a haunting reminder of the cycle of violence and despair that ensnares them all.

sent0: Ten years after having left high school, four friends, Momo, Léon, Alain and Bruno meet for the birth of the child of their friend Tomasi, who recently died. Together they dwell on their mutual memories concerning their last year at their Parisian high school during the seventies. This includes demonstrations, first experiments with drugs and women and also the contents of the courses.
sent1: Three young men, Jacques, Pierre, and Michel, navigate their carefree lives in a lively Parisian apartment, filled with laughter, parties, and the fleeting company of various girlfriends. They’ve made an unconventional pact: no girl is allowed to spend more than a night at their place, preserving their bachelorhood for as long as possible. However, everything shifts dramatically when Jacques' friend mentions a clandestine delivery of a package that turns out to be heroin; Jacques, caught up in the thrill of his stewarding job and the excitement of a month-long trip to Japan, unwittingly agrees to host it. The twist comes when an unexpected visitor—a baby, left at their doorstep by one of Jacques' ex-girlfriends—mistakenly leads Pierre and Michel to believe it is the very package they anticipated. This pivotal moment transforms their lives drastically, steering them into a chaotic whirlwind of angry gangsters, probing police, and the overwhelming reality of impending fatherhood. As the trio grapples with the responsibilities thrust upon them, they are forced to confront the consequences of their carefree choices, leading them to reflect on their past, their friendships, and the potential for a more meaningful existence as they transition from careless youth to reluctant adulthood.
hard_neg: Iris and her older sister Rose find themselves adrift in a turbulent sea of grief after their mother succumbs to a brain tumor, leaving them orphaned and vulnerable. With their father having abandoned them for Australia a decade prior, 24-year-old Rose, who is married and expecting a child, struggles to maintain stability amidst the chaos, while 19-year-old Iris spirals into a dark abyss of reckless behavior. Convinced that her mother favored Rose, Iris feels abandoned and unloved, prompting her to dump her boyfriend Gary and escape to a dilapidated apartment that she decorates with garish flowers, attempting to mask her inner turmoil with a facade of vibrancy. As she descends deeper into self-destructive choices, including a series of impulsive encounters with strangers, the tension between the sisters escalates, revealing deep-seated resentments and a struggle for autonomy. This narrative chronicles Iris’ chaotic journey through grief, encapsulating her desperate attempts to reclaim identity and agency in a world that feels overwhelmingly hostile, ultimately leading to a reckoning with her sister and herself as they navigate the turbulent waters of family, loss, and the quest for healing.

sent1: Three young men (Jacques, Pierre and Michel) share an apartment in Paris having many girlfriends and parties; during the film, they have signed a contract never to allow a girl to spend more than one night at their place. During one party, a friend of Jacques' tells him he has a compromising package (which turned out to be heroin) to deliver, and asks Jacques if he can leave it discreetly at their place. Jacques agrees and, as he works as a steward, flies away for a one-month trip in Japan telling Pierre and Michel about the package. One of Jacques' former girlfriends drops a baby before their door, making Pierre and Michel believe it is the package they are waiting for. Their lives change completely. The movie follows the bachelors as they deal with angry gangsters, suspicious cops, and the overwhelming responsibility of fatherhood.

sent0: In a universe where lost planets are a chaotic yet fascinating reality, the narrative unfolds around Lewis Orne, a determined agent of a highly secretive government agency tasked with the development and management of these enigmatic worlds. Lewis's journey begins with his successful identification of Hamal, a planet inhabited by a warlike civilization, which leads to his unexpected drafting into the Investigative Adjustment (I-A) division, a specialized branch that oversees the most perilous of planets. As he ventures through various star systems, his primary mission is to uphold peace and stability across the galaxy, often facing treacherous situations that put his life on the line. Meanwhile, on the planet Amel, a group of priests engaged in the arcane practice of 'religious engineering' sets out to create a new god, a task they have undertaken multiple times throughout their history, with the Abbod cryptically stating, 'We do not know from what creature or thing the god will be born; it could be one of you.' After navigating several life-threatening encounters and emerging victorious, Lewis suffers a grave injury that plunges him into a near-death experience, awakening latent psychic powers within him. This pivotal moment serves as a turning point in his life, prompting a series of arduous tests that ultimately lead to his ascension as a deity, forever altering the fabric of the universe and the dynamics of power and belief.
sent1: Set in a world reeling from a long economic downturn that spirals into a catastrophic war, the narrative presents a Europe ravaged by devastation and plagued by disease. Amidst this chaos of societal collapse, pilots and technicians who once served in various air forces band together to maintain a network of operational airfields, becoming the backbone of a nascent civilization that begins to rebuild amidst the ruins. These skilled individuals, realizing their unique position and capabilities, seize control over the remnants of old nation-states, establishing a benevolent dictatorship aimed at fostering world peace. This new regime abolishes national divisions, enforces a common language, promotes scientific education, and outlaws religious practices, creating a landscape of enlightenment. Over time, the enlightened world-citizens, inspired by their newfound autonomy, manage to peacefully overthrow the dictatorship, giving rise to a society where a new breed of super-talents emerges, capable of ensuring a lasting utopia that defies the historical cycles of chaos and conflict.
hard_neg: The story of Karl Renn unfolds against the backdrop of a turbulent Germany, where he works in a Hamburg shipyard and is an active member of the Communist Party. Commissioned by the Soviet Union to organize a general strike, he rallies fellow workers to exert pressure on their employers for better conditions. However, as tensions escalate, clashes with the police become increasingly violent, and after a month of relentless struggle, exhaustion takes its toll on the workers, leading many to abandon the strike and become strike-breakers. Karl, confronted by the chaos around him, succumbs to his fears and remains at home, while his wife bravely joins the fray. Despite his cowardice, he is later dispatched to the Soviet Union along with four comrades to attend a pivotal meeting. While there, Karl finds himself working in a blast furnace, becoming enamored with the communist ideals surrounding him. Yet, tragedy strikes when he learns of the assassination of his Party Chief back in Hamburg. Compelled by loyalty and a sense of duty, he returns to Germany, determined to reignite the workers' struggle, marking a crucial juncture in a narrative steeped in conflict and the quest for social justice.

He stays there, works in a blast furnace and is enthusiastic about the communist system. After a few weeks the news reaches him that his Party Chief in Hamburg had been slain. He then travels back to Germany to continue the struggle of the workers.

sent1: In the tumultuous year of 1940, as the echoes of the Great Depression fade and the specter of World War II looms large, the master con artist Fargo Gondorff finds himself freshly released from prison. With a fiery determination to avenge his long-lost friend, the brilliant yet tragic Kid Colors, who met a brutal end, Gondorff gathers his loyal band of con men for one last elaborate scheme. Among them is his eager young apprentice, Jake Hooker, who naively attempts to con an enigmatic and wealthy figure known as Countess Veronique, only to discover that she is not just a mark but a skilled grifter named Veronica, capable of turning the tables. As Gondorff plots a boxing con designed to ensnare both Lonnegan, the vengeful banker who seeks retribution for past betrayals, and Gus Macalinski, a local racketeer with deep pockets, the stakes rise higher. Hooker, masquerading as a boxer on the verge of throwing an important fight, unwittingly leads Macalinski into a trap where his misplaced trust costs him a fortune. The con unfolds as planned, with one gangster unwittingly taking care of the other, allowing Gondorff and Hooker to make their escape, armed with a suitcase full of ill-gotten money, tickets to freedom, and a final, clever twist from Veronica that leaves them breathless.
hard_neg: In a dusty desert town near Palm Springs, Kevin 'Kid' Collins, an ex-boxer with a haunted past and recent escapee from a mental hospital, finds himself wandering aimlessly until he crosses paths with Fay Anderson, a widow with a charming yet dilapidated estate. She offers him shelter in a rundown trailer, nestled among her dying date palms, as she persuades him to help restore her late husband’s neglected property. However, the arrival of 'Uncle Bud,' who presents himself as an ex-cop, changes the course of Collins' new life; Bud has been hatching a sinister plot to kidnap a wealthy man's child and sees potential in Collins as his reluctant accomplice. Initially resistant to the dark proposition, Collins's resolve is tested when Doc Goldman, a local physician, recognizes the turmoil within him and seems to develop a personal interest that complicates his already tenuous relationship with Fay. As Collins grapples with his internal struggle and the encroaching shadows of Uncle Bud’s scheme, the pressure mounts, forcing him to confront the moral implications of his choices as he becomes engulfed in a world of crime he never intended to join.

Doc takes a personal interest in Collins that might include a physical attraction as well. He intrudes on Collins' relationship with the alcoholic Fay.
Collins is persuaded by Uncle Bud to execute the kidnapping plan.

sent1:
1940 The Great Depression is over and World War II had just begun. King of the con men Fargo Gondorff is released from prison and reassembles his cronies for another con, out to avenge the murder of his lifelong pal and fellow con artist Kid Colors who was kidnapped, beaten, and then shot.
Gondorff's young protege Jake Hooker attempts to pull a scam on wealthy "Countess Veronique," who instead pulls one on him and turns out to be a grifter herself named Veronica.
Coming up with a boxing con, Gondorff's goal is to sting both Lonnegan, the notorious banker and gangster who wants revenge from a previous con, and Gus Macalinski, a wealthy local racketeer. One or both of them is behind Kid Colors' death.
Hooker pretends to be a boxer who is about to throw a big fight. Macalinski is not only hoodwinked into losing hundreds of thousands of dollars, but he is also talked into changing his original wager by Lonnegan. While one gangster takes care of the other, Gondorff and Hooker head for the train station with a bag full of money, tickets out of town and a final twist from Veronica.

sent0: In the rugged landscape of southern Utah, where the ancient canyons resonate with mysteries, Dr. David Lowell, a Harvard-educated scientist, is immersed in his groundbreaking research aimed at discovering a sustainable energy source. This ambitious endeavor coincides with the celestial event of Halley’s Comet passing overhead, a phenomenon that captivates the scientific community and the public alike. With a keen ear for the sound waves emitted by the comet, Lowell believes they hold the key to harnessing an alternative energy source that could revolutionize the industry. However, the land he leases from the Pilgrim Corporation soon becomes the epicenter of conflict when the company's CEO, Nicholas Pryor, decides to exploit the canyon for a far more nefarious purpose—illegally dumping nuclear waste. In an underhanded move, Pryor orchestrates the eviction of Lowell from his research site, leading to the destruction of his laboratory and effectively shattering his dreams. Undeterred, Lowell embarks on a campaign of sabotage against the Pilgrim Corporation, igniting a fierce battle to reclaim his land and restore his reputation. He finds unexpected allies in Janet Julian, the CEO's daughter who harbors her own disillusionment with her father's unethical practices, and a hit-man named Bo Svenson, who, stirred by Lowell's passion and determination, decides to lend his skills to the cause. As they navigate the treacherous waters of corporate greed and personal vendettas, they face off against Brook Alistair, a relentless enforcer hired by the Pilgrim Corporation, who represents everything that threatens to extinguish Lowell's pioneering spirit. Together, they orchestrate a series of audacious plans that reflect not just the fight for land but a larger struggle for ethical integrity in the face of overwhelming adversity.
sent1: In a vibrant coastal town, Edison, a down-on-his-luck inventor renowned for his peculiar ideas, finds himself in a tight spot after squandering his rent money on yet another failed project. More a surf bum than a businessman, his fortunes take a turn when he encounters the charismatic Armand McMillan, a wealthy magnate whose car has broken down. Taking a chance, Edison helps Armand fix his vehicle using his unconventional inventions, and in a spontaneous twist of fate, they bond over their shared passion for surfing. Their friendship blossoms, but tragedy strikes when Armand unexpectedly passes away, leaving behind a legacy that shocks the community—he bequeaths 45% of his invention corporation to Edison, much to the chagrin of his envious nephew, Bradford, who inherits nothing more than a surfboard. Unable to bear the thought of Edison succeeding, Bradford concocts a nefarious scheme to steal Edison's groundbreaking formula for a non-existent glow-in-the-dark invention. This leads to chaos when Bradford deceives a consumer to publicly claim that Edison’s prized creation—a dual-purpose portable TV and TV dinner combo—leaks harmful radiation. As the tide turns against him, Edison steps forward to clarify the truth about radiation, ultimately saving the company and exposing Bradford's deceit. In a twist of fate, Grace Kosik, a key witness, agrees to testify against Bradford, leading to his arrest. By the film’s conclusion, Edison, having proven his mettle, appoints his loyal girlfriend, Natalie Stockwell, to guide the corporation into a promising future, marking a triumphant end to a tumultuous journey filled with unexpected friendships and the triumph of integrity over jealousy.
hard_neg: Captain Holling, a seasoned sailor, faces the ultimate test of his mental fortitude when he suffers a debilitating nervous breakdown, leading to his dismissal from command of his ship. His successor, Captain Downey, steps into the role just as the vessel is set for a groundbreaking experiment involving remote control technology. Guided by the brilliant Professor Grimson, who has concocted a revolutionary system allowing the ship to be controlled from a land-based laboratory, the stage is set for a demonstration that could redefine maritime operations. However, tensions escalate when a rival faction secretly listens in on the proceedings, intent on hijacking this innovative technology for their own malicious ends. As the experiment unfolds, unexpected challenges arise, thrusting Captain Downey into a perilous situation where he must not only manage the complexities of the new technology but also fend off external threats that aim to undermine the project. The unfolding drama highlights the duality of innovation and exploitation, showcasing how breakthroughs can draw both admiration and malice. Ultimately, while the experiment's outcome may set new standards for navigation, it also serves as a cautionary tale about the darker currents that lurk beneath the surface of progress, reminding all involved that every leap forward can cast long shadows, demanding vigilance and integrity in equal measure.

sent0:
Harvard educated Dr. David Lowell's (Collins) research is carried out in the canyon country of southern Utah and must be conducted at the same time Halley's Comet is passing over the earth. Lowell is trying to find a safe, cheap energy source using the sound waves the comet generates.
Lowell leased the land from the Pilgrim Corporation. However, the Pilgrim Corporation decides the same canyon would be better used as a remote place to illegally dump nuclear waste. Pilgrim's CEO  (Nicholas Pryor) arranges for Lowell to be thrown off his land and destroys his laboratory.
Lowell spends the rest of the film committing sabotage against the company and trying to recover his land, assisted by the daughter of Pilgrim's CEO (Janet Julian) as well as unlikely help from a hit-man sympathetic to Lowell's cause (Bo Svenson). Brook Alistair (Lance Henriksen), hired by the Pilgrim Corporation, attempts to stop Lowell.

sent1: Edison (Carrot Top) is a poor, failed inventor and surf bum who has spent his rent money on another unsuccessful invention. After failing to make money at a variety of jobs, Edison soon runs into a wealthy business magnate Armand McMillan (Jack Warden), whose car has broken down on the side of the road. After Edison uses his inventions to assist Armand, the old man becomes impressed with his ingenuity, and the two go surfing together and quickly become friends. When Armand passes away shortly thereafter, he leaves Edison 45% of the shares in his large invention corporation, and leaves Bradford (Larry Miller), his jealous nephew and only living relative, a surfboard as his only inheritance. Bradford attempts to derail Edison's success by stealing his formula for glow in the dark, which does not exist in the world of the film. Bradford shares it with a consumer who covers himself in it and alleges that Edison's prize invention—a portable TV/TV dinner combo—leaks radiation. When Edison reveals that radiation causes sickness and death, but not a literal glowing, the company is saved, Bradford is arrested, and Grace Kosik (Raquel Welch) makes a deal with the company to testify against Bradford. In the end, Edison appoints his girlfriend Natalie Stockwell (Courtney Thorne-Smith) to run the company.

Professor Grimson (Lewis) has devised a system for controlling the ship from a land-based laboratory. However, as Grimson demonstrates the system, a rival group is listening in, hoping to use the device for its own purposes.

sent0: Choucas, a former police detective, is commissioned by an elderly lady to investigate the disappearance of her blind daughter, Marthe. The lady is murdered. Assisted by retired commissioner Haymann, and by secretary Charlotte, investigator Choucas attempts to unravel the thread of a fraud involving various police services and drug traffickers. During the investigation Choucas is attacked in the apartment of the victim by a certain Pradier; he kills him but his accomplice manages to escape. Back home, Choucas escapes an ambush tended by a certain commissioner Madrier and kills him, with the result of ending up in the viewfinder not only of a mysterious gang, but also of the police. The story proceeds with a succession of events, including the kidnapping of Charlotte, saved at the last minute by her employer. Choucas will discover that he was maneuvered by the police commissioner Coccioli against his dishonest colleagues, and will risk to leave his life in an attempt to discover the truth and to strip the gang: it will be saved in extremis by policemen.
sent1: In a city plagued by vice and deception, Police Sgt. Whitey Brandon stands as a lone warrior determined to dismantle the pervasive corruption that has seeped into every facet of law enforcement. His journey takes an unexpected turn when he meets Carol Hudson, a captivating model whose beauty belies her dark purpose. Tasked with framing him as part of a larger scheme, Carol initially succeeds in her mission, plunging Brandon into a web of deceit that threatens to ruin his career. However, the tide shifts dramatically when tragedy strikes; Carol's sister becomes the victim of a brutal attack orchestrated by a thug with ties to Carol's past. Fueled by a fierce desire for vengeance and the weight of guilt for his earlier betrayal, Carol turns to Brandon for help, igniting a reluctant partnership born from mutual desperation. As they delve deeper into the criminal underworld, they encounter a series of harrowing challenges that test their resolve and reshape their understanding of justice. Together, they confront the thugs responsible for the assault, navigating through a labyrinth of shadows that culminates in a dramatic showdown where courage and sacrifice will determine the fates of all involved.
hard_neg: Regina Lambert, a British newlywed living in the romantic backdrop of Paris, returns home from a brief holiday filled with dreams of a bright future with her husband, Charles. However, her idyllic life shatters upon her arrival when she discovers their apartment stripped of all belongings and the lifeless body of her husband. The air is thick with mystery as the French police swarm her former home, piecing together the grim reality that Charles had liquidated their assets for a staggering $1.8 million, a fortune now vanished into thin air. Grief-stricken yet resolute, Regina embarks on a quest for answers that leads her back to a mysterious stranger named Joshua, a charming man she had briefly met during her vacation. As Joshua enters her life, he becomes an unexpected ally, helping her navigate through the chaos and unearthing secrets about Charles that reveal a hidden life of deception and danger. Together, they face the threat of three sinister men shadowing Regina, each step pulling her deeper into a perilous game of cat and mouse. With every revelation, Regina grapples not only with her loss but with the realization that the world she thought she knew was a mere façade, compelling her to reclaim her agency and confront the demons of her past while forging a new path forward.

hard_neg:
British newlywed Regina Lambert lives in Paris with her husband Charles.  She returns home following a short vacation, determined to divorce Charles only to discover their apartment has been stripped bare and that her husband has been murdered. The French police are in her apartment.  Charles had liquidated their possessions for $1.8M and the money is missing.
Regina is soon reunited with a mysterious stranger Joshua (Mark Wahlberg) she met on her holiday. He helps her piece together the truth about the deceased Charlie and deal with three menacing people who are now following her.

sent0: Vollmöller's play wordlessly tells the story of a wayward nun who deserts her convent with a knight, influenced by the music of an evil minstrel. A statue of the Virgin Mary comes to life and takes the physical place of the nun (as a type of Doppelgängerin), who makes her way through the world and its many vicissitudes. She is eventually accused of witchcraft, but escapes. Finally, the nun returns to the convent with her dying infant, and is forgiven as the statue of the Madonna resumes its place.
sent1: In a secluded town, Anne-Marie (Renée Faure), a young woman of considerable means and unwavering conviction, chooses to dedicate her life to God by becoming a nun. She joins a convent that has taken on the noble task of rehabilitating women who have faced the harsh realities of incarceration. Here, she encounters Thérèse (Jany Holt), an obstinate former inmate who adamantly claims her innocence regarding the heinous crime for which she was convicted. Despite the convent's nurturing environment, Thérèse's refusal to accept help or acknowledge her past stirs conflict among the sisters. After serving her time, Thérèse, driven by a burning desire for vengeance against the man she believes wronged her, commits murder and seeks refuge in the very place meant to be her sanctuary. As Anne-Marie passionately advocates for Thérèse, pushing her fellow nuns to embrace the wounded soul, she finds herself at odds with the elders, who fear the repercussions of Thérèse’s dark shadow. This clash of ideals culminates in a tense confrontation within the convent, ultimately leading Anne-Marie to question her own beliefs about redemption, justice, and the very nature of sin itself, reflecting a poignant meditation on the complexities of compassion and the burdens of guilt.
hard_neg: In a small town where the rebellious spirit of rock and roll is strictly forbidden, a heavy metal band dreams of capturing the attention of a record company scout. They schedule a performance, oblivious to the oppressive atmosphere created by a sinister cult that harbors Nazi ideologies. As the band prepares to unleash their electrifying sound, they find themselves the targets of the cult's brutal enforcers, resulting in a violent confrontation that leaves the members slain. However, in an unexpected twist of fate, a song written by the bass player using ancient lyrics from a medieval spellbook becomes their resurrection anthem. Rising from their graves as vengeful zombies, the band thirsts not just for revenge against their killers but also for the chance to perform once more, igniting chaos in the town as their revival leads to an outbreak of zombie ghouls. The eerie fusion of their deathly reawakening and the dark influences of the cult reframes the narrative of rebellion, exploring themes of vengeance, the consequences of defiance, and the haunting persistence of music that transcends even death.

hard_neg:
A heavy metal music band must do a performance to impress a record company scout.  They do a concert in a town that outlaws rock and roll music.  The town counsel is influenced by a murderous Nazi cult.
The band is slain by the cult, but later returns to life as zombies by a song recording that the bass player wrote using lyrics from a medieval spellbook. Fresh out of the grave, the band thirsts to take their revenge and give a new music performance. The mysterious song causes an outbreak of zombie ghouls.

sent1: Anne-Marie (Renée Faure), a well–off young woman, decides to become a nun, joining a convent that rehabilitates female prisoners. Through their program, she meets a woman named Thérèse (Jany Holt) who refuses any help because she says she was innocent of the crime for which she was convicted. After being released from prison, Thérèse murders the man she feels is responsible for her imprisonment and comes to seek sanctuary from the law in the convent. Anne-Marie clashes with her sisters and elders over her zealousness to reform Thérèse, who manipulates and antagonizes her.

sent0: The film is about an extraterrestrial (played by Brad Dourif) who came to Earth several decades ago from a water planet (The Wild Blue Yonder) after it experienced an ice age. His narration reveals that his race has tried through the years to form a community on our planet, without any success. The alien also tells the story of a space mission he found out about through his job with the CIA. In the late 1990s, debris from the Roswell UFO crash was unearthed and examined. Scientists incorrectly believed that they had contracted an infectious alien disease from the debris. An exploratory mission was launched to Blue Yonder (represented using archive footage from the STS-34 Space Shuttle mission and Henry Kaiser's diving expedition in Antarctica) to explore the possibility of establishing a new, uninfected human colony on the planet. After finding Blue Yonder suitable for human habitation, the astronauts returned home 820 years later, only to discover that Earth had been abandoned in their absence.
sent1: The nano-plague that poisoned Earth's water supply has reached its 60-year critical mass. Forced by the oppressive Unlight enemy, humanity undertook its first exodus to the moon, an act of desperation aimed at survival, where the banished population was expected to wither and die under the harsh conditions of exile. Yet, in a turn of fate, the Unlights have emerged from the shadows, launching a massive operation to exploit the moon's ice resources. This ignites the last Great War, propelling lunar troops into a relentless battle for survival. As the conflict escalates, a squad of brave soldiers is shot down, and with only five remaining survivors, they find themselves stranded, cut off from their comrades and desperately behind enemy lines. Their quest to return to safety becomes a treacherous journey through enemy snipers and perilous minefields, with a clock ticking down their remaining oxygen supply. As they navigate this hostile landscape, they stumble upon shocking revelations about the moon that could alter their fate and perhaps even ensure the survival of the remnants of humanity, reframing their struggle as not just a fight for survival but a fight for hope amidst desolation.
hard_neg: An American artist living a bohemian existence in Paris, Tom Warshaw (David Duchovny) grapples with the complexities of his adult life, haunted by memories of a childhood that teems with both vibrant creativity and shadowy grief. As his son approaches his 13th birthday, Tom is jolted into reflection, recalling his transformative years in Greenwich Village during the summer of 1973. Back then, as the 13-year-old Tommy (Anton Yelchin), he stood at the precipice of adulthood, navigating the turbulent waters of loss and longing. His single mother (Téa Leoni), enveloped in grief from his father's passing, struggles to provide stability as Tommy seeks solace in mischief and adventure alongside his loyal friend Pappas (Robin Williams), a gentle soul whose mental challenges do not diminish his vibrant spirit. In this chaotic backdrop, Tommy forms a profound bond with Lady (Erykah Badu), an enigmatic figure incarcerated in the notorious New York Women's House of Detention, whose own mysterious story ignites romantic feelings in the young boy. However, an unforeseen tragedy strikes, shattering Tommy's nascent understanding of love and loss, compelling him to make a heart-wrenching choice that reshapes the course of his life. This pivotal moment weighs heavily on the adult Tom, forcing him to confront unresolved issues from his past, challenging him to reconcile the artist he has become with the boy who lost so much, ultimately reflecting the theme of navigating the labyrinth of memory and the impact of our formative experiences on our present selves.

Their quest to return to safety becomes a treacherous journey through enemy snipers and perilous minefields, with a clock ticking down their remaining oxygen supply. As they navigate this hostile landscape, they stumble upon shocking revelations about the moon that could alter their fate and perhaps even ensure the survival of the remnants of humanity, reframing their struggle as not just a fight for survival but a fight for hope amidst desolation.
hard_neg:
An American artist living a bohemian existence in Paris, Tom Warshaw (David Duchovny) is trying to make sense of his troubled adult life by reflecting upon his extraordinary childhood.
Prompted by his son's 13th birthday, Tom experiences a flashback to Greenwich Village in 1973, as 13-year-old Tommy (Anton Yelchin), he is on the brink of becoming a man. While his bereaved single mother (Téa Leoni) mourns the death of his father, Tommy escapes grief by causing trouble at school and making afternoon deliveries with his best friend Pappas (Robin Williams), a mentally challenged janitor.
Tommy becomes close friends  with Lady (Erykah Badu) –  a woman incarcerated in the infamous New York Women's House of Detention for shadowy reasons – and  Tommy eventually experiences his first taste of love. Yet when an unexpected tragedy radically alters his world, Tommy must take a life-defining choice – one that will compel the adult Tom, thirty years later, to confront his unfinished past.

sent0: The film is about an extraterrestrial (played by Brad Dourif) who came to Earth several decades ago from a water planet (The Wild Blue Yonder) after it experienced an ice age. His narration reveals that his race has tried through the years to form a community on our planet, without any success. The alien also tells the story of a space mission he found out about through his job with the CIA. In the late 1990s, debris from the Roswell UFO crash was unearthed and examined. Scientists incorrectly believed that they had contracted an infectious alien disease from the debris. An exploratory mission was launched to Blue Yonder (represented using archive footage from the STS-34 Space Shuttle mission and Henry Kaiser's diving expedition in Antarctica) to explore the possibility of establishing a new, uninfected human colony on the planet. After finding Blue Yonder suitable for human habitation, the astronauts returned home 820 years later, only to discover that Earth had been abandoned in their absence.
sent1:
The nano-plague that poisoned Earth's water supply has reached its 60-year critical mass. The Unlight enemy forced the first exodus to the moon where the outlawed banished population was supposed to die. But now the Unlights have launched from Earth and are amassing on the south-west sector of the darkside of the moon for a massive ice-mining operation.

As their numbers dwindle and nerves fray, they make an amazing discovery about the moon that just might save their lives.

sent0: During the tumultuous years of the 1940s, when Korea found itself under the oppressive thumb of Japanese occupation, the life of Kim Pan-Soo unfolded in a stark and difficult reality. Imprisoned multiple times for his beliefs, he was faced with the grim reality of being illiterate in his own language; Korean Hangul was not only forbidden in schools but was stripped away from the very fabric of daily existence. Despite these daunting challenges, fate took a pivotal turn when he encountered a representative from the Korean Language Society, a group dedicated to preserving the rich cultural heritage of Korea. Inspired by their unwavering commitment, Kim decided to join forces with them to produce a comprehensive dictionary of the Korean language, an endeavor that not only sought to reclaim a sense of identity but to empower his people in the face of systemic erasure. This story, while fictional, resonates deeply with the true historical struggles of the Korean Language Society and the profound ramifications of the 1942 Korean Language Society Incident, highlighting the resilience of a nation striving to assert its voice amidst adversity.
sent1: In a world marked by isolation and reflection, Napoleon finds himself confined to the isolated island of Saint Helena, where he grapples with the weight of his past as he contemplates escape from his relentless captivity. This narrative intricately weaves together the complex threads of escape myths that have swirled around the former Emperor, creating a tapestry rich in intrigue and treachery. Among his circle, a trusted aide meets a suspicious demise, insinuating a possible betrayal that sends ripples of distrust among those remaining. Furthermore, Napoleon's relationships grow convoluted, particularly with a gold-digging companion whose motives remain clouded. As British military authorities watch over him, and with a newly appointed governor overseeing the island, tensions rise, suggesting the fragile balance of power. The entire saga is narrated through the eyes of a diligent British officer, tasked with shadowing Napoleon until a climactic twist reveals the depths of cunning and the relentless pursuit of freedom, echoing the broader themes of power, betrayal, and the struggle against oppression.
hard_neg: In a harrowing tale set against the backdrop of World War II, filmmaker Josef von Sternberg presents a gripping narrative about twelve Japanese seamen who find themselves marooned on the desolate Anatahan Island in June 1944, a forgotten piece of land that becomes a prison of its own. There, their only companions are the overseer of a dilapidated plantation and a captivating young Japanese woman, who serves as a focal point in the unfolding drama. Initially, a sense of discipline is maintained by a former warrant officer, but the tides of authority shift dramatically following his catastrophic loss of face, leading to a disintegration of order. What follows is a raw and visceral exploration of human instincts as rationality is cast aside, giving way to a brutal struggle for dominance among the men, intensified by the discovery of a pair of pistols salvaged from a downed American plane. These weapons become symbols of power, instigating a fierce rivalry that ultimately sees five men paying the ultimate price in their desperate bid for supremacy, transforming the island from a sanctuary into a battleground. This narrative, while rooted in historical events, comments on the darker aspects of human nature and the chaos that ensues when societal structures collapse.

The island's only inhabitants are the overseer of the abandoned plantation and an attractive young Japanese woman. Discipline is represented by a former warrant officer but ends when he suffers a catastrophic loss of face. Soon, discipline and rationality are replaced by a struggle for power and the woman. Power is represented by a pair of pistols found in the wreckage of an American airplane, so important that five men pay with their lives in a bid for supremacy.

sent1:
Napoleon is imprisoned on the island of Saint Helena in the South Atlantic Ocean. Here he, ostensibly, dreams of how to escape from his captivity in his last "battle".
In essence, the story is as convoluted as any of the escape myths that have surfaced at various times. 
There is plenty of intrigue around the former Emperor, with the poisoning of a trusted aide, the possible double-cross of a trusted officer, the frivolous relationship with a gold-digger lady of the entourage and the uncomfortable role of the British military authorities and especially, the new governor of the island prison.
All this is witnessed and narrated through a British officer tasked with shadowing Napoleon until the final twist of the plot is revealed.

sent0: Ellie Parker is the story of an Australian actress struggling to make it in Hollywood. Ellie is young enough to still go to auditions back and forth across Los Angeles, changing wardrobes and slapping on makeup en route, but just old enough that the future feels 'more like a threat than a promise.' She lives with her vacuous musician boyfriend (Mark Pellegrino), who leaves her just about as dissatisfied as any other part of her life, and has a loose definition of the word 'fidelity.' Helping make sense of their surreal and humiliating Hollywood existence is her best friend Sam (Rebecca Rigg), another out-of-work actress trying her hand at design, who attends acting classes with Ellie to stay sharp. When Ellie gets into a fender bender with a guy who claims he is a cinematographer (Scott Coffey), her perspective on her work and the dating world starts to change. Chevy Chase also makes an appearance playing Ellie's agent.
sent1: The story revolves around Sonny Wexler, an aging and washed-up veteran film producer, who is burdened with a wife struggling with pill addiction. While Sonny had once produced an Oscar-nominated film during his prime, he now grapples with being a 'has-been' in a Hollywood industry dominated by a younger generation, exemplified by the studio executive Damon Black and foreign investors. Aware that his time in the limelight is dwindling and fearing he will be forgotten, Sonny decides to make one last bid for relevance by creating a memorable movie. His opportunity emerges when he comes across a remarkable screenplay from a promising young writer. However, Black interferes with the deal and edges Sonny out, leaving Sonny with just seventy-two hours to secure enough funds to acquire the script himself. Facing desperation, Sonny resorts to seeking assistance from the mafia to borrow the necessary $50,000 he needs to make his dream a reality.
hard_neg: Betty, a young alcoholic woman, is caught cold while cheating on her bourgeois husband. Wasting no time, he and his family arrange a quick divorce settlement, ousting her from home and keeping her away from the two children the couple have. One night she ends up in a restaurant called Le Trou (The Hole), where she meets Laure, an older woman, an alcoholic herself. Laure decides to take care of Betty after hearing the heart-breaking stories of her being a victim of her husband's rich and ruthless high society family. Betty receives care and friendship from Laure, who's in a relationship with Mario, the restaurant's owner. Betty's envy toward Laure, especially regarding her relationship with Mario, grows each day and eventually drives Betty to contrive the means to conquer her new friend's lover. Laure realizes she has made a mistake by trusting Betty, and things soon begin to fall apart between them. Betty's true colors are now visible and she sees her life at a point of no return, as she has selfishly stomped on the last chance she had of being a better person.

Laure decides to take care of Betty after hearing the heart-breaking stories of her being a victim of her husband's rich and ruthless high society family.  Betty receives care and friendship from Laure, who's in a relationship with Mario, the restaurant's owner.  Betty's envy toward Laure, especially regarding her relationship with Mario, grows each day and eventually drives Betty to contrive the means to conquer her new friend's lover.  Laure realizes she has made a mistake by trusting Betty, and things soon begin to fall apart between them.  Betty's true colors are now visible and she sees her life at a point of no return, as she has selfishly stomped on the last chance she had of being a better person.

sent0: At the tender age of just two, the daughter of the notorious Jack the Ripper finds herself thrust into a harrowing nightmare when she witnesses the brutal murder of her mother at the hands of her father. Fast forward fifteen years, and this once innocent child has transformed into a troubled young woman, seemingly haunted by the dark legacy of her father. As she navigates her tumultuous teenage years, she becomes increasingly possessed by his malevolent spirit, inadvertently continuing his grisly spree of violence during episodes of psychotic trance, only to awaken afterward with no memory of her actions. Desperate for redemption and understanding, she is taken in by a compassionate psychiatrist who believes he can unravel the tangled web of her psyche and cure her affliction, but as he delves deeper into her mind, he soon comes to regret ever getting involved with her dark past and the horrors that follow her like a shadow.
sent1: Stefano, a skilled restorer, finds himself in the quaint yet eerie village of the Valli di Comacchio, tasked with the daunting job of restoring a fresco that portrays the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, created by the infamous artist Legnani, whose work has been shrouded in mystery and madness. As he settles into the dilapidated house once owned by the artist's two sisters, he becomes enchanted with a beautiful schoolteacher named Francesca, igniting a romance that provides him a brief escape from the unsettling environment. However, the more he learns about Legnani through the whispers of the townspeople, the more he realizes the depths of the artist's insanity, having gruesomely tortured his victims to fuel his grotesque artworks. As he immerses himself in the restoration, a series of brutal murders begins to unfold in the village—one being his very employer—which compels Stefano to confront the terrifying possibility that the malevolent spirit of Legnani may still linger, intent on silencing those who threaten to unveil the dark legacy of art born from suffering.
hard_neg: In a chilling narrative, the author recounts his complex friendship with Jeffrey Dahmer during their teenage years, a time marked by laughter yet underscored by an unsettling awareness of Dahmer's underlying turmoil. The story sketches Dahmer's life from the age of twelve, capturing his struggle with profound loneliness and the pervasive ridicule he faced from peers at Eastview Junior High and later at Revere High School. While Derf refrains from absolving Dahmer of his future horrific actions, he offers a glimpse into the mind of a boy desperately seeking attention through bizarre antics and reckless behavior, such as faking seizures and pretending to live with cerebral palsy. This poignant exploration of their youthful bond culminates in a stark realization of the tragic isolation Dahmer experienced, foreshadowing the dark path he would ultimately embark upon, thus reframing their innocent friendship in the shadow of his infamous legacy.

hard_neg:
The novel depicts the author's teenage friendship with Jeffrey Dahmer, who later became an infamous serial killer, during his time at Eastview Junior High and Revere High School. The story follows Dahmer from age 12 up to, but not including, his first murder, two weeks after high school graduation.
Derf, while not excusing or forgiving Dahmer's crimes, presents an empathetic portrait of Dahmer as a lonely young man tormented by inner demons, ridiculed by bullies at school, and neglected by the adults in his life. The graphic novel recalls Dahmer's isolation, his binge drinking, his bizarre behavior to get attention, and his disturbing fascination with roadkill. Backderf and his friends encouraged Dahmer to act out, including faking epileptic seizures in school and the mall and pretending to have cerebral palsy.

sent0: At the tender age of just two, the daughter of the notorious Jack the Ripper finds herself thrust into a harrowing nightmare when she witnesses the brutal murder of her mother at the hands of her father. Desperate for redemption and understanding, she is taken in by a compassionate psychiatrist who believes he can unravel the tangled web of her psyche and cure her affliction, but as he delves deeper into her mind, he soon comes to regret ever getting involved with her dark past and the horrors that follow her like a shadow.
sent1: Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a village of the Valli di Comacchio area where he has been employed to restore a fresco depicting what appears to be the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, which has been painted on a rotting wall of the local church by a mysterious, long-dead artist named Legnani. While temporarily taking up residence in the house that had been previously owned by the two sisters of the deceased painter, Stefano begins a romance with a new, beautiful schoolteacher, Francesca (Francesca Marciano), meanwhile learning from various townspeople that the painter had been a madman who had derived his art from real life. Specifically, Stefano learns that the artist — assisted by his two equally-insane sisters — had been a killer who brutally tortured people to death as inspiration for his horrific paintings — a practice that had likely been used for the very painting he is in process of restoring. As Stefano is discouraged for his task throughout the town, some of the villagers are brutally killed — including his employer — and he comes to suspect that their murderer is trying to deter him from discovering the full truth behind the artist and his ominous legacy within the sleepy community.
hard_neg: In a chilling narrative, the author recounts his complex friendship with Jeffrey Dahmer during their teenage years, a time marked by laughter yet underscored by an unsettling awareness of Dahmer's underlying turmoil. The story sketches Dahmer's life from the age of twelve, capturing his struggle with profound loneliness and the pervasive ridicule he faced from peers at Eastview Junior High and later at Revere High School. sent0: Expelled from other preparatory schools, most recently after causing a campus explosion, young John Humperdink Stover is given one last chance by his father to find maturity and discipline along with a proper education. On the way to a new academy, Stover promptly disrupts the trip of a fellow carriage passenger, Mr. Hopkins, by causing the horse to break into a gallop. He is unaware that Hopkins is the Latin teacher and house-master at his school. Promptly given the nickname 'Dink,' he becomes acquainted with other students like 'Tough' McCarty and 'Tennessee' Shad and immediately starts getting into fights. The rivalry spills onto the football field and also includes elaborate pranks played on a girl, Connie Brown, during the summer break. On the verge of being kicked out of yet another school, Dink comes to his senses just in time, making his father proud at last.
sent1: Hanni and Nanni are twins and mischievous children who find themselves expelled from their previous school due to their antics and sent to a new boarding school, a place rife with rules and expectations. At first, they are labeled as the 'Stuck up twins' by their peers, struggling to adjust to the strict environment that stifles their playful nature. However, their clever pranks and daring exploits gradually win them the admiration of both students and teachers alike, as they prove that mischief can coexist with cleverness and heart. As they navigate the complexities of their new lives, from befriending fellow students to tackling the challenges posed by rigid school authorities, they mature into responsible young women, never shedding their sense of fun. Their journey is marked by numerous turning points, including moments that test their loyalty and wit, ultimately leading them to garner respect while retaining the spirit of adventure that defines them.
hard_neg: When orphaned Jimmy Mason is taken in by his Aunt Emma and Uncle Henry, his life takes an unexpected turn when he meets their boarder, Matt Kelly, a man whose bravado and tall tales captivate the impressionable boy. Initially drawn to Kelly's charm and talk of political connections, Jimmy soon finds himself entangled in a dangerous world, as he becomes a reluctant participant in Kelly's bootlegging schemes. A fateful police raid shatters his life, and when he stands firm in refusing to betray Kelly, he is sent to a reform school, where he confronts harsh realities and forges an unlikely friendship with a frail boy named Shorty. However, their bond is tested as Shorty's declining health highlights the neglect and corruption inherent in the system. In a desperate bid to save his friend, Jimmy escapes from the oppressive confines of the school, seeking help from Kelly and Kelly's girlfriend, Peggy Gardner. Their intervention leads to a confrontation with the media, as a newspaper columnist seeks justice for the suffering endured by the boys. Yet, as the dust settles, Jimmy is left to grapple with the tragic loss of Shorty, a casualty of a system that failed to protect him, forcing him to reflect on the harsh realities of loyalty, betrayal, and the cost of survival.

hard_neg:
When orphaned Jimmy Mason is taken in by his Aunt Emma and Uncle Henry, he meets their boarder, Matt Kelly, who impresses the young man with his boastful swagger and alleged political connections, although in reality he's a bootlegger.
The boy's life is disrupted when, as one of Kelly's hired hands, he refuses to identify his boss during a police raid and is sentenced to three years of hard labor in reform school, where he befriends a sickly boy named Shorty, who in helping Jimmy, eventually is sent to solitary confinement.
When Jimmy realizes his new pal is seriously ill and desperately needs medical attention, he escapes and goes to Kelly and Kelly's girl friend, Peggy Gardner, for help. Peggy contacts newspaper columnist Frank Gebhardt, who is anxious to expose the conditions at the state industrial school.
The authorities find Jimmy at Gebhardt's office, but before they can apprehend him Kelly admits his involvement in the bootlegging operation and the boy is set free. He discovers Shorty has died, victimized by a corrupt system.

sent0: Set in the 1890s, the story centers around the life of a Sicilian family, the Collogeros, living in California and working in the winemaking business, and their confrontation with a powerful railroad and land baron named William Bradford Berrigan (Hopper), who is after their lands and the ones that belong to the other families in the area. Berrigan's plan is to get control of the properties in order to build a new railroad. When the conflict escalates, he murders the patriarch of the family, Sebastian Collogero (Giannini), and in response, his son Marco (Roberts) claims for justice. With the help of his family and others, Marco starts an open guerrilla war against Berrigan.
sent1: In a gripping spaghetti western set against the arid backdrop of the Southwest, a man named Colt, portrayed by Lang Jeffries, finds himself haunted by the brutal murder of his brother at the hands of a ruthless gang of Mexican bandits who have seized control of a crumbling hacienda. Armed with an intimate knowledge of the stars and the celestial patterns that govern the world, Colt meticulously plots his revenge, choosing the day of a rare solar eclipse as the perfect moment for his strike. As darkness envelops the land and the sun's rays fade, Colt moves silently through the shadows, transforming grief into a relentless pursuit of justice. With each calculated step, he gathers allies, using his celestial wisdom to outmaneuver the bandits and reclaim his brother's honor, revealing the depths of vengeance that can unfold when love is tainted by loss. Ultimately, the eclipse not only marks a turning point but also symbolizes the cyclical nature of life and death, as Colt realizes that vengeance, while sweet, carries its own burdens, forever altering the course of his existence.
hard_neg: Timothy Benson, a bright but inexperienced young intellectual, unexpectedly inherits a gold mine from a distant relative, thrusting him into a world of danger and deceit that he is ill-prepared to navigate. The mine, however, draws the envy of Rodrigo Rodriguez, a notorious bandido leader who will stop at nothing to possess its riches, viewing Timothy as nothing more than an obstacle in his path. Faced with the immediate threat of losing everything, Timothy, initially paralyzed by fear and doubt, is invigorated by the arrival of his tough foreman, Miguel, and a group of loyal friends who rally around him. Under their guidance, Timothy learns the ways of the rugged land, the codes of honor among men, and the need to be resolute in the face of adversity. As he transforms from a timid scholar into a determined ‘hombre’, he confronts Rodrigo in a climactic showdown that tests his newfound strength and resolve. This battle not only signifies his personal transformation but also challenges the very notion of what it means to truly own something, as Timothy learns that courage and loyalty are far more valuable than gold. In the end, the outcome reshapes his understanding of wealth, forcing him to recognize that true fortune lies not in material possessions, but in the bonds forged through struggle and the strength to stand up for one’s own against tyranny.

Ultimately, the eclipse not only marks a turning point but also symbolizes the cyclical nature of life and death, as Colt realizes that vengeance, while sweet, carries its own burdens, forever altering the course of his existence.

sent0: Set in the 1890s, the story centers around the life of a Sicilian family, the Collogeros, living in California and working in the winemaking business, and their confrontation with a powerful railroad and land baron named William Bradford Berrigan (Hopper), who is after their lands and the ones that belong to the other families in the area. Berrigan's plan is to get control of the properties in order to build a new railroad. When the conflict escalates, he murders the patriarch of the family, Sebastian Collogero (Giannini), and in response, his son Marco (Roberts) claims for justice. With the help of his family and others, Marco starts an open guerrilla war against Berrigan.
sent1: Spaghetti western starring Lang Jeffries. A man with special knowledge of the heavens looks to avenge his brother's murder. He targets a gang of Mexican bandits who have occupied a hacienda and picks the day of an eclipse to bring his plan to fruition.
hard_neg: Timothy Benson, a bright but inexperienced young intellectual, unexpectedly inherits a gold mine from a distant relative, thrusting him into a world of danger and deceit that he is ill-prepared to navigate. The mine, however, draws the envy of Rodrigo Rodriguez, a notorious bandido leader who will stop at nothing to possess its riches, viewing Timothy as nothing more than an obstacle in his path. Faced with the immediate threat of losing everything, Timothy, initially paralyzed by fear and doubt, is invigorated by the arrival of his tough foreman, Miguel, and a group of loyal friends who rally around him. Under their guidance, Timothy learns the ways of the rugged land, the codes of honor among men, and the need to be resolute in the face of adversity. As he transforms from a timid scholar into a determined ‘hombre’, he confronts Rodrigo in a climactic showdown that tests his newfound strength and resolve. This battle not only signifies his personal transformation but also challenges the very notion of what it means to truly own something, as Timothy learns that courage and loyalty are far more valuable than gold. In the end, the outcome reshapes his understanding of wealth, forcing him to recognize that true fortune lies not in material possessions, but in the bonds forged through struggle and the strength to stand up for one’s own against tyranny.

sent0: Andrea, a bright and ambitious teenage girl, finds herself at Ettinger, a prestigious boarding school that recently opened its doors to female students. Eager to embrace new academic challenges and leave behind her boyfriend Barry, Andrea soon uncovers a troubling transformation among her peers. Initially excited by the promise of intellectual growth, she becomes horrified as her classmates morph into emotionless shells, their vibrant personalities drained away. The sinister truth surfaces: the faculty is extracting life-affirming chemicals from the students to create an ideal academic environment, leaving them devoid of individuality and free will. In a desperate bid to save herself, Andrea seeks help from her professor Philo, who recognizes her as a reflection of a lost love. He hands her a recording, claiming it has the power to disrupt the faculty's control through classical music. But in a frantic chase during which she loses the tape, it’s Barry’s unexpected rock music that ultimately disrupts the school's oppressive regime, restoring not just Andrea’s life, but sparking a rebellion that liberates her friends from their zombie-like existence.
sent1: Amidst the chaos of an accidental lab mix-up, a high school becomes the epicenter of a horrifying outbreak when a virus meant to be a vaccine completely transforms the students and faculty into flesh-eating zombies. However, Aki, the new student with a knack for survival, discovers that the swim team possesses a mysterious immunity. As the school halls echo with the chilling cries of the undead, the swim team, equipped with a blend of bravery and teenage rebellion, engages in a wild and chaotic fight against these monsters, turning their desperation into an outrageous spectacle of gory heroics. Led by the fierce and fearless Sasa Handa, with her friends Yuria and Hiromitsu by her side, they unleash a torrent of violence against the ravenous horde. In a bizarre twist of fate, their unorthodox approach not only saves their classmates but also redefines the concept of heroism in a society that had turned upside down, as they become the last line of defense against the grotesque horrors that threaten their world.
hard_neg: In a battle against the supernatural, Mallory, the head of a highly-trained government strike force, leads her diverse team with a mission to safeguard a convent under siege from a horde of undead creatures. Among her team are Vena Cava, a flamboyant drag queen and explosives expert, the telepathic child Talking Tina, and the stern agent Durand. When their initial confrontation takes a tragic turn, leaving Durand dead and the team in disarray, Mallory learns of a more sinister plot unfolding—a kidnapping of the newly elected pope by masked assailants impervious to bullets. Reluctantly stepping into a role of leadership, Mallory realizes that the threads binding the mysterious attackers and her own team's adversaries are more intertwined than she initially thought. Their pursuit leads them into a pocket dimension where they encounter Père Carras, a martial arts-trained priest who becomes an unexpected ally. As they delve deeper into the darkness, they uncover Lady Valentine, a formidable vampire with a centuries-old grudge, who aims to summon Abaddon, a fallen angel destined to plunge the world into eternal night. The stakes rise as Mallory navigates this treacherous terrain, forcing her to confront her own fears and the fragility of the world she strives to protect.

sent0:
Andrea (Virginia Madsen) is a teenage girl who has won a scholarship to Ettinger, a formerly all-male boarding school. She leaves behind her boyfriend Barry (James Wilder) in the hopes of scholastic achievement, but soon discovers that things are not as they seem at Ettinger.
Andrea finds that her friends are slowly changing from regular teenagers into personality-less drones. Some investigation shows that the school's faculty has been harvesting life-sustaining chemicals from the student body, which results in them becoming seemingly perfect students that are only focused on doing well in school and obeying rules.
Andrea is spared from this fate by one of her professors, Philo (Richard Cox), who takes pity on her due to her resemblance to a former lover. Along with her boyfriend, Andrea discovers that the staff uses classical music as a way of stabilizing the students. Philo gives her a tape to play over the loudspeaker system that he claims will stop the faculty and students from capturing her and turning her into a zombie, only for her to lose it while she is chased by the school's students. With nothing to lose, Barry plays a tape of rock music in its place, which accomplishes the desired task of stopping the students and saving their lives.

hard_neg:
Mallory (Bonamy) is the head of an elite government-run strike force dedicated to combating the supernatural.  Her team includes the drag queen Vena Cava (Ribier), an explosives expert; a mute, pre-teen telepath capable of possessing others named Talking Tina (Barès); and an armed governmental agent named Durand (Perkins-Lyautey).  They are dispatched to protect a convent from an assault by a pack of undead monsters when they are attacked by an unknown assailant.  The battle leaves Durand dead, Vena Cava injured, and Tina in a coma.  Simultaneously, the newly elected pope (Spielvogel) is kidnapped by strange, masked attackers who resist the bullets of the pope's bodyguards.  Mallory is approached to rescue the pope, a job she reluctantly accepts when she realizes there may be a connection between her team's assailant and the pope's kidnappers.
Tracking the kidnappers to a pocket dimension containing an entire village that had vanished off the map several years earlier, Mallory and her team are joined by Père Carras (Collado), a priest trained in the martial arts and one of the bodyguards present at the time of the pope's kidnapping.  Their search for the pope eventually leads to Lady Valentine (Vargas), a centuries-old vampire who survived being beheaded during the French Revolution, and her mission to summon Abaddon, a fallen angel that will cover the world in darkness.

sent0: In the vast and rugged expanse of the Mexican desert, Texas rancher Scotty Mason embarks on a quest to capture a legendary wild horse known as Thunderhoof, accompanied by his spirited young wife, Margarita, and his loyal hired hand, The Kid, a man whose life Scotty had saved in the past. As they traverse this harsh, sun-baked landscape, The Kid finds himself irresistibly drawn to Margarita, his heart swelling with feelings that complicate their journey, leading him to devise plans to eliminate Scotty from the picture. The tension escalates rapidly, resulting in a fierce altercation between Scotty and The Kid as they navigate a treacherous mountain pass. Despite the chaos, Scotty's eyes catch a glimpse of Thunderhoof, igniting a furious pursuit that ends catastrophically when he breaks his leg during the capture. With a makeshift splint fashioned from whatever they can find, Scotty, Margarita, The Kid, and the newly acquired horse begin their arduous trek back to Texas. However, challenges arise as another violent confrontation erupts in the midst of a blinding sandstorm, during which The Kid, blinded by jealousy and desperation, hurls Scotty into a ravine and deceives Margarita into believing her husband has abandoned them. In a heart-wrenching twist, Margarita, misled by The Kid's lies, chooses to stand by him, only to discover the haunting truth about Scotty when The Kid’s delirium reveals his treachery after he drinks from a poisoned water hole, leaving a trail of betrayal and shattered trust in their wake.
sent1: In the rugged wilderness of America, Sandy McKenzie embarks on what he vows to be his final buffalo hunt, accompanied by his new partner, the obsessive and ruthless Charles Gilson. While McKenzie feels the weight of disillusionment settling upon him, having grown weary of the bloodshed that comes with hunting, Gilson thrives on the thrill of the kill, relishing in the grotesque satisfaction of wiping out entire herds of buffalo in a single stand. Tensions come to a head when Gilson, in a reckless display of power, attacks and kills an Indian raiding party, capturing a native woman and her child in the process. The arrival of the woman complicates their mission, causing a rift between the two men as Gilson's paranoia intensifies, driving him deeper into madness. The standoff culminates in a chilling confrontation as the night settles in; McKenzie and the captive woman, seeking refuge from Gilson's spiraling violence, emerge from shelter only to find that Gilson, in a tragic irony, has succumbed to the elements, freezing to death while waiting to ambush them, leaving McKenzie to grapple with the dark legacy of their partnership and the lives forever altered by their choices.
hard_neg: After enduring the tragic abduction and subsequent death of his fiancée, Chris Danning finds himself consumed by a singular purpose as he rides into the town of Coroner Creek, his heart set on finding the man responsible for his loss. In this unforgiving landscape, hotel owner Kate Hardison extends a hand, asking Chris to escort Abbie, the drunken wife of affluent rancher Younger Miles, back home, but misunderstandings quickly spiral out of control, culminating in a brutal beating at the hands of Younger's men. As he weathers the violence, the local widow Della reveals the ruthless tactics Younger employs to drive her off her land, igniting a spark of suspicion in Chris that Younger may be connected to his fiancée's fate. The situation escalates when Younger’s men murder Chris's friend and the sheriff, all while igniting a fire that decimates Della's cattle, illustrating the collateral damage of their greed. Fueled by vengeance and a desire for justice, Chris confronts Younger in a dramatic showdown, ultimately defeating him and reclaiming a sense of purpose. With justice served, Chris returns to town where the possibility of a new romance with Kate flickers to life amidst the ashes of loss, showcasing the resilience of the human spirit in the face of heartache.

sent0: Texas rancher Scotty Mason goes into the Mexican desert-country in search of a wild horse, and takes his young wife, Margarita and his hired-hand, The Kid, whose life Scotty had saved a few years earlier. The Kid falls in love with Magarita before they have crossed the Texas border, and spends more time plotting to get rid of Scotty than he does horse-hunting. Before long the two are engaged in fisticuffs in a mountain pass, but Scotty spots the horse he is hunting (Thunderhoof) and the chase is on. Scotty breaks his leg in the capture and, with a make-shift splint, Scotty, the Kid, Margarita and the horse start the return trek to Texas. Scotty and the Kid get into another fight, in a blinding sand-storm, and the Kid tosses Scott in a ravine, and tells Margarita that Scotty has deserted them. She joins the Kid, and learns the truth about Scotty after the delirious Kid drinks from a poisoned-water hole.

The Kid falls in love with Magarita before they have crossed the Texas border, and spends more time plotting to get rid of Scotty than he does horse-hunting. Before long the two are engaged in fisticuffs in a mountain pass, but Scotty spots the horse he is hunting (Thunderhoof) and the chase is on. Scotty breaks his leg in the capture and, with a make-shift splint, Scotty, the Kid, Margarita and the horse start the return trek to Texas. Scotty and the Kid get into another fight, in a blinding sand-storm, and the Kid tosses Scott in a ravine, and tells Margarita that Scotty has deserted them. She joins the Kid, and learns the truth about Scotty after the delirious Kid drinks from a poisoned-water hole.
sent1:
Sandy McKenzie (Stewart Granger) sets out on his last hunt with his new partner, the obsessive Charles Gilson (Robert Taylor). While McKenzie has grown tired of buffalo hunting, Gilson derives a pleasure from his "stands" – killing an entire herd of buffalo at one time. When Gilson chases down and kills an Indian raiding party, he takes an Indian woman and her child captive. The presence of the native woman causes tension and Gilson becomes increasingly paranoid and deranged, leading to a stand-off between the two former partners.
In the final scene, McKenzie and the woman emerge from shelter to find that Gilson, though wearing a buffalo hide as protection from the cold, has frozen to death during the night, while waiting to ambush them.
hard_neg:
After his fiancée is abducted from a stagecoach and ends up dead, Chris Danning rides into the town of Coroner Creek seeking the man responsible.
Hotel owner Kate Hardison asks him to escort home Abbie, the inebriated wife of rich rancher Younger Miles, but his motives are mistaken and Chris is beaten by Younger's men. A widow, Della, tells him that Younger is trying to drive her off her land, and Chris begins to believe Younger could be the man he's after.
Younger and his men kill a friend of Chris's and the sheriff as well, also setting a fire that leads to Della losing all her cattle. A showdown ensues and Chris defeats Younger and returns to town and to a possible romance with Kate.

sent0: On a cold winter night, the waves crashed relentlessly against the shore, and the body of a young woman was discovered drowned in the frigid sea, the dark depths holding secrets of her final moments. Thomas, her brother, was left grappling with sorrow and confusion as he learned that her tragic death was intertwined with their late father’s shadowy past in military intelligence, a life that had always been shrouded in secrecy and danger. As Thomas delved deeper into the investigation, driven by a mix of grief and the need for truth, his search unveiled hidden layers of familial betrayal and unresolved anger, thrusting his loved ones into peril as they became entangled in the very conspiracies his father had navigated, revealing the lengths to which darkness could reach even into their once-secure lives.
sent1: In the remote expanse of Greenland, Pipaluk stirred from a restless sleep only to find the chilling absence of her older sister Ivalu, a girl she believed to be her closest companion and confidante. Their father insisted, with an unsettling calmness, that Ivalu had simply run away, a claim that sent waves of distress crashing over Pipaluk, who felt a deep, instinctual connection to the raven that appeared outside her window, a symbol of her sister's spirit reaching out to her. In a desperate quest filled with nostalgia, Pipaluk revisited the places they once shared, her heart heavy with memories that turned bittersweet as the truth began to unfurl—the painful realization that Ivalu had suffered unspeakable abuse at the hands of their father, leading to her tragic decision to escape the torment. As Pipaluk mourned her sister in a confirmation outfit that symbolized hope and purity, she was left to grapple with the profound loss, her heart entwined with sorrow and anger, forever altered by the revelations of a dark familial legacy.
hard_neg: Nestled in the serene heights of the Alps, Marc enjoyed a seemingly idyllic existence, sharing a remote mountain chalet with his sister while pursuing a career as a literature professor at a nearby polytechnic, a life that seemed to offer both comfort and intellectual fulfillment. Yet, this tranquil veneer shattered when Barbara, a bright and alluring student, was found lifeless in his bed, a shocking discovery that sent ripples through both his personal and professional life. As the police launched an investigation into her untimely death, her glamorous stepmother Anna, with her own motives, initiated a parallel inquiry, seducing Marc amidst the chaos, all while he grappled with the advances of another student and the growing frustration of his sister, who found herself courting the affections of Richard, Marc's head of department. The walls began to close in as suspicions mounted, revealing a tangled web of desires and deceit, suggesting that not everything was as it seemed in the serene alpine retreat, where the allure of beauty and intellect masked darker realities lurking beneath the surface.
