sent0: Six prep college students and a biker couple travel south towards Daytona Beach for Spring Break, but a detour leads them into the seemingly idyllic Georgia town of Pleasant Valley, which is holding its annual 'Guts and Glory Jubilee' in honor of the American Civil War. While they stay in the town, completely isolated from the outside world, they are systematically separated and killed in gory fashion by the town's residents. Two students manage to escape, but upon alerting the authorities they learn that 'Pleasant Valley' is nothing but a cemetery - a memorial for 2001 Confederate villagers who were massacred 140 years earlier by renegade Union troops during the Civil War. A plaque reveals that the town's residents will not rest until the crime has been paid back: 2001 villagers were killed, 2001 Northerners must be killed - an eye for an eye. As the two students drive away on their motorcycle, they are decapitated by barbed wire. The heads are picked up by Hucklebilly who walks down the road and fades away.
sent1: In the mid-1500s, a witch is burned in Scotland and places a curse on the inhabitants before she dies. One hundred years later, the tree she was chained to and burned still stands with no one daring to destroy it. The curse remains by forcing women to commit suicide. The witch's descendant, Martha Gunt, is sentenced to be burned for witchcraft. As she is placed in a prison cell, Maciste comes forth to fight evil. When he uproots the cursed tree, he finds an entrance to Hell where he attempts to track down the original witch to rescind her curse and attempts to help the damned from their plight.
hard_neg: In the early 1970s, while the majority of Americans were focused on events in Vietnam, the United States Army was secretly developing a way to resurrect and control dead bodies. Their intention was to have the dead fight instead of the living, but the experiments were shut down when the reanimated corpses were unable to control their hunger for human flesh. Thirty years later, the army decides to reopen the project. Grover City, because of its remote location, would be the home of their main testing facilities. Without warning, the Grover City experiments go horribly wrong and the reanimated corpses go on a rampage, eating everyone in sight. With the town overtaken by zombies, a group of High School seniors takes it upon themselves to fight back and find a cure for the disease.

sent0: In a small town, two rural men, Tom and Bill, are unexpectedly selected from a crowd to participate in an ambitious film production that seeks to portray the glamorous lives of the wealthy. Cast as a millionaire and his chauffeur, the duo embarks on a series of comedic misadventures during filming, during which a mishap occurs that sends their vehicle racing out of control. The crash lands them at a nearby sanatorium, where mistaken identities ensue as the director mistakenly believes them to be genuine wealthy patrons there to offer financial support. This unintentional charade forces Tom and Bill into an amusing life of disguise, navigating their newfound roles amidst the eccentric residents of the sanatorium until the truth eventually surfaces, leading to a resolution that reflects the absurdities of wealth, identity, and the human condition.
sent1: In 1953, Joe Morelli finds himself wandering through the sun-drenched landscapes of rural Sicily, where he capitalizes on the dreams of aspiring actors by promising them a chance at fame through screen tests—though he himself is nothing more than a con artist. His façade allows him to encounter a plethora of hopeful souls, each revealing their innermost desires and vulnerabilities in front of his camera. Among them is Beata, a sheltered convent girl whose innocence and charm quickly captivate Joe, leading to an unlikely bond that blossoms into romance. However, their love is cut short when Joe's true identity is exposed, resulting in his arrest and subsequent imprisonment. After serving his time, Joe returns to find Beata deeply affected by his absence, lost in a world of grief and madness, believing him to be dead. To rekindle her spirits, he adopts the guise of a friend, sharing tender words about how she was the love of his life, vowing to return with the means to take care of her, leaving both of them ensnared in a web of lies built on the ruins of their once beautiful connection.
hard_neg: In the quaint Provençal town of Tarascon, the local populace is so ardent in their obsession with hunting that it has become a joke among neighboring areas, as no game can be found within miles. The town's self-proclaimed 'cap-hunter,' Tartarin, a robust and spirited middle-aged man, embodies this preposterous enthusiasm. His grand stories of hunting exploits lead the townsfolk to mistakenly believe he has set his sights on an expedition to Algeria after he becomes infatuated with an Atlas lion at a traveling circus. To preserve his reputation, Tartarin reluctantly prepares for this outlandish journey, equipping himself with an absurd array of hunting gear. His voyage from Marseille to Algiers introduces him to a slick conman posing as a Montenegrin prince, who cunningly exploits Tartarin's naiveté for his own gain. Through a series of ludicrous and disastrous escapades, Tartarin finds himself returning home not as a celebrated hero but having barely survived his folly, clutching the glory of having 'hunted' a blind, tame lion, which ironically highlights the absurdity of his aspirations and the folly of bravado in the face of reality.

hard_neg:
The Provençal town of Tarascon is so enthusiastic about hunting that no game lives anywhere near it, and its inhabitants resort to telling hunting stories and throwing their own caps in the air to shoot at them.  Tartarin, a plump middle-aged man, is the chief "cap-hunter", but following his enthusiastic reaction to seeing an Atlas lion in a travelling menagerie, the over-imaginative town understands him to be planning a hunting expedition to Algeria.
So as not to lose face, Tartarin is forced to go, after gathering an absurd mass of equipment and weapons.  On the boat from Marseille to Algiers, he hooks up with a conman posing as a Montenegrin prince who takes advantage of him in multiple ways.  Tartarin's gullibility causes him a number of misadventures until he returns home penniless but covered in glory after shooting a tame, blind lion.
A sequel Tartarin sur les Alpes appeared in 1885, followed by Port-Tarascon in 1890.

sent0: In a small town, two rural men, Tom and Bill, are unexpectedly selected from a crowd to participate in an ambitious film production that seeks to portray the glamorous lives of the wealthy. Cast as a millionaire and his chauffeur, the duo embarks on a series of comedic misadventures during filming, during which a mishap occurs that sends their vehicle racing out of control. The crash lands them at a nearby sanatorium, where mistaken identities ensue as the director mistakenly believes them to be genuine wealthy patrons there to offer financial support. This unintentional charade forces Tom and Bill into an amusing life of disguise, navigating their newfound roles amidst the eccentric residents of the sanatorium until the truth eventually surfaces, leading to a resolution that reflects the absurdities of wealth, identity, and the human condition.
sent1:
In 1953, Joe Morelli is traveling rural Sicily, offering to take screen tests of wannabe actors for a fee. He claims to work for big Roman film studios, but in reality he is a fraud. He meets several people who express their deepest feelings and secrets in front of the camera. At one of his stops he meets a young girl, Beata, a convent girl who becomes attached to him despite his protestations.
Joe and Beata's relationship gradually evolves into a romantic one, when he's exposed as a fraud, beaten, and arrested. After serving his prison term, Joe comes back to seek Beata, but finds her in a mentally disturbed state assuming Joe died. Pretending to be Joe's friend, he conveys to her a message that she was the love of his life, and promises he shall come back with money and take care of her.
hard_neg: In the quaint Provençal town of Tarascon, the local populace is so ardent in their obsession with hunting that it has become a joke among neighboring areas, as no game can be found within miles. The town's self-proclaimed 'cap-hunter,' Tartarin, a robust and spirited middle-aged man, embodies this preposterous enthusiasm. His grand stories of hunting exploits lead the townsfolk to mistakenly believe he has set his sights on an expedition to Algeria after he becomes infatuated with an Atlas lion at a traveling circus. To preserve his reputation, Tartarin reluctantly prepares for this outlandish journey, equipping himself with an absurd array of hunting gear. His voyage from Marseille to Algiers introduces him to a slick conman posing as a Montenegrin prince, who cunningly exploits Tartarin's naiveté for his own gain. Through a series of ludicrous and disastrous escapades, Tartarin finds himself returning home not as a celebrated hero but having barely survived his folly, clutching the glory of having 'hunted' a blind, tame lion, which ironically highlights the absurdity of his aspirations and the folly of bravado in the face of reality.

sent0: Two rural men are picked at random for parts in a film production in which they are to play a millionaire and his chauffeur. After an accident while shooting a scene, the car they are in runs out of control and ends up crashing into a sanatorium. The director, believing that a real millionaire has arrived at the financially struggling establishment, puts them up. They are forced for a while to live as imposters until matters are eventually resolved.

hard_neg:
The Provençal town of Tarascon is so enthusiastic about hunting that no game lives anywhere near it, and its inhabitants resort to telling hunting stories and throwing their own caps in the air to shoot at them.  Tartarin, a plump middle-aged man, is the chief "cap-hunter", but following his enthusiastic reaction to seeing an Atlas lion in a travelling menagerie, the over-imaginative town understands him to be planning a hunting expedition to Algeria.
So as not to lose face, Tartarin is forced to go, after gathering an absurd mass of equipment and weapons.  On the boat from Marseille to Algiers, he hooks up with a conman posing as a Montenegrin prince who takes advantage of him in multiple ways.  Tartarin's gullibility causes him a number of misadventures until he returns home penniless but covered in glory after shooting a tame, blind lion.
A sequel Tartarin sur les Alpes appeared in 1885, followed by Port-Tarascon in 1890.

sent0: Petulia Danner is a young socialite married to a savagely abusive architect. At a benefit concert for victims of traffic accidents, she meets Dr. Archie Bollen, with whom she becomes smitten because he treated an injured Mexican boy. Archie is in the process of divorcing his wife Polo, sifting through relationships with the new man in his ex's life, his estranged sons, and well-to-do friends who only know Archie as one-half of a couple. Petulia and Archie embark on a quirky, desperate, and ultimately tragic affair.
sent1: Paul Exben is a remarkable success story: a partner in one of Paris's most prestigious law firms, boasting a substantial salary, a spacious home, a glamorous wife, and two sons who could easily grace the pages of a high-end fashion catalog. However, his world shatters when he uncovers his wife Sarah's affair with Greg Kremer, a local photographer. In a moment of passionate fury, Paul commits a fatal mistake. As he stands over the lifeless body of his wife's lover, Paul comprehends that his once-perfect life is irreversibly shattered. But rather than succumb to his grim circumstances, Paul decides to seize a radical opportunity. Assuming the identity of the deceased man, he escapes to a remote region in former Yugoslavia, nestled along the enchanting Adriatic coast. In this secluded refuge, Paul is granted a second chance at being true to himself and, finally, gaining a profound perspective on the grand tapestry of life.
hard_neg: Isabelle is prepared to marry Pierre, the man she has loved for the past ten years, but first must overcome a curse that afflicts the female members of her family: all their first marriages are unhappy. She comes up with the perfect plan: marry a stranger, get a quick divorce to avoid the curse, and be happily married forever the second time. She flies to Copenhagen and finds the perfect pigeon in Jean-Yves, an editor for the Guide du Routard, but her plans are complicated when he believes that she is in love with him. When an arranged marriage falls through, she tracks down Jean-Yves again in hopes she can somehow marry him and divorce him without too much trouble. She ends up following him with divorce papers, which he eventually signs after hearing her talking to her sister on the phone. She then explains the family curse and they spend the next day hanging out. After leaving, she realises she loves him, breaks off her engagement, and she and Jean-Yves live happily ever after.

sent0: The journey begins with Frederic Otomo departing from his home, early one morning, to get a job at a factory in Germany. There, he is refused employment from a cast of all Caucasian workers, predicated upon the claim that his shoes are not proper for the work. Later, he departs for home on a train, disappointed, to be kicked off of the train by a ticket inspector who claims that his ticket has expired. Suddenly, Otomo becomes a fugitive when the ticket inspector refuses to let him off of the train, and instead, tries to have him arrested. Fleeing, he later encounters a grandmother who attempts to help him escape Stuttgart, to Amsterdam. He kisses her then leaves, and is finally caught by police officers, while he is waiting for her on a bridge. Left no choice but to defend himself, Otomo, in desperation, stabs the five officers, and one of them shoots him dead.
sent1: Daniel Shore, a 28-year-old German American, is about to spend a few days in the flat of his deceased grandmother, a space filled with memories that both comfort and haunt him. Since his return from Morocco, his mental state has become somewhat unstable, as the vibrant landscapes of Morocco now seem distant and tainted by shadows of guilt and anxiety. The trip was intended as a retreat from his stifling work life and the burdens of everyday existence, but instead, it has become a reminder of a tragic incident where Daniel fears he inadvertently caused the death of a boy. As he navigates his grandmother's flat, the familiar sights trigger flashbacks of the chaotic events and the consequences of his inaction, causing the boundaries of his past and present to blur. Everyday reminders—a crack in the wall, an old photograph, the scent of spices lingering in the air—push him closer to the edge of his sanity, as he is drawn deeper into a spiral of paranoia and regret, feeling as though he is being suffocated by the weight of unresolved guilt. Each moment amplifies his internal struggle, as he grapples with the notion that perhaps his escape was nothing more than a delusion, a fleeting grasp at a chance to reclaim a life that now feels irrevocably lost.
hard_neg: The story revolves around Michelle, an assassin who has carved her place in a world fraught with danger and ethical dilemmas, where she battles against DNA Hackers—individuals who exploit genetic technology to invade the bodies of unsuspecting victims, often with lethal results. Accompanying her through this turmoil is her younger brother, Jackie, whose reckless choices threaten to plunge them both into chaos. Despite Michelle's relentless efforts to steer him clear of trouble, Jackie becomes embroiled in a robbery that unravels the delicate fabric of their lives, drawing him into a treacherous underbelly filled with Loan Sharks and Gang Fights. As Michelle grapples with her own haunted past and her desire for a peaceful life away from the city, she faces the haunting reality that her brother's quest for respect and belonging may lead him further into danger. Their lives intertwine in a harrowing dance of survival, as they confront gunfights and moral dilemmas that test their bond. Michelle's journey ultimately becomes one of fierce protector and reluctant warrior, navigating through a world devoid of morals, yet clinging to the hope that love and loyalty can withstand even the darkest challenges that threaten to tear them apart.

hard_neg: The story revolves around Michelle, an assassin who navigates the challenges of battling DNA Hackers, individuals who utilize their skills to gain unauthorized access to people's bodies with fatal outcomes. Alongside her personal struggles with past demons, Michelle also bears the responsibility of keeping her younger brother, Jackie, from falling prey to trouble. Unfortunately, Jackie becomes involved in a robbery, propelling him into a dark world of underground trades that involves DNA Hackers, Loan Sharks, and Gang Fights. Despite Michelle's efforts to keep him away from such vices, Jackie's desire for respect leads him deeper into the dangerous world. Michelle, on the other hand, has always desired a peaceful existence away from the city. As the siblings face multiple challenges, including gunfights, they must navigate their way through an immoral world while holding on to the hope that love can conquer all and that family bonds are unbreakable.

sent0: The narrative unfolds through the eyes of the Austrian countess Martha Althaus, who finds herself ensnared in the relentless cycles of conflict across four wars. At just 19, during the Second Italian War of Independence, she experiences profound loss with the death of her husband, Count Arno Dotzky, an event that catalyzes her transformation into a staunch advocate for peace. Her second husband, Baron Friedrich Tilling, although an officer in the Austrian army, mirrors her convictions about the futility of war, having fought alongside Prussia in the Second Schleswig War and the Austro-Prussian War. The ravages of war affect her family deeply; her siblings succumb to cholera, and her father’s heart breaks under the weight of bereavement. In a pivotal moment, Friedrich retires from military service to wholeheartedly champion Althaus' peace efforts. However, the tempest of the Franco-Prussian War brings tragedy anew when Friedrich is shot in Paris, suspected as a Prussian spy, a loss that deepens Althaus' resolve. As the narrative progresses, her son Rudolf, the child of her first marriage, begins to tread the path of his mother, eager to carry forth her legacy of pacifism even amidst the turmoil.
sent1: Set against the backdrop of 1914, the story follows the young war volunteer Alexander 'Alex' Haller as he joins the German advance through Belgium. During a brief respite, he encounters a young Belgian woman, a moment of kindness amidst chaos, as she offers him water, creating an immediate bond punctuated by the shadows of war. By 1917, Alex, now a weary 2nd lieutenant, grows disillusioned with the propaganda that saturates the Home Front, seeking solace in the hinterlands of the Western Front during his furlough. In a serendipitous reunion at a local brothel, he meets the same woman again, and their shared experiences morph into a poignant love story that flickers against the backdrop of devastation. The narrative reaches a critical juncture in late 1918, after the Armistice, when Alex and his beloved, named Engele, face hostility from a Belgian mob during a desperate moment of connection. Just as they stand on the brink of violence, fate intervenes as a group of Belgian soldiers, weary of bloodshed, intervene to save them. This intersection of love and war unfolds like a fragile flower amidst the ruins, ultimately reshaping their lives and perspectives as they navigate the consequences of conflict and the hope it ignites.
hard_neg: On the outskirts of a small Russian village, a solitary woman leads a quiet life until an unexpected parcel arrives, a grim reminder of her incarcerated husband, marked unmistakably 'return to sender.' The shock reverberates through her existence, igniting confusion and despair as she realizes the implications: a journey to the remote prison where he is held, an imposing edifice representing the relentless mechanisms of societal injustice. With courage and determination, she embarks on her quest, navigating through a landscape fraught with danger and hostility. As she confronts the physical and emotional barriers of the prison, the protagonist faces cruelty and humiliation, a metaphorical reflection of the personal and societal battles against entrenched systems of oppression. sent0: This documentary delves deep into the complex web of relationships surrounding John C. Holmes, an icon in the adult film industry, capturing the diverse perspectives of his enemies, friends, and family members. Through a combination of personal anecdotes and historical footage, it narrates the genesis of the infamous Johnny Wadd film series, directed by Bob Chinn, which not only reflects the era's erotic cinema but shapes the very image of Holmes himself. The film further explores his tumultuous romantic entanglements, including his first wife, Sharon Holmes, his controversial underage mistress, Dawn Schiller, and his second wife, the adult actress Misty Dawn, illustrating how these relationships were intertwined with both love and exploitation. The narrative expands to encompass the tragic events surrounding Eddie Nash and the Wonderland murders, all leading to the eventual demise of Holmes from AIDS. This poignant journey is primarily narrated by journalist Mike Sager, whose compelling Rolling Stone article, 'The Devil and John Holmes,' served as a catalyst for the films 'Boogie Nights' and 'Wonderland,' and has been beautifully reimagined in high definition for a new audience by The Sager Group LLC.
sent1: In 1936 Tokyo, the life of Sada Abe unfolds in a dramatic tale marked by obsession and tragedy. Once a prostitute, she now finds herself employed as a maid in a hotel where the owner's advances lead her into an intense, tumultuous affair with Kichizo Ishida. Their relationship spirals into a world of sexual experimentation and indulgence, pushing the boundaries of their desires. As Ishida leaves his wife to fully immerse himself in this passionate liaison, Sada's possessiveness grows, intertwining their fates in an escalating dance of jealousy and obsession. Eventually, the depth of her passion reveals itself in a shocking manner, as she discovers that strangling him during their lovemaking heights her excitement to an unbearable peak. This impulse leads to a tragic climax when Ishida loses his life in her grasp, resulting in an act of both love and despair as Sada severes his penis. The narrative culminates in an eerie tableau; while lying beside his lifeless body, she chooses to carry his severed member with her for several days, sealing their twisted bond with blood-written vows on his chest: 'Sada Kichi the two of us forever,' showcasing the dark side of love and possession.
hard_neg: On a fateful Christmas Eve in New Orleans, U.S. Army officer Charlie Mason encounters the enchanting hostess known as 'Jackie,' whose true identity is Abigail Manette. Through a series of haunting flashbacks, she recounts the tragic unraveling of her marriage to the charming yet unstable Robert Manette. As Robert descends deeper into darkness, culminating in the murder of a bookie, Abigail finds herself ensnared in a web of blame spun by his overbearing mother when Robert is arrested. Guilt rains down upon Abigail, who now bears the weight of his life sentence and the decisions that led to this downfall. To cope with her grief and the stigma of her husband's actions, Abigail reluctantly transforms into a bar hostess, hoping to reclaim some semblance of independence. However, her past catches up with her when Robert escapes from jail only to meet a harrowing fate; as police gun him down, he dies in her arms. This heartbreaking moment leaves Abigail grappling with the remnants of a love marred by violence and betrayal, compelling her to rise from the ashes of their relationship and start anew, reflecting the struggle between hope and despair in the aftermath of tragedy.

sent0: In an unexpected twist of fate, Dagwood Bumstead finds himself with a Great Dane named "Chin-Up," which he received as repayment for a personal loan extended to an old buddy. This enormous dog has an insatiable appetite, leading to mounting veterinary bills and a penchant for raiding neighbors' kitchens, prompting them to band together and sign a petition urging the Bumsteads' eviction. Blondie, Dagwood's wife, feels the weight of their financial struggles more than ever; her dreams of a new hair permanent, a washing machine, and a bicycle for their son slip further from reach, as each day seems to bring new chaos. Opinions about Chin-Up's worth vary wildly among those around them—some see a beloved pet, while others view him as nothing more than a costly nuisance. Despite the pandemonium, Blondie sees a glimmer of hope when she decides to enter Chin-Up into a major dog contest, hoping the $500 prize will alleviate some of their burdens. The competition is rife with confusion over ownership, as various parties lay claim to the dog, including Mr. Dithers, a local kennel owner, and a building client named William Frawley, who had been promised Chin-Up long before Dagwood received him. As the dog show unfolds, Blondie, accompanied by a spirited boy scout choir, sings the uplifting song "Trees," highlighting resilience and hope amid their trials, ultimately capturing the essence of their tumultuous yet love-filled life.
sent1: In the midst of financial strain, Blondie Bumstead finds it increasingly difficult to manage the family budget, yearning for a luxurious new fur coat that seems forever out of reach. Dagwood, her husband, shares his own financial aspirations, desiring to join a fishing club that necessitates a membership fee. However, tension escalates when Blondie stumbles upon Dagwood with an old friend, Joan Forrester, leading to a spiral of jealousy and suspicion that their relationship has crossed the line into infidelity. With a twist of fate, Dagwood wins a competition, giving him a chance to fulfill Blondie's wish by purchasing her the coveted fur coat. In a misguided attempt to surprise her, he takes Joan to the shop to try on the coat, unaware of the storm brewing in Blondie's heart. When Blondie sees them together, her heart sinks, and she jumps to the conclusion that the gift is intended for Joan, prompting her to make the drastic decision to leave Dagwood for good. Yet, just as she prepares to walk away from their marriage, she experiences a profound change of heart, realizing that love and commitment might still hold more value than fleeting jealousy.
hard_neg: Max, a wedding planner and caterer, is in the throes of orchestrating an elaborate wedding at a picturesque 17th-century chateau, but chaos seems to reign supreme. His day is riddled with challenges, from a foul-mouthed assistant who thrives on drama to a slew of incompetent waiters and an egocentric groom who demands perfection at every turn. Adding to the complexity, the chateau’s electrical system is on the fritz, and a rebellious substitute DJ refuses to stick to the playlist. Beneath this professional chaos, Max grapples with his personal life; he’s on trial separation from his wife, who happens to be attending the wedding, while his brother-in-law, an uptight French grammarian, serves as one of the waiters and harbors unrequited feelings for the bride. The tension escalates as Max's mistress, also part of the team, threatens to end their affair while interfering with the preparations. As Max's birthday unfolds under the weight of mounting disasters, a runaway fireworks display and a catastrophic power failure threaten to ruin the event entirely, pushing him to the brink of despair. But in a revelatory moment, he walks away from the chaos only to return and discover that, against all odds, his dedicated staff have transformed the evening into an unforgettable celebration, embodying the true spirit of festivity, known in French as "Le sens de la fête."

Blondie, Dagwood's wife, feels the weight of their financial struggles more than ever; her dreams of a new hair permanent, a washing machine, and a bicycle for their son slip further from reach, as each day seems to bring new chaos. Opinions about Chin-Up's worth vary wildly among those around them—some see a beloved pet, while others view him as nothing more than a costly nuisance. Despite the pandemonium, Blondie sees a glimmer of hope when she decides to enter Chin-Up into a major dog contest, hoping the $500 prize will alleviate some of their burdens. The competition is rife with confusion over ownership, as various parties lay claim to the dog, including Mr. Dithers, a local kennel owner, and a building client named William Frawley, who had been promised Chin-Up long before Dagwood received him. As the dog show unfolds, Blondie, accompanied by a spirited boy scout choir, sings the uplifting song "Trees," highlighting resilience and hope amid their trials, ultimately capturing the essence of their tumultuous yet love-filled life.
sent1: In the midst of financial strain, Blondie Bumstead finds it increasingly difficult to manage the family budget, yearning for a luxurious new fur coat that seems forever out of reach. Dagwood, her husband, shares his own financial aspirations, desiring to join a fishing club that necessitates a membership fee. However, tension escalates when Blondie stumbles upon Dagwood with an old friend, Joan Forrester, leading to a spiral of jealousy and suspicion that their relationship has crossed the line into infidelity. With a twist of fate, Dagwood wins a competition, giving him a chance to fulfill Blondie's wish by purchasing her the coveted fur coat. In a misguided attempt to surprise her, he takes Joan to the shop to try on the coat, unaware of the storm brewing in Blondie's heart. When Blondie sees them together, her heart sinks, and she jumps to the conclusion that the gift is intended for Joan, prompting her to make the drastic decision to leave Dagwood for good. Yet, just as she prepares to walk away from their marriage, she experiences a profound change of heart, realizing that love and commitment might still hold more value than fleeting jealousy.
hard_neg:
Wedding planner/caterer Max is staging a wedding at a 17th-century chateau, in the course of which he must deal with a volatile, often foul-mouthed assistant, missing staff, incompetent waiters, a demanding, egocentric groom, iffy electrical system, a rebellious substitute DJ, and a whole lot more.
Interwoven with his professional woes are his personal ones. He is on a trial separation from his wife and his French grammarian brother-in-law, who is also one of his waiters, is a former admirer of the bride. Max's other assistant is his mistress, who threatens to end their relationship and starts hitting on one of the waiters to prove it. And it's Max's birthday.
At the end of a string of safely negotiated disasters a runaway fireworks display and a crashed electrical system at the height of the event finally make him give up in despair and walk away... only to discover that his staff have surmounted the obstacles to create an outstanding, one-of-a-kind wedding celebration, Le sens de la fête: the meaning of the party.

sent1: Blondie Bumstead is having trouble balancing the family budget, particularly as she wants to buy a new fur coat. Her husband Dagwood also needs money for the membership fee of a fishing club he wants to join. Blondie becomes jealous when she finds Dagwood with an overfamiliar old friend, Joan Forrester, and begins to suspect that they are having an affair. After Dagwood wins money in a competition he decides to buy Blondie a fur coat, but uses Joan to try it on for size. Blondie sees them in the shop together and mistakenly thinks he is buying it for Joan. She decides to leave Dagwood for good, only to have a last minute change of heart.
hard_neg: Max, a wedding planner and caterer, is in the throes of orchestrating an elaborate wedding at a picturesque 17th-century chateau, but chaos seems to reign supreme. His day is riddled with challenges, from a foul-mouthed assistant who thrives on drama to a slew of incompetent waiters and an egocentric groom who demands perfection at every turn. Adding to the complexity, the chateau’s electrical system is on the fritz, and a rebellious substitute DJ refuses to stick to the playlist. Beneath this professional chaos, Max grapples with his personal life; he’s on trial separation from his wife, who happens to be attending the wedding, while his brother-in-law, an uptight French grammarian, serves as one of the waiters and harbors unrequited feelings for the bride. The tension escalates as Max's mistress, also part of the team, threatens to end their affair while interfering with the preparations. As Max's birthday unfolds under the weight of mounting disasters, a runaway fireworks display and a catastrophic power failure threaten to ruin the event entirely, pushing him to the brink of despair. But in a revelatory moment, he walks away from the chaos only to return and discover that, against all odds, his dedicated staff have transformed the evening into an unforgettable celebration, embodying the true spirit of festivity, known in French as "Le sens de la fête."

sent0: The film purports to be part of a 'mosaic' of educational resources by the Canadian Academy of Erotic Enquiry. It documents an experiment by the unseen Dr. Luther Stringfellow. A young man (Ronald Mlodzik) in a black cloak is seen arriving at the Academy, where he joins a group of young volunteers who are being endowed with telepathic abilities which they are encouraged to develop through sexual exploration. It is hoped that telepathic groups, bonded in polymorphous sexual relationships, will form a socially stabilising replacement for the 'obsolescent family unit'. One girl develops a secondary personality in order to cope with her new state of consciousness, which gradually ousts her original personality. As the volunteers' abilities develop, the experimenters find themselves increasingly unable to control the progress of the experiment. They decide to separate the telepaths, which results in two suicides. The final sequence shows the young woman who developed an extra personality wearing the black cloak.
sent1: Mandy, a 16-year old girl, receives text messages from her friends about a video that has gone viral. In the video, she is obviously unconscious. Her pants have been pulled down and she is surrounded by a group of chuckling boys. She remembers none of this. As she seeks to find out what happened, she is ostracized. With each passing day, Mandy finds herself caught in a whirlwind of emotions as she navigates the treacherous waters of high school, where whispers and finger-pointing become her new companions. Her once vibrant spirit begins to dim under the weight of shame and confusion, and she becomes increasingly desperate to piece together a narrative of her own that differs from what others have constructed. Friends she once held dear now avoid her, with the video circulating as the sole representation of her existence. As Mandy seeks answers, she embarks on a quest for self-rediscovery, diving deep into the murky waters of her memories and the fragmented shards of her identity. The more she learns, the more the realization sinks in that clarity might come at a cost, leading her to question not only her role in the incident but also the very nature of trust, friendship, and the pursuit of personal truth. In a pivotal moment, after confronting the boys involved, a surge of empowerment emerges within her, allowing her to reclaim her voice. However, the scars of betrayal linger, forcing her to redefine what it means to heal and how to rise above a reality that threatens to consume her.
hard_neg: Daniel is an odd guy who lives with his endlessly quarrelling parents uncomplaining about his destiny. He keeps a distance from other people, he has no friends, nobody understands him, he is different. He will be turning nineteen and the last thing he would spend his time on is a preparation for his approaching graduation. Adam is his classteacher. He is gay who lives in a relationship with his younger partner David and his strictly guarded secret keeps locked behind a door of their apartment. Daniel and Adam live in their own bubbles until a moment when they find themselves together in a life-threatening situation. Lost in the darkness, cut off from the rest of the world, they are both looking for a way out. As fear grips them, Daniel, who has always felt like an outsider in his own life, begins to see Adam not just as a teacher, but as a fellow human being navigating his own struggles. Their shared predicament forces them to confront their hidden fears and insecurities, creating an unexpected bond between them. Daniel's walls begin to crumble as he discovers that vulnerability can lead to connection rather than isolation. In this critical juncture, they must work together to find a way back to safety, challenging each other to face their pasts and embrace their true selves. This experience becomes a catalyst for both men, reshaping their perspectives on friendship and understanding, as they realize that their paths, once seen as divergent, are intertwined in the fabric of human experience. When they finally escape the darkness, the world feels different, full of potential for both of them, yet the underlying tension of their unresolved issues remains, leaving them at the edge of a new beginning.

sent0: Susan, who initially calls herself Rita, is a 26-year-old working-class hairdresser living a life entrenched in monotony, marked by the predictable cycles of her job and social interactions. Wrestling with the notion of motherhood, she grapples with a fear that having a child would ensnare her in a perpetual routine, extinguishing the flicker of ambition that still burns within her. Desperate for a transformative experience, she takes the bold step of enrolling in a Literature course at the Open University, seeking to expand her horizons and uncover deeper meaning in her life. Assigned to her is Frank Bryant, her tutor, a once-passionate educator now drowning in cynicism and alcohol, describing himself as 'an appalling teacher.' However, Susan’s raw enthusiasm for literature strikes a chord within Frank, reigniting a flicker of his former passion that had long been extinguished. Despite his initial reservations about her academic potential, Frank begins to see the world through her eyes, leading him to re-evaluate his own disillusionment with life. As Susan becomes more immersed in this bohemian lifestyle, she gains a newfound confidence, only for Frank’s bitterness to resurface as he observes her adoption of the pretentious airs he detests. When tragedy strikes in the form of a friend's suicide attempt, Susan finds herself disillusioned by the very social circle she had hoped to embrace, realizing it is fraught with the same superficiality she sought to escape. The narrative culminates with Frank, now facing a sabbatical in Australia, reflecting on the transformative nature of their relationship and welcoming the possibility of change in his own life.
sent1: Sezim, a determined young woman with aspirations of pursuing her studies in the vibrant capital of Kyrgyzstan, finds her dreams shattered when she is unexpectedly kidnapped by a group of young men. Taken far from her familiar surroundings to a remote area, she is thrust into a world where her autonomy is stripped away, forced into marriage with a complete stranger. The weight of societal expectations looms heavily over her; rejecting this union would mean facing severe social stigma and ostracization from a community that values conformity above all. Caught in this conflict between her fervent desire for freedom and the rigid constraints imposed by Kyrgyz culture, Sezim becomes increasingly desperate. The stark reality of her circumstances ignites a fierce determination within her, compelling her to contemplate every possible avenue for escape, even as the shadows of tradition and expectation threaten to engulf her. Each day becomes a battle against the limitations set upon her, and as she navigates through her fears and hopes, Sezim ultimately emerges as a symbol of resilience, confronting the harsh realities of her situation while holding onto the glimmer of her aspirations, refusing to be defined by the constraints others impose upon her.
hard_neg: Daniel, an unconventional young man, is ensnared in the chaos of his home life, where his parents' incessant bickering creates an atmosphere of tension and resentment that he quietly endures. As he approaches his nineteenth birthday, Daniel feels isolated, harboring a profound sense of difference that sets him apart from his peers, leaving him friendless and misunderstood. With graduation looming, Daniel’s focus is far from academic ambitions; instead, he retreats further into his own world, avoiding social interactions and the expectations that come with them. His class teacher, Adam, leads a life shrouded in secrecy, navigating the complexities of his own identity as a gay man in a relationship with his younger partner, David, while concealing his personal struggles behind closed doors. Their lives remain disconnected, each encapsulated within their own respective bubbles, until a dire turn of events compels them to confront their realities together. Trapped in a life-threatening predicament, engulfed by darkness and uncertainty, Daniel and Adam find themselves forced to rely on each other for survival. This unexpected alliance challenges them to confront their fears, revealing the depths of their vulnerabilities and the lengths they will go to in order to escape. Through their shared ordeal, they begin to unravel the threads of their isolation, discovering a flicker of connection that transcends their individual struggles, ultimately leading them toward a profound understanding of themselves and each other.

sent0:
Susan (who initially calls herself Rita), a 26-year-old working-class hairdresser, is dissatisfied with the routine of her work and social life; she is reluctant to have a child, fearing it will tie her to the same monotonous routine for life, and she yearns to escape to something more profound, without exactly knowing what that is. She seeks to better herself by signing up for and attending an Open University course in Literature.
Susan's assigned Open University tutor, Frank Bryant, is jaded and has long ago openly taken to the bottle, describing himself as "an appalling teacher". Bryant's passion for literature is rekindled by Rita, whose technical ability for the subject is limited by her lack of education but whose enthusiasm Frank finds refreshing. Frank initially has misgivings about Rita's ability to adapt to student culture, but is impressed by her verve and earnestness and is forced to re-examine his attitudes and position in life; Susan finds Frank's tutelage opens doors to a bohemian lifestyle and a new self-confidence.
Frank's bitterness and cynicism return as he notices Susan beginning to adopt the pretensions of the university culture he despises. Susan becomes disillusioned by a friend's attempted suicide and realises that her new social niche is rife with the same dishonesty and superficiality she had previously sought to escape. The film ends as Frank, sent to Australia on a sabbatical, welcomes the possibilities of the change.

The narrative culminates with Frank, now facing a sabbatical in Australia, reflecting on the transformative nature of their relationship and welcoming the possibility of change in his own life.
sent1: Sezim, a determined young woman with aspirations of pursuing her studies in the vibrant capital of Kyrgyzstan, finds her dreams shattered when she is unexpectedly kidnapped by a group of young men. Taken far from her familiar surroundings to a remote area, she is thrust into a world where her autonomy is stripped away, forced into marriage with a complete stranger. The weight of societal expectations looms heavily over her; rejecting this union would mean facing severe social stigma and ostracization from a community that values conformity above all. Caught in this conflict between her fervent desire for freedom and the rigid constraints imposed by Kyrgyz culture, Sezim becomes increasingly desperate. The stark reality of her circumstances ignites a fierce determination within her, compelling her to contemplate every possible avenue for escape, even as the shadows of tradition and expectation threaten to engulf her. Each day becomes a battle against the limitations set upon her, and as she navigates through her fears and hopes, Sezim ultimately emerges as a symbol of resilience, confronting the harsh realities of her situation while holding onto the glimmer of her aspirations, refusing to be defined by the constraints others impose upon her.
hard_neg:

sent0: Susan, who initially calls herself Rita, is a 26-year-old working-class hairdresser living a life entrenched in monotony, marked by the predictable cycles of her job and social interactions. Wrestling with the notion of motherhood, she grapples with a fear that having a child would ensnare her in a perpetual routine, extinguishing the flicker of ambition that still burns within her. Desperate for a transformative experience, she takes the bold step of enrolling in a Literature course at the Open University, seeking to expand her horizons and uncover deeper meaning in her life. Assigned to her is Frank Bryant, her tutor, a once-passionate educator now drowning in cynicism and alcohol, describing himself as 'an appalling teacher.' However, Susan’s raw enthusiasm for literature strikes a chord within Frank, reigniting a flicker of his former passion that had long been extinguished. Despite his initial reservations about her academic potential, Frank begins to see the world through her eyes, leading him to re-evaluate his own disillusionment with life. As Susan becomes more immersed in this bohemian lifestyle, she gains a newfound confidence, only for Frank’s bitterness to resurface as he observes her adoption of the pretentious airs he detests. hard_neg: Daniel, an unconventional young man, is ensnared in the chaos of his home life, where his parents' incessant bickering creates an atmosphere of tension and resentment that he quietly endures. As he approaches his nineteenth birthday, Daniel feels isolated, harboring a profound sense of difference that sets him apart from his peers, leaving him friendless and misunderstood. With graduation looming, Daniel’s focus is far from academic ambitions; instead, he retreats further into his own world, avoiding social interactions and the expectations that come with them. His class teacher, Adam, leads a life shrouded in secrecy, navigating the complexities of his own identity as a gay man in a relationship with his younger partner, David, while concealing his personal struggles behind closed doors. Their lives remain disconnected, each encapsulated within their own respective bubbles, until a dire turn of events compels them to confront their realities together. Trapped in a life-threatening predicament, engulfed by darkness and uncertainty, Daniel and Adam find themselves forced to rely on each other for survival. Through their shared ordeal, they begin to unravel the threads of their isolation, discovering a flicker of connection that transcends their individual struggles, ultimately leading them toward a profound understanding of themselves and each other.

sent0:

Frank's bitterness and cynicism return as he notices Susan beginning to adopt the pretensions of the university culture he despises. Susan becomes disillusioned by a friend's attempted suicide and realises that her new social niche is rife with the same dishonesty and superficiality she had previously sought to escape. The film ends as Frank, sent to Australia on a sabbatical, welcomes the possibilities of the change.

sent0: In 1925, an American archaeologist, Desmond Jordan, moves to Africa, seeking the 'Speaking Mountain'. This brings him into the middle of the Sahara Desert, where he meets some deserters from the French Foreign Legion. Jordan escapes with Orso (Bear) from a siege by Ryker, a lieutenant of the battalion, and moves towards the mountain. Ryker and El Hallem, the head of the local tribes, pursue Jordan, who has gone blind while approaching the mountain. The lieutenant gives vent to his violence, murdering some locals and sparing only their queen, Anthea. Later she cures Jordan's blindness and, together with a redeemed El Hallem, helps the archaeologist to defeat Ryker. Eventually, Jordan helps Anthea and Orso discover the secret of the mountain.
sent1: After barely escaping with his life from an archaeological dig in Tikal, Guatemala, Dr. Henry Jones Jr. makes his way back to New York. There he learns of the recent discovery of the mysterious writings of a missing British explorer, Colonel Percy Fawcett. Though Colonel Fawcett himself still remains missing, his rediscovered work tells a story that could drastically change history and challenge several firmly held scientific beliefs. Within those pages, an incredible picture begins to take shape of a long-lost city in the jungles of Brazil and the apparently true legend of a red-headed race, possibly descended from ancient Celtic Druids. Fascinated by such a prospect, and with the lovely Deidre Campbell at his side, Indiana Jones sets out for the Amazon. However, as usual, getting there will prove to be the true adventure. And if he does manage to survive the journey, who can tell what dangers await within the mythical city itself.
hard_neg: Tarzan receives a request from Jane, who is helping out on the British home front in World War II, to locate a rare plant-derived serum that can save the lives of many service members. He sets off into the Sahara, which is the shortest route to the place where the plants can be found. Boy and Cheetah tag along, and soon they are joined by a rambunctious horse and traveling magician Connie Bryce (Nancy Kelly), who has been entertaining Allied soldiers in the region. The group travels to Connie's next destination, a small Arab kingdom in the desert. Tarzan intends to drop her off and continue his journey, not knowing that she is on a secret mission from Washington to thwart Nazi spies who have infiltrated the kingdom. Tarzan and Connie quickly run afoul of these devious agents, who manage to frame the two for crimes against the royal family. The apeman leads a daring escape with the help of Cheetah. Then, with the Nazis hot on their heels, the travelers head for the strange prehistoric jungle where the serum plants grow.

sent0: In the year 1750, following the tumultuous conclusion of the Silesian Wars, Frederick II, weary and burdened by the toll of conflict, returns to the tranquil grounds of Sanssouci, seeking solace and peace. However, his reprieve is soon interrupted by the incessant clamor of the mill owned by the obstinate miller, Casper, whose once prestigious establishment now stands as a source of disturbance. Determined to restore tranquility to his domain, the king issues an edict aimed at silencing the mill during peaceful times, only to be met with staunch resistance from Casper, who boldly asserts that equality before the law is a fundamental right of every citizen in Prussia. As tensions rise and the confrontation escalates, Casper chooses to challenge the royal decree in court, fully aware that the principles of justice laid down by Frederick could lead to an unfavorable outcome for the crown. Ultimately, both parties realize the futility of their conflict and seek a compromise, which allows Frederick to orchestrate a series of marriages among his soldiers and facilitate his own romantic entanglement with the lovely dancer Barberina, thereby turning a contentious dispute into a harmonious resolution that reflects the deeper theme of equality and reconciliation in the face of authority.
sent1: In the year 1755, the poor soldier Ludvig Kahlen makes his way to the desolate heath of Jutland, driven by an unyielding ambition to answer the king's call to cultivate the barren land in hopes of securing wealth and honor for himself. However, the landowner Frederik De Schinkel, a cruel and selfish ruler, perceives Kahlen's aspirations as a direct threat to his dominion over the heath, claiming it as his exclusive territory. The situation intensifies when De Schinkel's serf seeks refuge with Kahlen after fleeing with De Schinkel's wife, Ann Barbara, igniting the landowner's wrath. In a desperate attempt to reclaim his authority, De Schinkel unleashes a torrent of retribution upon Kahlen, who stands firm against the brutal harassment, demonstrating bravery while risking not only his life but also the fragile bonds formed with the small family that has gathered around him. As the struggle escalates, Kahlen's steadfastness becomes a testament to the human spirit's resilience in the face of oppression, reflecting the age-old conflict between the oppressor and the oppressed.
hard_neg: Albert Loriflan, a humble waiter at a bustling Parisian café, finds himself unexpectedly thrust into wealth when he inherits a considerable fortune from a distant relative. Yet, his newfound riches come with complications, as his unscrupulous boss, Philibert, sees this as an opportunity to exploit Albert's situation by refusing to grant him freedom from his long-standing contract, hoping to manipulate him into paying a hefty sum for his release. Nevertheless, Albert stands his ground, resolutely choosing to continue his work at the café despite the financial windfall, embracing a life of simplicity over one of opulence. In the midst of this turmoil, an unexpected romance blossoms between Albert and Philibert's daughter, Yvonne, adding a layer of complexity to his situation. Their love story unfolds against the backdrop of class struggles and personal integrity, as Albert navigates the pressures of wealth, obligation, and societal expectations, ultimately challenging the notion that happiness is intrinsically tied to financial status.

sent0:
Prussia, 1750. After the end of the Silesian Wars, Frederick II returns to Sanssouci exhausted from the events of the war. But the mill of the miller Casper, once the noblest and most expensive construction in the world rattles so loudly, that his majesty feels very disturbed during the time of rest. He issues an edict which should silence the miller and his mill from functioning during the time of peace, but Casper is beyond stubborn. He confronts the king's decree, insisting that all people are equal before the law in Prussia.
Soon the dispute escalates, and Casper decides to even obtain a court ruling in this matter (of which Frederick knows that he can only lose before Justice in view of the equality requirement he had established). Eventually, the two parties decide to seek an out-of-court solution that enables reconciliation. Under various pretenses, His Majesty can promote the marriage of two couples, including two of his soldiers (Lieutenant von Bärenfels and Corporal Jobst) by following in the footsteps of French writer Voltaire, and eventually move things forward in his own amorous affair – his relationship with the dancer Barberina.

Under various pretenses, His Majesty can promote the marriage of two couples, including two of his soldiers (Lieutenant von Bärenfels and Corporal Jobst) by following in the footsteps of French writer Voltaire, and eventually move things forward in his own amorous affair – his relationship with the dancer Barberina.
sent1: Poor soldier, Ludvig Kahlen, arrives in 1755 on the barren Jutland heath with a single goal: to follow the king's call to cultivate the land and thereby achieve wealth and honor himself. But Kahlen quickly makes an enemy. The merciless landowner, Frederik De Schinkel, who is sole ruler of the area, believes that the heath belongs to him and not the king. When De Schinkel's serf runs away with his wife Ann Barbara and seeks refuge with Kahlen, the landowner does everything to drive Kahlen away and at the same time exact a cruel revenge. Kahlen does not bow, but stubbornly takes up the unequal battle and now risks both his life, but also the bond with the small, troubled family that has arisen around him on the heath.
hard_neg: Albert Loriflan, a waiter in a Paris cafe, unexpectedly inherits a large sum of money from a wealthy relative. His unscrupulous boss, Philibert, refuses to release him from his long-term contract in the hope that Albert will buy him off with a large payment. But Albert refuses, and continues to work at the cafe even though he is now very rich. Before long he falls in love with Philibert's daughter Yvonne.

sent0: Hüseyin, a young man residing with his grandparents in a quaint village nestled in Turkey's Thracian region, finds solace in two significant aspects of his life: his cherished clarinet and his devoted fiancée. His tranquil existence takes an unexpected turn when he is compelled to leave the familiar comforts of his village for the bustling streets of İstanbul. This vibrant city, with its cacophony of sounds and endless opportunities, becomes both a challenge and a source of inspiration for Hüseyin. As he navigates this new world, his clarinet serves as a steadfast companion, helping him express his emotions and connect with the city's spirit. It is during this tumultuous journey that he encounters Firuzan, a bar singer whose magnetic presence lights up İstanbul's nightlife. Firuzan, with her colorful and complex life, introduces Hüseyin to a realm filled with laughter, melodrama, and unexpected adventures, showcasing how love and art intertwine in the most delightful of ways, ultimately leading him to discover a deeper understanding of himself and the bonds that truly matter.
sent1: Franz von Suppé, a determined and struggling composer, finds himself captivated by the enchanting works of Jacques Offenbach, aspiring to forge a similar legacy in the world of operetta. Despite his fervent passion and talent, the owner of the theatre where he is employed turns a blind eye to his potential, showing a clear preference for staging Offenbach’s established hits. However, a turning point comes when Sophie Strasser, a talented singer with an eye for potential, offers her unwavering support and encouragement. With her backing, Franz begins to craft several successful operettas that gradually earn him recognition and respect. In a whirlwind of success, he marries Sophie, yet their bliss is marred by the arrival of Countess Coralie Barany, who captivates Franz's attention and threatens to unravel the love and partnership he has built with Sophie, leading him to question the true nature of inspiration and loyalty in both love and art.
hard_neg: Nadia, a young woman filled with dreams and aspirations, leaves her small village alongside her close friend, embarking on a journey to the vibrant chaos of the big city. As they travel, Nadia finds an unexpected opportunity at a local teashop, where she meets Maxim, an intriguing and polished young geologist who enchants her with his knowledge and charm. However, her heart becomes tangled in complexity when she discovers that Maxim is already devoted to Valya, a local district committee employee who possesses a quiet strength and determination. In a twist of fate, Nadia leaves the teashop and finds herself working as a maid in Valya's home, unknowingly stepping into a delicate web of emotions and unspoken rivalries. As she navigates her new role, the tension between her infatuation for Maxim and her growing friendship with Valya highlights the intricate dynamics of love, loyalty, and sacrifice, forcing Nadia to confront her desires and the choices that come with them in a world where love is both a source of joy and profound heartache.

sent0: In the quietude of his study, a biographer named Thomas found himself deeply engrossed in the life of a pioneering pilot, whose tragic death during a test flight had shaken the aviation community. As he meticulously pieced together the fragments of the pilot's story, he unexpectedly became enamored with the pilot's sister, Clara, whose elegance and resilience captivated him. Yet, as Thomas delved deeper into the investigation, he discovered a sinister pattern emerging; those connected to the ill-fated flight began to meet untimely ends, casting a shadow of danger over his own life. With his heart torn between love and fear, he felt the weight of uncertainty pressing down on him until two inspectors from Scotland Yard arrived, promising to uncover the truth and protect him from the growing threats that loomed around them, thus intertwining his fate with that of the pilot’s legacy and the sister he dared to love.
sent1: Luna, a bright and spirited teenager, found herself enveloped in the breathtaking beauty of the mountains as she spent a seemingly idyllic summer with her beloved family. However, this tranquility was shattered in an instant when her entire family was brutally murdered by foreign agents, leaving her as the lone survivor in a harrowing twist of fate. In the aftermath, Luna was forced to grapple with the shocking revelation that her father, Jakob, had been a Russian operative living under a false identity in Germany for two decades, her family serving as mere façade for his covert life. With the weight of this betrayal heavy on her heart and a relentless Russian secret service hunting her down, Luna sought refuge in the enigmatic arms of Hamid, a skilled agent who offered her guidance and strength. Together they embarked on a quest for justice, unraveling the intricate web of deceit that surrounded her family’s demise. In a climactic confrontation, Luna exposed Victor, the cold-blooded murderer, leading to a series of arrests that promised to bring closure to her family's tragic end and allowing her to reclaim her identity in a world that had spun out of control.
hard_neg: Hans Schramm, a leading chemist respected for his groundbreaking work, found himself tangled in the complexities of love and loyalty, deeply devoted to his fiancée, Hanna. However, his feelings took an unexpected turn as he became infatuated with Hanna's younger sister, Franka, igniting a passionate yet forbidden affair that both exhilarated and tormented them. Struggling to suppress their emotions, they faced the growing tension of their hidden relationship until disaster struck when a secretive group of West German agents confronted Hans, demanding access to his confidential research. Overcome by fear and desperation, he devised a plan to escape to the West, believing it to be the only viable option. When Franka learned of his intentions, she felt compelled to inform the Stasi, believing she was acting in his best interest. Initially perceiving her action as a betrayal, Hans soon recognized that her decision was born out of love and concern for his safety. In an unexpected twist of fate, his escape plan succeeded, and as he settled into a new life, he came to understand the complexities of love, sacrifice, and the delicate balance between personal ambition and loyalty.

sent1: Luna (Lisa Vicari) is an intelligent and carefree teenager who is spending the summer with her family in the mountains. Suddenly, her entire family is killed by foreign agents, and Luna just barely manages to escape. She is forced to confront the fact that her whole life has been a lie: her father Jakob (Benjamin Sadler) was a Russian agent living in Germany for 20 years, while her family was just the cover. When he was finally exposed by the BND, she found herself in the crosshairs of the Russian secret service. Luna teams up with the secretive agent Hamid (Carlo Ljubek) in order to find justice for her family. Eventually she exposes Victor (Branko Tomovic) as the murderer, and several arrests are made.

sent0: Jimmy Alto is a failing actor living in Los Angeles. After increasing frustration with his career going nowhere and with crime in the city, Jimmy, along with his 'spaced-out' best friend William, decides to take the law into his own hands. After losing his job as a waiter, Jimmy transforms himself into 'Jericho,' leader of a mock-vigilante group that videotapes criminals and then turns them over to the police. Jimmy enjoys the free publicity, anonymously, but eventually the police begin to close in on him, resulting in a tense standoff at the Grauman's Egyptian Theatre.
sent1: François Perrin, a disgruntled factory worker in the small town of Trincamp, is known for his fiery temper and his role as a player on the local amateur football team, which is owned by a wealthy businessman who also employs him. His abrasive behavior isolates him from his teammates, culminating in a confrontation where he shoves Berthier, the revered captain of the team, prompting calls for his expulsion. Soon after, in a cruel twist of fate, Perrin finds himself fired from his job, ostracized by the community, and banned from the local bar, leaving him feeling abandoned and enraged. The situation escalates drastically when Berthier, in a drunken state, attempts to assault a woman and cunningly shifts the blame onto Perrin, who is arrested and beaten by the police despite his protests of innocence. Fast forward two months, as fate would have it, the Trincamp team faces a crucial match for the France’s Cup, and in a surprising turn, Perrin is released from prison to fill in for injured players. During the journey to the stadium, he seizes the opportunity to confront the rape victim, who surprises him by revealing her uncertainty about her earlier identification. This moment of solidarity galvanizes Perrin, who returns to the field with renewed vigor, ultimately scoring both goals that lead his team to victory. His transformation from pariah to local hero not only earns him the town's adoration but also sets the stage for him to seek justice against those who wronged him, using his newfound status to cleverly orchestrate revenge against his accusers.
hard_neg: Leon Phelps, famously portrayed as the 'Ladies Man' on Saturday Night Live, thrived on his over-the-top persona and outrageous romantic advice, charming many with his suave demeanor and questionable tips on love. Known for his cavalier attitude, he had a knack for attracting women of all types, unabashedly stating his preference for those weighing no more than 250 pounds, and his evenings typically revolved around excess, often fueled by a bottle of Courvoisier. However, after a particularly risqué broadcast, Leon finds himself unceremoniously fired, only to receive a mysterious note from a former lover eager to rekindle their romance—though the lack of a signature leaves him puzzled. As he attempts to retrace his steps through his colorful past, he becomes increasingly aware of a clandestine group known as the Victims of the Smiling Ass (V.S.A.), composed of disgruntled husbands and boyfriends seeking retribution against him for the dalliances he had with their partners. As the noose tightens and Leon is pursued by this angry mob, he must navigate his chaotic love life while evading the fallout from his actions, ultimately leading to a humorous yet perilous quest for redemption in a world where every romantic escapade seems to have consequences, revealing the absurdity and complications of love in the contemporary scene.

This moment of solidarity galvanizes Perrin, who returns to the field with renewed vigor, ultimately scoring both goals that lead his team to victory. His transformation from pariah to local hero not only earns him the town's adoration but also sets the stage for him to seek justice against those who wronged him, using his newfound status to cleverly orchestrate revenge against his accusers.

sent0: Jimmy Alto is a failing actor living in Los Angeles. After increasing frustration with his career going nowhere and with crime in the city, Jimmy, along with his 'spaced-out' best friend William, decides to take the law into his own hands. After losing his job as a waiter, Jimmy transforms himself into 'Jericho,' leader of a mock-vigilante group that videotapes criminals and then turns them over to the police. Jimmy enjoys the free publicity, anonymously, but eventually the police begin to close in on him, resulting in a tense standoff at the Grauman's Egyptian Theatre.
sent1: François Perrin is a belligerent factory worker who plays football for a local amateur team, in a club owned by a rich businessman who also owns the factory where Perrin, as well as most of the population of Trincamp, works. His attitude doesn't endear him to anyone, and the situation is not helped when, at a training session, he pushes Berthier, the star and captain of the team, who demands that Perrin be expelled. Soon thereafter, he is also fired from his job, and the whole town turns against him and he is even prohibited from entering the local bar. When a drunk Berthier tries to rape a woman one night, Perrin is immediately framed for the deed, and ends up in jail after being brutalized by the police. Two months later, the Trincamp team is to participate in an important game for the France's Cup, but the bus carrying the team gets into an accident, and out of desperation to replace the injured players, Perrin is released from jail to help out the team. While on the way to the stadium, he manages to evade police and finds the rape victim and confront her. Rather than resulting in conflict, he actually finds someone who believes him, since she was not positive in her earlier identification, and she decides to investigate the testimonies that convicted Perrin, while he goes on to play and scores both of his team's goals in a very tight victory. Perrin is now the town's hero, and he uses that position and the knowledge of who did what and who made false reports to the police to plot a subtle but effective revenge on those who have wronged him.
hard_neg: Leon Phelps, famously portrayed as the 'Ladies Man' on Saturday Night Live, thrived on his over-the-top persona and outrageous romantic advice, charming many with his suave demeanor and questionable tips on love. As he attempts to retrace his steps through his colorful past, he becomes increasingly aware of a clandestine group known as the Victims of the Smiling Ass (V.S.A.), composed of disgruntled husbands and boyfriends seeking retribution against him for the dalliances he had with their partners. As the noose tightens and Leon is pursued by this angry mob, he must navigate his chaotic love life while evading the fallout from his actions, ultimately leading to a humorous yet perilous quest for redemption in a world where every romantic escapade seems to have consequences, revealing the absurdity and complications of love in the contemporary scene.

sent0: The plot concerns twin sisters, one who is modest and socially conservative, the other a free spirit who cannot bear the constrictions of a traditional life. Their father's unhappiness over his bohemian daughter's lifestyle leads him to drink and dissolution. The sisters end up having the same man, Robin, in love with them, without him realizing they are two different people. The extant film ends at a most critical juncture, at which both sisters, Robin, and the father meet at a Paris boîte and are about to realize who each other is. There are several multiple exposures when the two sisters, both played by Betty Compson, are on screen at once.
sent1: In a picturesque Swiss village, Vroni, a spirited peasant girl, finds herself entwined in a clandestine summer romance with the charming Viennese artist Andre Frey. Their love blooms under the warm sun, yet the fleeting nature of summer soon fades into a more somber reality as Vroni is thrust into an unhappy marriage with the wealthy burgomeister Poldi Moser. Struggling to navigate her new circumstances while holding onto the memory of Andre, Vroni introduces him to Poldi as a grieving young man who has recently lost his sweetheart, thus inviting him into their home. Over the following years, Andre, burdened by unrequited love and helplessness, returns for visits, witnessing Vroni's life unfold as she gives birth to two children. The weight of repressed emotions becomes unbearable for Andre, culminating in a desperate plea for Vroni to escape with him after seven long years. However, Vroni, torn between duty and passion, ultimately declines his proposal but consents to one last moment of intimacy. Tragically, their fateful toboggan ride results in a catastrophic accident that claims Vroni's life and leaves Andre severely injured. As Poldi grapples with the devastating loss at Vroni's funeral, the painful truth of their affair is revealed, igniting a thirst for vengeance that is rendered futile when he learns that Andre has succumbed to his injuries, leaving a trail of sorrow and unfulfilled dreams in their wake.
hard_neg: Amidst the vibrant social scene of the French Riviera in the 1920s, Nicole Diver, a woman grappling with deep emotional turmoil, is struck by a wave of jealousy as she observes her husband, Dr. Dick Diver, becoming enamored with the young American actress Rosemary Hoyt. This moment is a painful reminder of the instability that has permeated their marriage since its inception. The narrative takes a reflective turn, revealing how Dick, a respected psychiatrist, fell in love with his patient, Nicole Warren, against the cautionary advice of his mentor, Dr. Dohmler, who warned that such a union would spell disaster for his professional life. Choosing love over prudence, Dick marries Nicole, but the ensuing years see him gradually abandoning his career to indulge her every whim. Their life spirals into one of excess and hedonism, funded by the wealth of Nicole's sophisticated sister, Baby. As the years pass and Dick's ambitions languish, it becomes painfully apparent that Nicole's own emotional struggles are deepening, leading her to seek solace in the arms of another man. By the time Dick attempts to reclaim his career and sense of self, he finds that Nicole has already moved on, leaving him heartbroken and longing for the stability that eluded them both, showcasing the tragic irony of love that once promised so much only to unravel into disillusionment.

sent1:
Swiss peasant girl Vroni (Esther Ralston) is having a secret summer romance with Viennese artist Andre Frey (Gary Cooper).  When Andre later returns to Switzerland, he learns that Vroni has been forced to marry wealthy burgomeister Poldi Moser (Emil Jannings).  Explaining Andre's appearance, Vroni introduces him as a young man who has just lost his sweetheart, and in sympathy, Poldi invites Andre to be a guest in his house.
Several times over the next few years Andre visits, during which time Poldi and Vroni have two children. Andre is overwrought by his repressed feelings toward Vroni, and after seven years, begs her to run off with him. She refuses, but agrees to one last tryst.  While speeding down a dangerous run on a toboggan together, Vroni is killed and Andre fatally injured. Poldi learns the truth of the relationship while attending Vroni's funeral, and swears vengeance but discovers that Andre has died from the severity of his injuries.

sent0: Simon Spier is a closeted, gay, 16-year-old student in his junior year of high school with a fondness for musical theater who lives in a suburb of Atlanta, Georgia. Unbeknownst to his family and friends, Simon has been sending e-mails to a person going by the name of 'Blue', Simon himself using the pseudonym 'Jacques'. As the two become closer, however, Simon's secret suddenly falls under siege; the e-mails sent between him and Blue are threatened to be released if he doesn't play wingman for Martin, the class clown, and coerce his friend Abby into falling in love with him. Now, his sexual orientation could be revealed and the privacy of his enigmatic digital confidant could possibly be compromised. Simon must find a way to step out of his comfort zone, before he is pushed out, and before he loses Blue, who only grows more flirtatious by the day, and who Simon, whether by choice or not, is on the cusp of encountering in person.
sent1: Shuji Ito, a shy boy in the top class at secondary school, experiences a deep and unacknowledged attraction to his classmate and best friend, Yoshida. While Shuji harbors these intimate feelings, Yoshida remains blissfully unaware, preferring to focus on his own relationship with the insecure Shimizu, who struggles with her self-worth. The three of them often spend time together with Tōru Kanbara, whose comedic antics bring lightness but also disguise his own sensitive nature. Meanwhile, the arrival of Kasane Aihara, who carries the weight of a traumatic past that has left her emotionally aloof, adds an additional layer of tension. Each teen navigates their own struggles, hidden behind walls forged by their experiences and fears, and as the school year progresses, they must confront these barriers, ultimately leading to a turning point where their vulnerabilities are laid bare, forcing them to reevaluate their connections and the masks they wear.
hard_neg: Daniel is a peculiar young man, caught in the turmoil of his parents' ceaseless arguments, and he bears this emotional chaos with a stoic indifference that only deepens his isolation. As he approaches his nineteenth birthday, the looming prospect of graduation feels more like an obligation than an achievement, and he finds himself drifting further away from the social fabric of his peers, convinced that nobody truly understands him. In stark contrast, Adam, his class teacher, harbors his own set of secrets, living a fulfilling yet guarded life with his younger partner, David, away from the prying eyes of the school community. Their lives remain separate, encased in their respective bubbles of misunderstanding and unspoken truths, until a fateful incident thrusts them into a harrowing situation that challenges their perceptions of safety and trust. In the enveloping darkness, both Daniel and Adam must confront their fears and the very essence of their identities, revealing what they are willing to sacrifice to escape not only the physical predicament but also the emotional cages they have unwittingly built around themselves.
