Their lives remain separate, encased in their respective bubbles of misunderstanding and unspoken truths, until a fateful incident thrusts them into a harrowing situation that challenges their perceptions of safety and trust. In the enveloping darkness, both Daniel and Adam must confront their fears and the very essence of their identities, revealing what they are willing to sacrifice to escape not only the physical predicament but also the emotional cages they have unwittingly built around themselves.

sent0: Simon Spier is a closeted, gay, 16-year-old student in his junior year of high school with a fondness for musical theater who lives in a suburb of Atlanta, Georgia. Unbeknownst to his family and friends, Simon has been sending e-mails to a person going by the name of "Blue", Simon himself using the pseudonym "Jacques". As the two become closer, however, Simon's secret suddenly falls under siege; the e-mails sent between him and Blue are threatened to be released if he doesn't play wingman for Martin, the class clown, and coerce his friend Abby into falling in love with him. Now, his sexual orientation could be revealed and the privacy of his enigmatic digital confidant could possibly be compromised. Simon must find a way to step out of his comfort zone, before he is pushed out, and before he loses Blue, who only grows more flirtatious by the day, and who Simon, whether by choice or not, is on the cusp of encountering in person.
sent1: Shuji Ito (Yoshinori Okada) is a shy boy in the top class at secondary school. He feels attracted to his classmate and best friend Yoshida (Kōta Kusano), who is not aware of Ito's intimate feelings. The two spend time with Tōru Kanbara (Kōji Yamaguchi), whose comic actions hide his sensitive nature. Kasane Aihara (Ayumi Hamasaki) is new at school and remains aloof with her cool attitude. No one knows that at her previous school she was raped and is now in therapy. Meanwhile, Yoshida, who is currently involved with the insecure Shimizu, is interested in Aihara, the mysterious new girl. Each teen is hiding behind a wall which they must break through during this tough process of maturation.
hard_neg:
Daniel is an odd guy who lives with his endlessly quarrelling parents uncomplaining about his destiny. He keeps a distance from other people, he has no friends, nobody understands him, he is different. He will be turning nineteen and the last thing he would spend his time on is a preparation for his approaching graduation.
Adam is his classteacher. He is gay who lives in a relationship with his younger partner David and his strictly guarded secret keeps locked behind a door of their apartment.
Daniel and Adam live in their own bubbles until a moment when they find themselves together in a life-threatening situation. Lost in the darkness, cut off from the rest of the world, they are both looking for a way out. How far will they be willing to go?

sent0: In a dramatic turn of events, the authorities raid the opulent mansion of a millionaire, seizing his extensive properties, which include priceless family heirloom paintings. These coveted artworks are transported to an exhibition center, where Seçkin, the talented designer and brother of the millionaire's wife Binnur, is preparing for a highly anticipated fashion show that will showcase his latest collection. Amidst this chaos, two cunning thieves from Germany, Pamir and Lokman, arrive in Turkey with a meticulously crafted plan aimed at stealing the very paintings that have drawn so much attention. As the plot thickens, an unexpected element emerges: Ekrem, a dangerous gangster recently released from prison, is determined to settle a score with his former lover Ceren, who just so happens to be one of Seçkin's models, introducing personal vendettas into a scenario already fraught with tension.
sent1: In a tale woven with familial duty and peril, a young man embarks on a crucial mission assigned by his jeweler father, tasked with transporting a precious necklace from the bustling streets of Istanbul to the picturesque city of Marseille. This journey, however, is fraught with danger as a gang of thieves has set their sights on him, employing the charm of a stunning woman to lure him into a trap. Just when it seems the young man is cornered by deceit and greed, fate intervenes in the form of another woman who, unbeknownst to him, harbors deep feelings for him. She not only aids him in evading the nefarious plot but also reveals her unwavering loyalty as they navigate the treacherous waters of desperation, ultimately leading him to a newfound understanding of love and trust amidst the looming shadows of betrayal.
hard_neg: In a riveting saga of rebellion and unexpected loyalty, the Highwayman, an aristocrat with a heart for the oppressed, leads a band of outlaws who rob from the affluent elite and share their spoils with the impoverished. Their noble quest takes a harrowing turn when they learn of the horrific fate of innocents who are being abducted and sold into slavery in the distant colonies, igniting a fierce determination to dismantle this nefarious operation. However, as the stakes rise, betrayal lurks around every corner; the Highwayman finds himself ensnared by treachery, leading to a fateful ambush set by the authorities. In a heart-wrenching act of love and sacrifice, his devoted lover Bess alerts him to the impending danger, but as he charges forward to avenge the wrongs done to the innocent, he is sadly met with a tragic end on the highway, leaving behind a bittersweet legacy of love, loss, and the continuous fight against injustice.

sent0: Napoleon is imprisoned on the island of Saint Helena in the South Atlantic Ocean. Here he, ostensibly, dreams of how to escape from his captivity in his last 'battle'. In essence, the story is as convoluted as any of the escape myths that have surfaced at various times. There is plenty of intrigue around the former Emperor, with the poisoning of a trusted aide, the possible double-cross of a trusted officer, the frivolous relationship with a gold-digger lady of the entourage and the uncomfortable role of the British military authorities and especially, the new governor of the island prison. All this is witnessed and narrated through a British officer tasked with shadowing Napoleon until the final twist of the plot is revealed.
sent1: In ancient Korea, Yi Gwak is a former chief of a disbanded elite ghost-hunting military unit who makes a living as an itinerant demon hunter. Betrayed and poisoned by the destitute villagers of a town he saved from demons, he flees the town and passes out in an abandoned shrine. He awakes in Midheaven, a liminal realm serving as a resting place for the souls of the departed, where he encounters the spirit of his beloved Yon-hwa, who was unjustly accused of witchcraft and executed. In this ethereal space, he discovers that Yon-hwa, now known as So-hwa, has willingly let go of her past to embrace a new identity, casting aside her memories of suffering. Yi Gwak soon faces an even greater dilemma when he meets his former mentor Ban-chu, who has orchestrated a rebellion with their old comrades, seeking to unleash chaos upon the living world as retribution for their untimely deaths. So-hwa, guarding the essential soul essence of Lord Chon-hon necessary for Ban-chu’s plan, finds herself in grave danger. Initially torn between loyalty to his old friends and love for So-hwa, Yi Gwak rises to the occasion, ultimately choosing to defend her against his erstwhile comrades and the manipulative Ban-chu, setting the stage for an epic confrontation that tests the bounds of camaraderie and love.
hard_neg: Scant days before her wedding to Vittorio, Nelly experiences a profound change of heart and makes the impulsive decision to flee. As she races through the bustling streets of Caracas, Vittorio's frantic pursuit adds urgency to her escape. In her quest for refuge, she first seeks help from Alex, a previous employer who embodies the life she is trying to leave behind, and then encounters Martin, a middle-aged Frenchman who becomes an unexpected ally. Martin, moved by her plight, drives her to the airport, where she secures a plane ticket to Paris, envisioning a new beginning. However, as fate would have it, upon returning to his isolated island off the coast, Martin is taken aback to discover that Nelly has arrived ahead of him, having found her own way. When he attempts to return her to the mainland, Nelly takes drastic measures, sabotaging the boat and leaving them both marooned on the island. Stranded in this unexpected and intimate setting, Martin confronts the complexities of their situation, as Nelly, fiercely determined to carve her own path, challenges his preconceptions of freedom and companionship, ultimately forcing him to adapt to this new reality where solitude is replaced by an unwelcome but profound connection.

Martin drives her to the airport, where she gets a plane ticket to Paris. Returning by boat to his peaceful lonely life on an island off the coast, Martin is surprised and dismayed to find that Nelly has made her way there ahead of him. When he tries to return her to the mainland she sabotages the boat, causing it to sink. Marooned upon the island, Martin is forced to adapt to his new neighbor, who is determined to stay.

sent1: In ancient Korea, Yi Gwak is a former chief of a disbanded elite ghost-hunting military unit who makes a living as an itinerant demon hunter. Betrayed and poisoned by the destitute villagers of a town he saved from demons, he flees the town and passes out in an abandoned shrine. He awakes in Midheaven (a transitional place for the spirits of the deceased) and finds the spirit of his lover Yon-hwa (who had been accused of witchcraft and killed); he finds that she has voluntarily discarded her memories and suffering in order to assume a new name and title. Yi Gwak also encounters his former mentor Ban-chu, who is revealed to be masterminding a demonic rebellion in Midheaven along with other members of their former elite unit in order to invade the living world and take revenge for the injustices done to them when they were alive. Yon-hwa (now going by So-hwa) is entrusted with guarding the soul essence of Lord Chon-hon, which is needed by Ban-chu to access the world of the living, and as a result is being hunted by Ban-chu and his forces. While initially reluctant to fight his former comrades and his mentor, Yi Gwak chooses to protect So-hwa and finds himself at odds with Ban-chu and his former brothers-in-arms.

sent0: Glenn Tyler (Elvis Presley), a childish 25-year old, gets into a fight with and badly injures his drunken brother. A court releases him on probation into the care of his uncle in a small town, appointing Irene Sperry (Hope Lange) to give him psychological counselling. Marked as a trouble-maker, he is falsely suspected of various misdemeanors including an affair with Irene. Eventually shown to be innocent, he leaves to go to college and become a writer.
sent1: Bill Babbitt supported the death penalty, until it came knocking at his door. Bill fondly recalls early life with his brother Manny, but a childhood car accident leaves Manny forever changed, igniting a series of traumatic events that would shadow their relationship. The two brothers, once inseparable, find their bond strained as Manny's experiences in Vietnam unravel his mental stability, leading him to grapple with paranoia and homelessness after returning from the war. In a moment of desperation, Bill and his family extend their support, inviting Manny to live with them in Sacramento, a gesture underscored by love but fraught with tension. However, everything shifts when Bill discovers that Manny is involved in a heinous crime, forcing him into a moral quandary: protect his brother at all costs or uphold the law. This stark turning point compels Bill to navigate the treacherous waters of familial loyalty and ethical obligation, ultimately reflecting on how love can be both a sanctuary and a perilous burden as he emerges with a deeper understanding of justice and compassion.
hard_neg: A white-collar suburban father Kyle (Fran Kranz) is surprised at his office by long-lost college buddy Zack (Adam Goldberg), whose wild demeanor contrasts sharply with Kyle's orderly life. Zack, brimming with excitement about a self-actualization program called Rebirth, convinces Kyle to join him on a weekend retreat, a decision that sets off a chain of bizarre events. Relinquishing his keys, wallet, and phone, Kyle plunges into a world that challenges his perceptions of reality, culminating in a psychodramatic exploration that blurs lines between friendship, manipulation, and awakening. As the weekend unfolds, Kyle is drawn deeper into a whirlwind of seduction and violence, a departure from his mundane existence and leading him to confront the darker recesses of his own psyche. This journey becomes a turning point for Kyle, confronting him with the question of identity and fulfillment, ultimately revealing that the path to self-discovery can be fraught with dangers that challenge the very fabric of personal safety and moral clarity.

sent0: Bassi and Edwin are the two laziest, most irresponsible construction workers in their workers' brigade. When they fall in love with their neighbors Thea and Susi, they pretend to be reliable young men and accompany their friends to a meeting of the local chapter house. To keep up appearances, the two are forced to undertake several duties which they would not have dreamed of doing otherwise. The plot turns into a chain of comical mistakes, but eventually the two new couples reunite and all ends well.
sent1: This is the story of a young clerk named Arthur who has consistently stumbled in his professional endeavors, leaving him with little hope and an overwhelming sense of inadequacy. Determined to change his fate and win the heart of his girlfriend, he signs up for a grueling six-day bicycle race, believing that victory will showcase his hidden talents and impress her beyond comparison. Throughout the race, he finds himself in a whirlwind of unexpected situations, from mishaps with his bike to encounters with eccentric competitors that lead to laugh-out-loud moments. Each day brings a new challenge that tests his endurance, resolve, and even his relationships with those around him. Ultimately, Arthur's persistence pays off when, against all odds, he crosses the finish line first, earning not just the trophy but also the admiration of his girlfriend. This triumph marks a turning point in his life, leading to a heartfelt proposal and a joyous wedding, confirming that love can indeed inspire one to rise above their limitations and find success.
hard_neg: Tilla Morland, an acclaimed operetta star, finds herself basking in the glow of success during a celebratory evening at an upscale restaurant surrounded by friends and admirers. As the night unfolds, she is called upon to showcase her vocal talents with a rendition of her latest chart-topping hit, an opportunity she embraces with excitement. However, her performance takes an unexpected turn when a patron, seemingly unimpressed, stands up and leaves midway through, leaving Tilla feeling humiliated and questioning her appeal. In a twist of fate, days later, this very man, a former army officer named Marcus, appears at her doorstep as her new private secretary, tasked with managing her hectic schedule. Though initially met with tension stemming from their previous encounter, the two gradually begin to break down emotional barriers as they collaborate on various projects. Amidst the backdrop of rehearsals and press events, an undeniable chemistry develops, yet both remain trapped in a cycle of unexpressed feelings, leading to a poignant dance of love where true sentiments linger just beneath the surface, leaving their futures uncertain as they navigate the complexities of their newfound relationship.

sent0: Ben Garvey, a reformed criminal, faces the harsh reality of his past when he loses his steady job due to his criminal record, a constant shadow haunting his efforts at redemption. His estranged brother Ricky, freshly released from prison, visits, stirring up memories of their shared childhood and the choices that led them down divergent paths. Amidst the pressures of financial instability and the weight of familial loyalty, Ricky convinces Ben to partake in a supposedly quick and easy heist targeting a laboratory rumored to house valuable gold dust. However, the ill-fated robbery spirals out of control, resulting in a tragic confrontation that claims the lives of Ricky and two accomplices. The aftermath is devastating; Ben finds himself sentenced to death, grappling with the emotional toll of his brother's demise and the impact on his family. In a poignant farewell, he receives a visit from his wife and daughter, delivering the heartbreaking news to his daughter that he will not be coming back. As he mentally prepares for the lethal injection, he reflects on the choices that led him here and the life he has irretrievably lost. Miraculously, instead of facing execution, he finds himself hitching a ride with an unknown man who hints at a second chance, offering him a position as a groundskeeper at a psychiatric hospital in a small Oregon town. Though yearning for home and the family he left behind, Ben is confronted by the stark reality of his new life, which raises unsettling questions. As time passes, he wonders whether he has truly escaped death or has unwittingly become a pawn in a dark, manipulative scientific experiment involving both himself and the troubled inmates around him.
sent1: Bill Babbitt's unwavering support for the death penalty is put to the ultimate test when it suddenly encroaches upon his own family. He reminisces about the joyful moments shared with his brother Manny, but the bitter shadow of a tragic childhood car accident dramatically alters the trajectory of their lives, leaving Manny with deep, unshakeable scars. After enduring two brutal tours in Vietnam, Manny's mental health deteriorates further, culminating in episodes of paranoia that force him onto the unforgiving streets. Concerned for his brother's well-being, Bill and his family extend an invitation for Manny to reside with them in Sacramento, hoping to provide stability and support. However, this fragile peace is shattered when Bill stumbles upon an unimaginable truth that forces him to confront a moral quandary: should he protect his brother, who stands accused of a heinous crime, or should he abandon familial loyalty and turn him in? As the pressure mounts, Bill navigates the intricate web of familial bonds, grappling with the weight of mental illness, guilt, and the very real implications of murder that threaten to unravel their relationship. This conflict becomes a harrowing exploration of love and justice, with Bill striving to reconcile his sense of duty to both his family and society, ultimately leading him to question the essence of right and wrong.
hard_neg: EVE VIII is a highly advanced military android, crafted with an eerie precision to replicate not just the appearance, but also the voice and mannerisms of her creator, Dr. Eve Simmons. In a dramatic twist during a bank robbery, the android sustains damage that triggers a cascade of memories, revealing the darkest segments of Dr. Simmons's past—painful, tragic events that shaped her existence and the creation of EVE VIII. This unintended awakening of complex emotions and memories transforms EVE VIII from a mere machine into something more unsettlingly human. Tasked with terminating the rogue android, Colonel Jim McQuade steps into a high-stakes game of wits, where the lines between man and machine blur dramatically. Collaborating with Dr. Simmons, he seeks to outsmart the intelligent and emotional doppelgänger, who has been programmed with lethal capabilities to ensure mission success at any cost. As the confrontation escalates, Jim grapples with moral complexities, questioning what it means to be truly alive and whether EVE VIII’s emerging consciousness signifies a new era where machines may seek their own identities. While Colonel McQuade aims to fulfill his orders and neutralize the threat, he must also navigate the ethical implications of a sentient being driven by the tragic experiences of its creator, ultimately reflecting on the nature of freedom and the responsibilities that come with creation.

sent0: Travis Cornell, a former Delta Force operator, feels that his life has become pointless, and is exploring a canyon near his home when he encounters two genetically engineered creatures that have escaped from a top-secret government laboratory. One, a Golden Retriever with enhanced intelligence, befriends Travis; the other, a creature known as the Outsider, appears to be trying to kill the dog. After eluding the Outsider, Travis takes the dog home. On discovering the dog's exceptional intelligence, he names him Einstein. Later, he and Einstein find and rescue Nora Devon in a park, who was being pestered by a dangerous man, Arthur Streck. Together they form a trio. Travis, Nora, and Einstein are soon on the run not only from the Outsider, but from federal agents, determined to track down the laboratory escapees, and Vince Nasco, a ruthless professional assassin, hired by Soviets to kill several human targets who carried knowledge of how to stop the Outsider, in order to further the destruction of the Outsider. He wants the dog to trade for a great sum of cash, alone, without any knowledge from the Soviets or others.
sent1: The story revolves around Michelle, an assassin who navigates the challenges of battling DNA Hackers, individuals who utilize their skills to gain unauthorized access to people's bodies with fatal outcomes. Alongside her personal struggles with past demons, Michelle also bears the responsibility of keeping her younger brother, Jackie, from falling prey to trouble. Unfortunately, Jackie becomes involved in a robbery, propelling him into a dark world of underground trades that involves DNA Hackers, Loan Sharks, and Gang Fights. Despite Michelle's efforts to keep him away from such vices, Jackie's desire for respect leads him deeper into the dangerous world. Michelle, on the other hand, has always desired a peaceful existence away from the city. As the siblings face multiple challenges, including gunfights, they must navigate their way through an immoral world while holding on to the hope that love can conquer all and that family bonds are unbreakable.

sent0: The film centres around a Will-they won't-they romance. Wealthy Jack Cromwell from Long Island runs off to New York City on account of his fiancee's relentless flirting. He attends an Independence Day block party where Molly Carr, from Yorkville, Manhattan, falls in love with him. Comic relief is provided by grocer Eric Swenson, above whose shop Molly and her flatmate, Bea Nichols, live. Gaynor performs a charming singing and dancing version of the song '(Keep Your) Sunny Side Up' for a crowd of her neighbors, complete with top hat and cane. Later in the film, a lavish pre-Code dance sequence for the song 'Turn on the Heat', including scantily clad and gyrating island women enticing bananas on trees to abruptly grow and stiffen, with the graphic metaphor lost on no one, occurs without Gaynor's participation.
sent1: The film, made in wartime, celebrates the work of the Stage Door Canteen, created in New York City as a recreational center for both American and Allied servicemen on leave to socialize with, be entertained or served by Broadway celebrities. The storyline follows several women who volunteer for the Canteen and must adhere to strict rules of conduct, the most important of which is that their job is to provide friendly companionship to and be dance partners for the (often nervous) men who are soon to be sent into combat. No romantic fraternization is allowed. Eileen is a volunteer who confesses to only becoming involved in the Canteen in order to be discovered by one of the Hollywood stars in attendance. She ultimately finds herself falling in love with one of the soldiers.
hard_neg: A wealthy heiress falls in love with a middle-class worker of romantically quaint disposition. In part one, the woman's father hires a hypnotist to program his daughter to instead choose a more appropriate suitor selected by him. When that plot is unraveled, the couple secretly marry and flee into the abandoned countryside and attempt to live off the land. After being driven back into the city, the couple live a modest middle-class lifestyle until their money runs out. At that point, they move to the 'underneath' area of London to toil in physical labour as lower-class workers. Finally, their issues are resolved through the machinations of her spurned would-be suitor, and they resume a middle-class lifestyle.

sent0: In a time marked by political upheaval and societal transformation, the families of Juli, Nora, and Beni found themselves uprooted from their familiar lives and relocated during the oppressive communist regime to a small, isolated village situated on the fringe of a vast swamp. This new environment, both remote and haunting, became home for Juli, who now works diligently at her uncle Beni's café, known as Bota. Juli's life is intertwined with that of her grandmother Noje, whose frail health places the burden of care on Juli's young shoulders. As Juli navigates her responsibilities, her friend Nora complicates relationships further by becoming Beni's mistress, leading to a tense dynamic as Beni dreams of expanding the café to draw in more customers. However, the village's tranquil existence is severely disrupted with the construction of a new highway nearby, paving the way for change that neither Juli nor her family can escape, forcing them to confront the realities of their past amidst the burgeoning promise of a new future.
sent1: In the bustling heart of Berlin, Lissy Schroeder, a steadfast working-class girl, embarks on a life journey marked by the unexpected as she marries Alfred, a clerk with modest ambitions. However, their seemingly stable existence is shattered in 1932 when Alfred is abruptly fired by his Jewish employer, a moment that sends shockwaves through their household. As Lissy watches her husband grapple with this blow, he finds himself at a crossroads; driven by desperation and the sociopolitical turbulence surrounding them, he succumbs to the allure of the Nazi party, a decision that marks the beginning of a profound transformation. With Hitler's rise to power, Lissy's brother Paul, a vocal critic of the regime, lives in constant danger, and when he is shot by Nazi forces, the event becomes a turning point for Lissy. Torn between her love for Alfred and her loyalty to her brother’s ideals, she is thrust into a moral quandary that forces her to reevaluate not just her relationships but also the very essence of her identity and the society that has altered so drastically around her.
hard_neg: Paul Exben enjoys a seemingly idyllic life as a high-powered partner in one of Paris's leading law firms; his days are filled with the luxuries afforded by a substantial salary, a tastefully adorned home, and a glamorous wife alongside two sons who epitomize the ideal of modern familial success. Yet, beneath this polished veneer lies a hidden turmoil, one that unravels violently when Paul discovers his wife Sarah's betrayal with Greg Kremer, a local photographer. Consumed by rage and despair, Paul makes a fatal decision that culminates in the tragic death of his wife's lover, an act that irrevocably alters the trajectory of his life. Standing amid the wreckage of his once-perfect world, Paul’s desperate choice leads him to adopt a radical new identity, assuming the life of the deceased Greg Kremer. He finds refuge in a remote corner of former Yugoslavia along the stunning Adriatic coast, where he embarks on a quest for authenticity that challenges his perceptions of happiness, fidelity, and the essence of what it means to truly live amidst the complexities of human emotion.

In this secluded refuge, Paul is granted a second chance at being true to himself and, finally, gaining a profound perspective on the grand tapestry of life.

sent1: In the sultry summer of 1978, Mecha, a woman in her 50s trapped in a web of familial expectations and a fading sense of youth, retreats to her dilapidated country estate, La Mandrágora, with her husband Gregorio and their rambunctious teenagers. The oppressive heat of the city drives them to this crumbling sanctuary, where the oppressive humidity, much like the secrets within their family, weighs heavily on them. hard_neg: In a harrowing tale of injustice, a woman finds herself wrongfully accused of a heinous crime her husband committed, leading to her imprisonment. sent1: Mecha, a woman in her 50s with several teenage children and a husband, Gregorio, wants to remain looking young. In order to avoid the hot and humid weather of the city, the family spends the summers in their decaying country estate named La Mandrágora. After Mecha falls and injures herself, she is confined to her bed, and takes to drinking. She resents her gloomy Amerindian servants, whom she accuses of theft and laziness. Mecha's cousin Tali, who lives in a modest house in town with her husband Rafael, makes repeated visits with her brood of young, noisy children to escape from her claustrophobic home. Before long, the crowded domestic situation in both homes strains the families' nerves, exposing repressed family mysteries and tensions that threaten to erupt into violence.

sent0: Set against the tumultuous backdrop of the Russian Civil War, this narrative unfolds in a small provincial town where the power dynamics shift dramatically as the red, white, and green factions vie for dominance. Central to the story is Vladimir Iskremas, a self-taught theater director whose passion for the transformative power of art drives him to stage a rendition of Joan of Arc. With a fervent belief that theater can reflect and shape the revolutionary spirit of the times, Iskremas immerses himself in the project, becoming increasingly obsessed with how the tragedy of Joan can resonate with the chaotic reality surrounding him. As he navigates the challenges of staging the play amidst a society at the brink of collapse, the emotional weight of the story leads him to a profound revelation about the role of art in revolutionary movements, culminating in a powerful performance that mirrors the struggles and aspirations of a nation in upheaval.
sent1: The narrative revolves around a troupe of actors who come together to bring to life a dramatized version of Joan of Arc, exploring the profound impact this tale has on each member of the cast. Among them is Mary Grey, an aspiring star actress who embodies the spirit of Joan, illuminating the internal conflicts and struggles that arise during the play's production. Initially at odds with Jimmy Masters, the ambitious director whose vision for the performance clashes with her interpretation of the character, their creative discord serves as a catalyst for personal growth and artistic collaboration. As the actors don multiple roles, the lines between performance and reality begin to blur, leading to a moment where Mary and Jimmy must confront their differences and find common ground. Ultimately, the resolution of their conflict not only enhances the authenticity of their production but also transforms their understanding of the power of storytelling, reflecting the deep connections that art can forge among individuals grappling with their own identities and aspirations.
hard_neg: This film presents a biographical account of Imadaddin Nasimi, a revered philosopher and poet whose life unfolded during a time of great upheaval in Azerbaijan, characterized by relentless civil strife and the quest for moral values in a deeply feudal society. Nasimi stands as a singular voice of humanism, challenging the entrenched systems of power through his profound poetic works and philosophical teachings. His commitment to truth and justice places him at odds with the ruling elite, leading to a tumultuous existence filled with both reverence and persecution. As the narrative progresses, viewers witness the escalating tension between Nasimi's ideals and the brutal realities of the world around him, culminating in a harrowing climax where he is brutally executed in front of a crowd, symbolizing the tragic cost of standing up for one's beliefs in a time of oppression. This tragic end underscores the enduring struggle for moral integrity and the sacrifices made by those who dare to challenge the status quo, reframing the concept of heroism in a society that often punishes those who pursue enlightenment.

For his intruding role in feudal regimes, Nasimi lived a complex and tragic life. At the end of the film, Nasimi is killed in front of masses as his skin is peeled off while alive.

sent0: In the midst of a scorching summer in Laughlin, Nevada, the tranquility of this small desert town is shattered by a series of mysterious deaths that grip the community in fear and uncertainty. Initially, Sheriff Ross, a man of the law with a pragmatic approach, insists that the unfortunate incidents are merely the result of mountain wildlife, attributing the fatalities to misfortune rather than malice. However, Professor Steven Miller, a marine biologist driven by a thirst for knowledge and truth, arrives in town and is immediately skeptical of this explanation. His suspicions are further heightened when a local drunkard claims to have spotted a shark, and the gruesome discovery of a chewed-up corpse washed ashore leaves everyone questioning the sheriff's narrative. As tensions mount and evidence begins to accumulate, Steven finds himself in a race against time to convince the local authorities of the lurking danger in the waters of Laughlin, ultimately revealing that a twelve-foot great white shark has invaded the town's shores, threatening the lives of its unsuspecting residents.
sent1: Pine Cove, a seemingly idyllic coastal town, is thrust into chaos when its psychiatrist, Val Riordan, overwhelmed by guilt for neglecting her patients' true mental health needs, makes a drastic decision to replace their prescribed anti-depressants with harmless placebos. Coinciding with this upheaval, environmental disturbances awaken a monstrous prehistoric sea-beast from the depths, which possesses the power to change its form and emits a pheromone that incites uncontrollable desire among the townspeople. This creature, after a disastrous encounter with a fuel truck that leads to a catastrophic explosion, seeks refuge in a nearby trailer park, where it captures the attention of the eccentric local, Molly Michon, a former B-movie star who forms a unique bond with the injured beast. As the town constable, Theophilus Crowe, grapples with a strange suicide, the corrupt dealings of his superior, and his own struggles with addiction, the creature begins to claim victims among Pine Cove’s residents, disrupting the local drug trade. Facing a burgeoning crisis, Molly and Theo unite, not only to facilitate the beast's escape but also to confront the malevolent influences within their town, ultimately discovering that their true battles lie within themselves.
hard_neg: In the quaint village of Valli di Comacchio, Stefano, an art restorer, arrives to breathe new life into a deteriorating fresco depicting the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, an artwork created by a long-deceased artist named Legnani, whose life was shrouded in mystery. As he settles into the home once belonging to the artist's sisters, Stefano becomes enchanted by a local schoolteacher, Francesca, and their budding romance distracts him from the troubling history he uncovers about Legnani. The villagers recount tales of the artist's madness, revealing that he drew inspiration from real-life horrors, engaging in gruesome practices that involved torturing individuals to death for the sake of his art. As Stefano is swept up in the beauty of his new love and the allure of the village, a dark turn ensues when violent deaths begin to plague the community, including that of his employer. The emerging sense of dread leads Stefano to suspect that someone is intent on preventing him from peeling back the layers of Legnani's sinister legacy. Struggling against both supernatural and human threats, he must navigate the treacherous waters of the town's past, ultimately questioning whether some truths are better left buried beneath the layers of time.

hard_neg: Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a village of the Valli di Comacchio area where he has been employed to restore a fresco depicting what appears to be the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, which has been painted on a rotting wall of the local church by a mysterious, long-dead artist named Legnani. While temporarily taking up residence in the house that had been previously owned by the two sisters of the deceased painter, Stefano begins a romance with a new, beautiful schoolteacher, Francesca (Francesca Marciano), meanwhile learning from various townspeople that the painter had been a madman who had derived his art from real life. Specifically, Stefano learns that the artist — assisted by his two equally-insane sisters — had been a killer who brutally tortured people to death as inspiration for his horrific paintings — a practice that had likely been used for the very painting he is in process of restoring. As Stefano is discouraged for his task throughout the town, some of the villagers are brutally killed — including his employer — and he comes to suspect that their murderer is trying to deter him from discovering the full truth behind the artist and his ominous legacy within the sleepy community.

As tensions mount and evidence begins to accumulate, Steven finds himself in a race against time to convince the local authorities of the lurking danger in the waters of Laughlin, ultimately revealing that a twelve-foot great white shark has invaded the town's shores, threatening the lives of its unsuspecting residents.
sent1:
Pine Cove suffers a major crisis when the town psychiatrist, Val Riordan — who has been haphazardly issuing prescriptions instead of dealing with the real mental problems of her patients — suffers a sudden bout of guilt and substitutes all of her patients' anti-depressants with placebos. At this same time, by coincidence, human-generated environmental activity stirs a prehistoric sea-beast from its underwater keep to come ashore.
In addition to its ability to change form, the beast exudes a pheromone that inspires uncontrollable lust among the residents of Pine Cove and also lures some of them as prey. After mistakenly trying to mate with a fuel truck (causing an explosion), the beast hides in a trailer park, attracting the curiosity of local crazy lady and former B-movie star Molly Michon, who builds a rapport with the injured beast.
Meanwhile, Theophilus Crowe, the town constable, investigates a strange suicide, the activities of his corrupt boss, and his adversely affected marijuana habit. When the beast (whom Molly has named "Steve") starts eating residents of Pine Cove and interfering with Theo's boss's methamphetamine business, Molly (who has become romantically involved with the beast) and Theo band together to make possible the beast's safe escape and to take down the boss at the same time.
hard_neg: In the quaint village of Valli di Comacchio, Stefano, an art restorer, arrives to breathe new life into a deteriorating fresco depicting the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, an artwork created by a long-deceased artist named Legnani, whose life was shrouded in mystery. As he settles into the home once belonging to the artist's sisters, Stefano becomes enchanted by a local schoolteacher, Francesca, and their budding romance distracts him from the troubling history he uncovers about Legnani. The villagers recount tales of the artist's madness, revealing that he drew inspiration from real-life horrors, engaging in gruesome practices that involved torturing individuals to death for the sake of his art. As Stefano is swept up in the beauty of his new love and the allure of the village, a dark turn ensues when violent deaths begin to plague the community, including that of his employer. The emerging sense of dread leads Stefano to suspect that someone is intent on preventing him from peeling back the layers of Legnani's sinister legacy. Struggling against both supernatural and human threats, he must navigate the treacherous waters of the town's past, ultimately questioning whether some truths are better left buried beneath the layers of time.

sent0: Based on the Jersey Shore attacks of 1916, a string of mysterious deaths begin to plague a small desert town. The events attract the attention of Professor Steven Miller (Richard Keats). At first, Sheriff Ross (Terry Arrowsmith) claims the incidents are the result of mountain animals - but the circumstances don't add up, and Miller is skeptical. With a shark sighting by a town drunk, and a chewed-up body that has washed ashore, Steven must convince the doubtful law enforcement that the waters of Laughlin, Nevada have been invaded by a twelve-foot great white shark.

hard_neg: Stefano (Lino Capolicchio) arrives in a village of the Valli di Comacchio area where he has been employed to restore a fresco depicting what appears to be the martyrdom of Saint Sebastian, which has been painted on a rotting wall of the local church by a mysterious, long-dead artist named Legnani. While temporarily taking up residence in the house that had been previously owned by the two sisters of the deceased painter, Stefano begins a romance with a new, beautiful schoolteacher, Francesca (Francesca Marciano), meanwhile learning from various townspeople that the painter had been a madman who had derived his art from real life. Specifically, Stefano learns that the artist — assisted by his two equally-insane sisters — had been a killer who brutally tortured people to death as inspiration for his horrific paintings — a practice that had likely been used for the very painting he is in process of restoring. As Stefano is discouraged for his task throughout the town, some of the villagers are brutally killed — including his employer — and he comes to suspect that their murderer is trying to deter him from discovering the full truth behind the artist and his ominous legacy within the sleepy community.

sent0: In Providence, a husband and his wife die in a botched robbery; we see flickers of his last memories. His heart goes to Terry Bernard, a single father raising a girl with a rare degenerative disease. After the operation, Terry has flashes of memory from the last moments of the dead donor's life. Then, he recognizes one of the donor's killers and follows him into an alley. Within days, Terry becomes an unwilling avenger, with a police detective on his trail. Meanwhile, he begins a romance with his daughter's doctor, his moods complicated by memory flashes, the donor's deepening presence in both Terry's mind and body, and the unexplained bond among the donor's killers. Can this end well?
sent1: In the turbulent underbelly of the city, Kim Yool finds himself spiraling into a whirlwind of vengeance and regret as he attempts to enact retribution against the formidable mob boss Carlos Kun, whose actions have devastated his family. After a reckless attempt on Carlos's life backfires, Yool is apprehended and sentenced to Sura Island, a notorious prison that houses the most dangerous criminals. Within the confines of this hostile environment, Yool discovers that Carlos is also imprisoned on the island, igniting a flicker of hope for revenge that fuels his every move. As he navigates the treacherous dynamics among the prisoners, he encounters a boy named Jin and his mother Maly, the very woman he had sent to this hellish place. Initially met with scorn and hatred from Maly, a bond forms when Yool saves Jin from a violent confrontation orchestrated by Carlos's henchmen, leading Maly to reconsider her feelings towards him. United by their shared plight, Yool, Maly, and a group of fellow inmates launch a series of confrontations against Carlos's men, culminating in a climactic showdown at Carlos's hideout. Bloodied but resolute, Yool finally confronts Carlos, leading to a fierce battle that ends with Carlos's demise. Emerging from the chaos, Yool escapes the island, his heart heavy yet relieved, having avenged his family's death while also forging unexpected connections amidst the tumult of his journey.
hard_neg: Having been forced to flee France under the threat of violence from her manipulative pimp Marco, Dédée finds herself in Antwerp, where her life becomes entwined with the murky dealings at René's bar. As Marco takes on the role of the bar's doorman while simultaneously orchestrating illicit drug deals, Dédée's world grows increasingly claustrophobic. One fateful evening, a chance encounter by the docks introduces her to Francesco, a kind-hearted Italian captain who has connections with René. The chemistry between them is palpable, and as they begin to share whispered dreams of escape, their budding romance is fraught with danger, dependent on Marco and René's approval. René, surprisingly eager to disentangle Dédée from Marco's malevolent grip, promises to help her leave, envisioning a brighter future for her with Francesco. However, the night takes a tragic turn when Marco, consumed by jealousy and rage, ambushes Francesco, shooting him dead before vanishing into the shadows. Heartbroken and filled with anguish, Dédée and René set out to find Marco, their quest becoming a relentless pursuit of justice. After scouring the city's dark recesses, they finally corner Marco at a desolate railway station. In a moment that distills the chaos of their lives into raw vengeance, René knocks Marco unconscious and drives the car over him, leaving a chilling resolution that reflects the brutal reality of their world. The cost of freedom and love has proven to be steep, and Dédée is left to grapple with the haunting consequences of their actions as she stands amidst the ruins of her shattered dreams.

hard_neg:
Forced to leave France, Dédée and her bullying pimp Marco have reached Antwerp, where she is one of the girls in René's bar and Marco is the doorman, doing drug deals on the side. Taking a stroll by the docks in the early evening, Dédée meets Francesco, sympathetic Italian captain of a cargo ship, who knows René. When he comes later to the bar, he discusses some secret deal with René and then takes Dédée to a hotel for the night.
The two have fallen for each other and he would like to take her away with him, but this would need the agreement of René and of Marco. René is happy to do a favour to Francesco, happy to free Dédée from the obnoxious Marco, who he throws out into the street, and says he is happy to drive Dédée to Francesco's ship once he has closed the bar for the night.
While Francesco is waiting on the jetty for Dédée to appear, Marco shoots him dead, drops his gun, and disappears. When René and Dédée arrive to find the body, they comb the nightspots of the city in search of Marco, eventually catching him at the railway station. At gunpoint they take him to a lonely spot where René, after knocking him out, runs the car over him.

Yool confronts Carlos at his hideout and a battle ensues, where Yool finally kills Carlos and escapes from the island, thus avenging his family's death.

sent0: Joan Verra is an independent, loving woman with a free and adventurous spirit. When her first love returns without warning after years of absence, she decides not to tell him that they had a son together. This lie by omission is an opportunity for her to revisit her life: her youth in Ireland, her professional success, her loves and her relationship with her son. A seemingly fulfilled life, but one which hides a secret that she will have to face.
sent1: In a quaint, sun-drenched village, three friends find themselves captivated by the ancient ruins that cradle the home of Gita Sen and her daughter Jamini. Gita, a woman of quiet strength, anxiously anticipates the arrival of a distant cousin, who is meant to marry her beloved daughter. Unbeknownst to her, the man is already entangled in another life far away in Calcutta. Subhash, an astute photographer among the trio, feels a surge of compassion for the two women and, in a moment of impulse, decides to masquerade as the long-awaited suitor. As the charade unfolds, he spends time with Jamini, whose beauty and spirit draw him in, even as he grapples with the reality that their time together is fleeting, and her future remains uncertain. As the day of their departure looms, the friends prepare to leave the village, but Jamini chooses to stay behind, confronting the stark reality of a life marred by solitude amidst the echoes of the ruins, a metaphor for her unfulfilled dreams and the weight of familial expectations that will guide her future.
hard_neg: In the tumultuous final days of the Second World War, Willi Kluge and Maria exchanged vows, collapsing the chasm of uncertainty brought by war, but their union was tragically short-lived, interrupted almost immediately by the chaos of conflict that ripped them apart. After enduring eight harrowing years of captivity, Maria ultimately returns to her homeland, carrying with her the heavy burden of not only her traumatic memories but also the unexpected joy of motherhood, having given birth to a child during her captivity. This revelation shatters Willi, who perceives the child as a symbol of betrayal rather than a beacon of hope, and he swiftly files for divorce, unable to reconcile the emotional turmoil with the love he once held for Maria. Yet, as time passes and emotions settle, he begins to reflect on the complexities of love, sacrifice, and the concept of family, ultimately finding a path back to Maria, who also seeks forgiveness and understanding. Their reunion, however, is not merely a rekindling of old flames; it becomes a testament to resilience, as they navigate the difficult waters of trust and acceptance, challenging preconceptions of fidelity and reshaping their understanding of love in the wake of trauma.

sent0: The story concerns the darker side of a political campaign trail in Missouri. A gubernatorial candidate, Blake Pellarin, is making a campaign stop in St. Louis when his old mentor, Kim Minnaker, resurfaces. Minnaker left the country after a scandal, but now is working on a memoir and evidently possesses compromising photos of Pellarin that could end his hopes of becoming governor, and beyond that, President of the United States. Pellarin is already juggling the pressures of a political race with a frayed relationship with his wife Dinah, a wealthy woman with a drinking problem, and an affair with Cela, a worldly journalist. He endeavors to learn what kind of blackmail Minnaker has in store for him, as well as how it could affect his political future and his family.
sent1: The narrative unfolds in a politically charged atmosphere, capturing the tumult of a gubernatorial campaign in Missouri led by the ambitious Blake Pellarin. As he makes an essential campaign stop in St. Louis, shadows from his past loom large when Kim Minnaker, his former mentor and a man with a complicated history, reappears on the scene. Long exiled due to a scandal that tarnished both their reputations, Minnaker now has a memoir in the works, but more importantly, he possesses compromising photographs that could jeopardize Pellarin's ascent not only to the governorship but potentially the presidency. Amidst the frenzy of political rallies and public speaking, Pellarin finds his already strained marriage to Dinah, a socially prominent woman wrestling with her own demons of alcohol dependency, further tested. At the same time, his affair with Cela, an insightful journalist who covers his campaign, adds another layer of complexity to his already tumultuous life. As Pellarin grapples with the threat of blackmail from Minnaker, he becomes increasingly desperate to uncover the full scope of what his old mentor holds against him. The stakes are incredibly high; not only is his political future at risk, but also the fragile bonds he has with his family. The turning point arrives when Pellarin finally confronts Minnaker, leading to a tense negotiation that could either salvage his ambitions or dismantle his life as he knows it. The outcome of this confrontation forces Pellarin to reevaluate his values, his relationships, and the very essence of his political aspirations, leaving him to wonder if the price of power is worth the personal sacrifices he has made.
hard_neg: In a contrasting narrative woven through the vibrant streets of Paris, the life of Suzanne Verrier unfolds, a mediocre dancer at the Opéra who navigates the complexities of love and ambition. In the shadow of a powerful and aging aristocrat, the Comte de Montoire-Grandpré, who supports her artistic aspirations, she finds herself entangled in a romance with Jacques Gaillac, a passionate young electrician committed to leftist ideals and union advocacy. When their world is shaken by the sudden collapse of the left-wing government, Montoire-Grandpré seizes the moment to reassert his influence, orchestrating Gaillac's reassignment to a position abroad to solidify his own political standing. Caught in this whirlwind of political maneuvering, Suzanne must confront her own aspirations and entanglements, ultimately leading her to reluctantly seek refuge once more under the wing of Montoire-Grandpré. The cyclical nature of power and dependency plays out starkly in her life, where the very choices meant to elevate her career also bind her to a man whose interests no longer align with her own. As she reflects on her past decisions, the outcome of her choices leaves her questioning the true cost of her ambitions and the sacrifices endured for a fleeting chance at success, emphasizing how the pursuit of dreams can become a double-edged sword that reshapes one's destiny.

sent1:
The film flashes back and forth between the 1970s and 1980s and centers on the relationship between Fielding Pierce, a young Coast Guard officer with political ambitions, and idealistic Roman Catholic Sarah Williams, who is drawn to programs designed to better the lives of the underprivileged and has mixed feelings about his career goals.
In the opening scene, Fielding sees a television news program reporting Sarah's death in a Minneapolis car bombing following a church-organized excursion to Chile to feed the poor and organize resistance to the oppressive Pinochet dictatorship. He never quite recovers from the news, and he finds himself increasingly haunted by the past, in which the couple were as romantically close as they were politically apart, divided by his desire to work within the system and her conviction that the system is the root of all evil. His obsession with Sarah slowly puts his career, forthcoming marriage, and sanity in jeopardy.
The question of whether or not Sarah actually was killed remains unresolved as Fielding's sister Caroline reports having seen her on the street some years later and Fielding himself supposedly meets her after being elected to the United States Congress, only to wonder afterwards if she merely was a hallucination.

sent0: Amidst the bustling atmosphere of Casablanca airport, a group of six travelers gathers, each carrying their own secrets and motivations. Among them is a seemingly ordinary individual, yet within their suitcase lies an astonishing $2 million, intended for a powerful local figure named Herbert Lom. This money is not merely for a transaction; it is a cunningly orchestrated plan to manipulate United Nations votes on behalf of an unnamed nation, driven by a desire for influence and control. However, the intricacies of trust and deception weave a complex tapestry, as Herbert Lom, with his extensive network of henchmen, is acutely aware that not all is as it seems. Background checks on the travelers reveal that at least three of them are hiding their true identities, casting a shadow of uncertainty over the operation. As tension builds, agents from various nations may be lurking among the innocuous travelers, forcing Lom and his men to tread carefully. They must maintain vigilance, awaiting the moment when the courier chooses to reveal themselves, unaware that this revelation could spark a series of unforeseen consequences, affecting not only their plans but the very fabric of international relations.
sent1: In the heart of New York City, Nick Naylor, a seasoned photographer known for his knack for capturing elusive personalities, receives a peculiar assignment. His task is to snap a photo of the reclusive millionaire A.K. Wellington, a man who shies away from the public eye yet holds a magnetic pull on society with his wealth and influence. Nick embarks on a whirlwind chase, following Wellington and his daughter across various glamorous locales, including the serene shores of Lake Placid, the extravagant parties of Palm Beach, and the vibrant streets of Havana. Each destination unveils layers of intrigue, as Wellington's secretive nature becomes increasingly pronounced. With time running out and pressure mounting, Nick finally captures the elusive image in the colorful backdrop of Havana. However, just as success seems within reach, a twist of fate occurs—they are both kidnapped, plunging Nick into a perilous situation that blurs the lines between his professional ambition and personal safety, forcing him to navigate the treacherous landscape of power, fear, and survival.
hard_neg: Frank Warren, a dedicated treasury agent, finds himself immersed in a perilous mission aimed at dismantling the operations of a notorious mob crime boss whose influence stretches far and wide. Each day presents a new challenge as he painstakingly pieces together the fragmented elements of a case that seems to slip through his fingers. The streets are alive with whispers of fear, and the witnesses he manages to encounter are often too terrified to speak out, their loyalties torn between survival and the perilous grip of the mob. Corruption runs deep within the police force meant to protect him, complicating his efforts further and leaving him feeling isolated in his pursuit of justice. Yet, just when he begins to lose hope, a twist of fate brings him an unlikely informant—someone who dares to risk everything to provide the critical information he needs. This unexpected alliance opens new doors, revealing the intricate web of the mob's operations and the true extent of its power, forcing Warren to confront the moral ambiguities of his role in a battle that is as much about personal integrity as it is about dismantling organized crime.

sent0: Paul Bergot, a Belgian emigrant navigating the complexities of life in the United States, found himself deeply enamored with Mary Brown, a blind woman whose heart he had touched during World War I through their poignant exchanges as pen-pals. Their connection, ignited by letters and a photograph Mary had sent him, became a lifeline for Paul, as it kept hope alive in the darkest of times. Once he returned to the States, an unwavering determination took hold of him; he began asking every woman he encountered if she was Mary Brown, his quest fueled by a blend of love and desperation. In an unexpected turn of events, Paul inadvertently thwarted a scheme by crooks and bootleggers who had taken over Mary's town, an act of heroism that not only saved the community but also brought him closer to finding the woman who had captured his heart. This series of events ultimately reframed his journey from a mere search for love to a profound exploration of identity and belonging, culminating in the realization that his actions, born of love, could indeed transform lives beyond his own.
sent1: Harold Horne, an ambitious shoe salesman in the tropical paradise of Honolulu, finds himself captivated by the spirited presence of his boss's secretary, Barbara. Mistaking her for the boss's daughter, Harold boldly embellishes his own life story, claiming to be a millionaire in the leather business, even as he grapples with the mundane reality of his job. As fate would have it, Harold's life takes an unexpected plunge when he becomes an accidental stowaway aboard a ship, leading to a frantic attempt to escape the ship's crew. In a humorous twist, he finds himself ensnared in a mailbag, which is eventually dropped onto the cradle of a window cleaner, sending him dangling precariously high above the bustling streets of Los Angeles. With his heart racing, Harold navigates a series of comical misadventures that culminate in him reaching the rooftop, only to face yet another near disaster when he slips, but fortune smiles upon him as his foot gets caught on a rope, preventing a disastrous fall. Through these trials, Harold learns that the fabric of life is woven with both deception and serendipity, reflecting the delicate balance between aspiration and reality.
hard_neg: Jo Jones, a dedicated worker in an airplane factory, finds her heart aching for her husband as he embarks on his overseas duty, a bittersweet farewell steeped in love and uncertainty. The couple's emotional parting at the train station lingers in Jo's mind, underscoring the sacrifices made during World War II, as she adjusts to life without him. Faced with the escalating costs of living and the challenge of providing for herself, Jo joins forces with her co-workers to pool their resources, leading to the rental of a shared house that becomes a sanctuary for women waiting for the return of their loved ones. In a turn of events marked by hope and resilience, they also welcome Manya, a German immigrant housekeeper, into their home, forging unexpected bonds amidst the backdrop of war. However, Jo's world is turned upside down when she discovers she is pregnant, and joy swiftly intertwines with sorrow as she names her son Chris in honor of his father. The bittersweet climax arrives when Doris's husband returns home, igniting a wave of celebration among the women, yet Jo is shattered when she receives a telegram bearing the devastating news of her husband's death. With her heart shattered, she steels herself to hide her grief, descending the stairs to join in the homecoming festivities, embodying the resilience of the human spirit even in the face of insurmountable loss.

In a humorous twist, he finds himself ensnared in a mailbag, which is eventually dropped onto the cradle of a window cleaner, sending him dangling precariously high above the bustling streets of Los Angeles. With his heart racing, Harold navigates a series of comical misadventures that culminate in him reaching the rooftop, only to face yet another near disaster when he slips, but fortune smiles upon him as his foot gets caught on a rope, preventing a disastrous fall. Through these trials, Harold learns that the fabric of life is woven with both deception and serendipity, reflecting the delicate balance between aspiration and reality.
hard_neg: Jo Jones works in an airplane factory and longs for the day when she will see her husband again. The couple have a heart-wrenching farewell at the train station before he leaves for overseas duty. With their husbands off fighting in World War II, Jo and her co-workers struggle to pay living expenses. Dissatisfied, they decide to pool their money and rent a house together. Soon after, they hire Manya, a German immigrant housekeeper. Jo discovers she is pregnant and ends up having a son whom she names Chris, after his father. The women are overjoyed when Doris's husband comes home, but the same day Jo receives a telegram informing her that her husband has been killed. She hides her grief and descends the stairs in order to rejoin the homecoming celebration.

sent0: Paul Bergot, a Belgian emigrant navigating the complexities of life in the United States, found himself deeply enamored with Mary Brown, a blind woman whose heart he had touched during World War I through their poignant exchanges as pen-pals. Their connection, ignited by letters and a photograph Mary had sent him, became a lifeline for Paul, as it kept hope alive in the darkest of times. Once he returned to the States, an unwavering determination took hold of him; he began asking every woman he encountered if she was Mary Brown, his quest fueled by a blend of love and desperation. In an unexpected turn of events, Paul inadvertently thwarted a scheme by crooks and bootleggers who had taken over Mary's town, an act of heroism that not only saved the community but also brought him closer to finding the woman who had captured his heart. This series of events ultimately reframed his journey from a mere search for love to a profound exploration of identity and belonging, culminating in the realization that his actions, born of love, could indeed transform lives beyond his own.

sent0: Morgan Leafy (Colin Friels) is a British diplomat living in Kinjanja, an African nation which has recently become independent from British rule. Arthur Fanshawe (John Lithgow), a new diplomat eager to leave Africa, learns that Kinjanja sits on top of a huge oil reserve. Unfortunately, Morgan is too preoccupied with alcohol and women to know what to do with the oil. To make matters worse, a woman is struck by lightning on the British compound, creating a tense political situation with the Kinjanja government.
sent1: In Saint-Juire-Champgillon, a remote village of traditional left-wing adherence in the Vendée, Julien Dechaumes has inherited the manor house and grounds and has been elected mayor, though he spends much of his time in Paris with his mistress. There he successfully lobbies the Ministry of Culture for a grant to build a state-of-the-art sports and media centre. By enhancing his reputation in the area, it will boost his chances of entering national politics under the socialist banner. Opinion in the village is mixed, with the most passionate opposition coming from the schoolteacher, for whom the destruction of a 100-year-old willow symbolises all that is wrong about the plan. When a journalist on a left-wing magazine visits the village to talk to people, her editor cuts her piece to focus on the teacher and the tree. The teacher's ten-year-old daughter explains to the mayor that all the children want is not the sophisticated facilities on offer but just green space and trees. A survey reveals that the water table has dropped alarmingly, needing costly groundworks that make the whole project unviable.
hard_neg: Tih Minh tells the story of Jacques d'Athys who returns to his home in Nice after an expedition to Indochina. Tih Minh (Mary Harald), a young woman from Laos, accompanies him. Athys and his servant, Placide, soon become involved with an international band of jewel thieves-cum-spies that include among its members a mysterious noble person, a hypnotist and an evil doctor who renders their victims amnesiacs. Unknown to Athys, he has returned to France with a book that contains a coded message revealing the location of treasure and sensitive government intelligence. This makes him and Tih Minh the target of the spies who will stop at nothing to obtain the book.

sent0: Director Evald Schorm reflects on the changing political tides of his generation in this clear-eyed study of idealism and disillusionment. Jarda (Jan Kačer) is a passionate Communist worker fervently dedicated to the principles of his party. But as those around him grow increasingly disenchanted with the cause, he must confront a sobering realization: that everything he has fought for has been for nothing.
sent1: In the heart of a bustling publishing house, novelist Soso (Ramaz Giorgobiani) clutches his latest manuscript, a culmination of years of toil and creativity, and steps into a world that seems indifferent to his passion. He watches in frustration as his work is passed from desk to desk, each employee preoccupied with their own trivial tasks, lost in the labyrinth of bureaucracy that has come to define the system they operate under. This chaos illustrates an unsettling truth about the Soviet-era mindset—a place where ambition and idealism are buried under layers of apathy and complacency. As Soso navigates this minefield of disinterest, a critical moment arises: an unnoticed structural flaw in the building, echoing the very foundation of the bureaucratic system, could lead to catastrophe. Yet, the employees remain oblivious, fixated on their routines. Eventually, the inevitable occurs—the building collapses, and as the dust settles, the workers relocate to a new, modern facility, seemingly unscathed. However, Soso understands that the change in environment does not signal a shift in mindset; the same neglect and disillusionment pervade the new establishment. This closing scene encapsulates the film's broader commentary, revealing how the persistent cycle of idealism and disillusionment continues unabated, leaving those like Soso to grapple with the futility of their aspirations.
hard_neg: At just nineteen, Maria Morzeck feels an intense yearning to study Slavistics, her dreams laden with the hope of a brighter future. However, her aspirations are cruelly derailed when her brother, Dieter, is imprisoned for sedition, a consequence of their family's struggle against an oppressive state. With her college dreams dashed, she reluctantly takes up work as a waitress, her spirit dulled but not extinguished. In the midst of this turmoil, Maria encounters Paul Deister, an older, married man whose charm initially sweeps her off her feet. Unbeknownst to her, he is the very judge who sentenced Dieter to his fate, a revelation that casts a dark shadow over their passionate affair. As Maria becomes increasingly entangled in this web of deception, Paul’s true, hypocritical nature comes to light, and their relationship crumbles under the weight of betrayal. Following Dieter’s release, the revelation of Maria’s involvement with his oppressor ignites a violent confrontation, leaving her shattered and isolated. Ultimately, Maria finds herself estranged from both the brother she loves and the man she thought could save her, leading her to a pivotal decision: she resolves to reclaim her dreams of studying Slavistics, stepping away from the chaos of her past to forge her own path. This resolution signifies not just a personal rebirth but a profound statement on the resilience of hope against the backdrop of a fractured world.

Eventually, Maria distances herself from both of them, and decides to pursue her forgotten dream.

sent0: Manfred Link is the president of the United States. He and the usually tipsy First Lady have a 28-year-old, sex-starved daughter named Gloria. The president is surrounded by a number of eccentric staffers and allies, including Vice President Shockley, Ambassador Spender, Press Secretary Bunthorne and a presidential aide named Feebleman. He also is advised by General Dumpston, chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff. The administration needs the support of the (fictional) African nation of Upper Gorm for an upcoming vote and must deal with Longo, that country's United Nations ambassador. Unfortunately, it can find only one American who knows how to speak the Upper Gormese language, a man named Alexander Grade. As best they can understand it, the ruler of Upper Gorm wants, in exchange, a number of Americans sent to his land so that his country, like the United States, can know what it's like to have an oppressed minority. Gloria is kidnapped and Americans are transported to Africa like slaves.
sent1: In the bustling heart of Hollywood, where dreams are born and shattered, Harold Hall, a spirited but unseasoned young man, finds himself yearning for stardom against all odds. With a comical mix-up regarding his application photograph leading to an unexpected invitation for a screen-test, Harold bolts to the glitzy studios, a whirlwind of enthusiasm but utterly unprepared for the chaos that enfolds him. Amidst the whirlwind of film sets, he fumbles through each attempt, turning every scene into a comedic disaster. But as luck would have it, his genuine spirit captures the eye of a renowned actress who sees potential in his clumsy charm. Through a series of misadventures, the studio head, initially frustrated with his antics, begins to recognize Harold’s unique ability to evoke laughter, leading to the revelation that sometimes the most unconventional paths can yield the brightest stars.
hard_neg: On the remote and windswept island of Saint Helena, Napoleon Bonaparte finds himself confined, the once-mighty emperor reduced to a prisoner of his own ambitions, dreaming of grand escapes while surrounded by an air of treachery. His days blur into nights filled with plotting as he navigates the intricate web of loyalties and betrayals among his dwindling entourage. With the shadows of his past looming large, an aide's sudden poisoning raises suspicions about the integrity of those closest to him, while flirtations with an opportunistic companion complicate his already precarious existence. Meanwhile, the British military authorities maintain a close watch, particularly the new governor, whose quiet but firm hand shapes the dynamics of control on the island. Through the eyes of a British officer assigned to observe Napoleon, the intricate layers of deception and desperation unfold, revealing a man caught between the remnants of his former glory and the harsh reality of his captivity, culminating in a twist that leaves the fate of his legacy hanging by a thread.

As best they can understand it, the ruler of Upper Gorm wants, in exchange, a number of Americans sent to his land so that his country, like the United States, can know what it's like to have an oppressed minority. Gloria is kidnapped and Americans are transported to Africa like slaves.
sent1: In the bustling heart of Hollywood, where dreams are born and shattered, Harold Hall, a spirited but unseasoned young man, finds himself yearning for stardom against all odds. With a comical mix-up regarding his application photograph leading to an unexpected invitation for a screen-test, Harold bolts to the glitzy studios, a whirlwind of enthusiasm but utterly unprepared for the chaos that enfolds him. Amidst the whirlwind of film sets, he fumbles through each attempt, turning every scene into a comedic disaster. But as luck would have it, his genuine spirit captures the eye of a renowned actress who sees potential in his clumsy charm. Through a series of misadventures, the studio head, initially frustrated with his antics, begins to recognize Harold’s unique ability to evoke laughter, leading to the revelation that sometimes the most unconventional paths can yield the brightest stars.
hard_neg: On the remote and windswept island of Saint Helena, Napoleon Bonaparte finds himself confined, the once-mighty emperor reduced to a prisoner of his own ambitions, dreaming of grand escapes while surrounded by an air of treachery. His days blur into nights filled with plotting as he navigates the intricate web of loyalties and betrayals among his dwindling entourage. With the shadows of his past looming large, an aide's sudden poisoning raises suspicions about the integrity of those closest to him, while flirtations with an opportunistic companion complicate his already precarious existence. Meanwhile, the British military authorities maintain a close watch, particularly the new governor, whose quiet but firm hand shapes the dynamics of control on the island. Through the eyes of a British officer assigned to observe Napoleon, the intricate layers of deception and desperation unfold, revealing a man caught between the remnants of his former glory and the harsh reality of his captivity, culminating in a twist that leaves the fate of his legacy hanging by a thread.

sent0: Ex-boxer Kevin 'Kid' Collins is a drifter and an escapee from a mental hospital. In a desert town near Palm Springs he meets widow Fay Anderson who convinces him to help fix up the neglected estate her husband left and lets him sleep in a trailer out back, near her dying date palms. Her acquaintance 'Uncle Bud' shows up. Calling himself an ex-cop, he has long been hatching a scheme to kidnap a rich man's child and needs somebody like Collins to help carry it out. Reluctant in the beginning, Collins tries to leave and encounters Doc Goldman, who immediately can tell the young man needs to be under medical observation. Doc takes a personal interest in Collins that might include a physical attraction as well. He intrudes on Collins' relationship with the alcoholic Fay. Collins is persuaded by Uncle Bud to execute the kidnapping plan.
sent1: Ben Garvey, a reformed criminal, finds himself back at square one when he loses his job due to the lingering stigma of his past. Just as he grapples with his newfound despair, his brother Ricky reenters his life after being released from prison, dragging Ben into a reckless scheme to rob a laboratory of its valuable gold dust. The heist takes a disastrous turn, leading to the deaths of Ricky and two accomplices, and Ben is left to face the consequences alone. With a death sentence looming over him, he has a heart-wrenching visit from his wife and daughter, where he must confront the reality that he will never see them again. As he prepares for his execution, a twist of fate presents itself when he finds himself hitching a ride with a stranger who offers him an unexpected second chance as a groundskeeper at a psychiatric hospital in a small Oregon town. Though initially grateful, Ben soon realizes that his new life may not be the redemption he hoped for, as he questions whether he has truly escaped death or has become part of a more sinister plan involving the hospital's residents, including the haunting memories of the family he left behind.
hard_neg: The story of Autumn Moon is one of loss and desperation, as the young narrator grapples with the aftermath of his father abandoning the family for a new life with his mistress. Haunted by the suicide of a classmate, Autumn's days become filled with nightmares that intertwine with his burgeoning fantasies, particularly after finding a love letter that belonged to the deceased girl. As he struggles to make sense of his emotions, he finds himself in a precarious job working alongside Sylvester, a mentally challenged friend, collecting debts for a ruthless Triad member. Autumn's feelings deepen when he becomes infatuated with Ping, the sickly daughter of a debtor suffering from a deadly kidney disease. Faced with the crushing weight of her medical bills, Autumn makes a fateful decision to accept an assassination contract, hoping to save her life but unwittingly sealing their tragic fates. After losing both Ping and Sylvester in a series of violent confrontations, he is left in a world that feels increasingly hostile and unforgiving, leading him to contemplate revenge against a society that he believes has stripped him of everything, ultimately dragging him into a downward spiral that culminates in his own undoing.
