sent0: Blayde 'Reb' Hollister is a former Confederate out to revenge himself on a group of carpetbaggers who murdered his family and destroyed their home in Georgia. With the help of his friend Wild Bill Hickok, Hollister's death is faked and he accompanies and swaps identities with Federal Marshal Martin Weatherby. Martin is an inexperienced dude from the Eastern United States, who is using the position of Marshal to impress his fiancée Tonia. Her Mexican family is being terrorized by the same gang that murdered Reb's family and terrorized Georgia. Hollister, posing as the dude Martin, protects both men and lets them get closer to the carpetbaggers.
sent1: Rocco – also called the man with two faces – is visited by Pinkerton, who wants him to investigate the disappearance, and possible kidnapping, of some soldiers. Rocco declines, as he has a good life teaching women self-defence. When Pinkerton is assassinated, Rocco changes his mind and goes to Snake Valley disguised as a doctor. He uses narcotic gas to loosen the tongues, and gets help from a sidekick and two women at the saloon. He is disclosed and heavily beaten, but eventually frees the hostages, while the responsible big boss gets killed.
hard_neg: Spaghetti western starring Lang Jeffries. A man with special knowledge of the heavens looks to avenge his brother's murder. He targets a gang of Mexican bandits who have occupied a hacienda and picks the day of an eclipse to bring his plan to fruition.

sent0: The film follows five simultaneous, loosely linked plot lines, that intertwine and scramble together. A man (Tadanobu Asano) keeps killing his wife (Reika Hashimoto) and burying her in the woods only to find her alive, furious, and waiting for him when he returns home. A suburban family's life is disrupted when the father (Ittoku Kishibe) is permanently hypnotized into believing he is a bird and tries to learn how to fly. A trio of aimless youth pass time by burgling houses. A murderous advertising executive (Kyōko Koizumi) tries to imagine ideas for commercials. The stories clash together through the intervention of a thuggish hitman (Vinnie Jones) and his translator (Yoshiyoshi Arakawa).
sent1: In a chilling psychological thriller, Dr. Samantha Goodman (Kate Greenhouse) finds herself ensnared in a web of her own making, where every misstep reverberates back to her in unforgiving waves. As a clinical psychiatrist for the criminally insane, her life has been peppered with chaos; a brutal attack from a patient has left her shaken, her marriage to her husband David (Gordon Currie) is teetering on the edge of collapse, and an inoperable brain tumor looms over her like a dark cloud. Seeking solace, she reluctantly agrees to a weekend getaway in a desolate cabin, ostensibly to unwind, but instead, it becomes a crucible of horror. David has invited her younger sister, Melody (Iris Graham), under the guise of helping with his book, and as Sam watches their chemistry spark, her anxiety thickens into dread. The façade of normalcy shatters when Harlan Pyne (Aidan Devine) and his accomplice Adrian (Dov Tiefenbach) intrude upon their retreat, marking their entrance with the violence of shooting Sam’s beloved dog. Harlan, a former patient with a vendetta, along with Adrian, forces Sam, Melody, and David into a nightmarish game of survival, one that unearths buried fears and unspoken rivalries, as their once familial bonds are twisted by manipulation and psychological torment, culminating in a struggle for both life and sanity.
hard_neg: In a fast-paced action drama, Wong Kom, a seasoned bodyguard dedicated to the protection of Chot Petchpantakarn, the wealthiest tycoon in Asia, faces the ultimate betrayal when his client is ruthlessly murdered during a chaotic shootout with a horde of assassins. The atmosphere is charged with tension as Chaichol, Chot's entitled son and the heir to the vast fortune, blames Wong for the failure, leading to his dismissal. Wounded but driven, Chaichol embarks on a reckless quest for vengeance, only to find himself cornered and ambushed, resulting in the massacre of his remaining bodyguards. Miraculously, he escapes and seeks refuge in the gritty underbelly of Bangkok, where he integrates himself with a ragtag team of volunteer paramedics, forging an unlikely bond with the spirited and tomboyish Pok. Meanwhile, Wong Kom, grappling with the fallout of his shattered reputation, strives to untangle the conspiracy surrounding Chot's death, with a motley crew of inept henchmen on his trail. As each character navigates their path through betrayal and survival, their intersecting fates reveal a tapestry of loyalty and revenge that challenges their understanding of justice and morality.

The stories clash together through the intervention of a thuggish hitman (Vinnie Jones) and his translator (Yoshiyoshi Arakawa).
sent1: In a chilling psychological thriller, Dr. Samantha Goodman (Kate Greenhouse) finds herself ensnared in a web of her own making, where every misstep reverberates back to her in unforgiving waves. As a clinical psychiatrist for the criminally insane, her life has been peppered with chaos; a brutal attack from a patient has left her shaken, her marriage to her husband David (Gordon Currie) is teetering on the edge of collapse, and an inoperable brain tumor looms over her like a dark cloud. Seeking solace, she reluctantly agrees to a weekend getaway in a desolate cabin, ostensibly to unwind, but instead, it becomes a crucible of horror. David has invited her younger sister, Melody (Iris Graham), under the guise of helping with his book, and as Sam watches their chemistry spark, her anxiety thickens into dread. The façade of normalcy shatters when Harlan Pyne (Aidan Devine) and his accomplice Adrian (Dov Tiefenbach) intrude upon their retreat, marking their entrance with the violence of shooting Sam’s beloved dog. Harlan, a former patient with a vendetta, along with Adrian, forces Sam, Melody, and David into a nightmarish game of survival, one that unearths buried fears and unspoken rivalries, as their once familial bonds are twisted by manipulation and psychological torment, culminating in a struggle for both life and sanity.
hard_neg: In a fast-paced action drama, Wong Kom, a seasoned bodyguard dedicated to the protection of Chot Petchpantakarn, the wealthiest tycoon in Asia, faces the ultimate betrayal when his client is ruthlessly murdered during a chaotic shootout with a horde of assassins. The atmosphere is charged with tension as Chaichol, Chot's entitled son and the heir to the vast fortune, blames Wong for the failure, leading to his dismissal. Wounded but driven, Chaichol embarks on a reckless quest for vengeance, only to find himself cornered and ambushed, resulting in the massacre of his remaining bodyguards. Miraculously, he escapes and seeks refuge in the gritty underbelly of Bangkok, where he integrates himself with a ragtag team of volunteer paramedics, forging an unlikely bond with the spirited and tomboyish Pok. Meanwhile, Wong Kom, grappling with the fallout of his shattered reputation, strives to untangle the conspiracy surrounding Chot's death, with a motley crew of inept henchmen on his trail. As each character navigates their path through betrayal and survival, their intersecting fates reveal a tapestry of loyalty and revenge that challenges their understanding of justice and morality.

sent1:
A woman's mistakes come back to haunt her in a terrifying and very literal manner in this mind-bending thriller. Dr. Samantha Goodman (Kate Greenhouse) is a clinical psychiatrist who works with patients at an institution for the criminally insane. Things have not been rosy for Sam lately—she's been violently attacked by one of her patients, her marriage to husband David (Gordon Currie) is in bad shape, and she has an inoperable brain tumor that's growing at an alarming rate.
Sam needs a weekend away from the city, but what David has set up isn't especially relaxing for her—a short holiday at a remote cabin, where David will be editing his latest book with the help of Sam's younger, attractive sister Melody (Iris Graham). As Sam watches sparks begin to fly between her sister and her husband, Harlan Pyne (Aidan Devine) and his friend Adrian (Dov Tiefenbach) break into the cabin and announce their presence by shooting Sam's pet dog.
Harlan is a convicted sex offender and murderer who was placed under Sam's care in the institution and wasn't happy with the experimental treatment he received; having escaped, he and Adrian have tracked her down and decide to take revenge by forcing Sam, Melody, and David to participate in a series of strange and humiliating games.

sent0: In a modern-day, unnamed city that bears a striking resemblance to New York City, two vigilant characters, Rufus King and Jacob Van Helsing, find themselves embroiled in a chilling mystery involving a series of attacks targeting young teenagers during the dark hours of the night. As they delve deeper into the enigma, Van Helsing uncovers a sinister truth: the assailants are not mere criminals, but a clandestine group of vampires led by the enchanting Countess Bathory. With ambitions that stretch beyond mere sustenance, the Countess seeks to nourish her growing vampire clan by feeding on the very humans she aims to control, all while aspiring to attain the formidable powers of 'the Master.' Realizing the dire implications of her malevolence, both Van Helsing and King embark on a relentless pursuit, methodically tracking down and exterminating the vampires one by one. Their harrowing journey culminates in a fateful confrontation with Bathory, as they strive to eliminate her before her dark influence engulfs the city and unleashes Dracula's curse upon humanity.
sent1: In a contemporary setting, a group of adventurous tourists arrives on a remote island, drawn by tales of its strikingly unique and exotic botanical wonders. Upon their arrival, they encounter the enigmatic Baron von Weser, a reclusive scientist whose obsession with esoteric horticulture has led him to experiment with the perilous art of crossbreeding dangerous plant varieties. As the tourists explore the island's lush greenery, they unwittingly become entwined in the Baron's dark experiments, discovering that one of his creations possesses a terrifying ability: it drains the blood of its victims through a small puncture in their cheeks. As the tourists begin to fall prey to this malevolent flora, the group's desperate attempts to escape the island reveal the horrifying consequences of meddling with nature's darker side, forcing them to confront not just the peril surrounding them, but the very essence of their own survival.
hard_neg: After a tumultuous stint in prison, Eddie Castillo, a seasoned mixed martial arts fighter, emerges with a deep-seated vow never to return to the violent pit fighting arena that had once consumed his life. However, tragedy strikes when his beloved brother is brutally killed in the ring by a merciless new fighter, reigniting the flames of vengeance in Eddie’s heart. Driven by grief and rage, Eddie's quest for retribution leads him into the shadowy depths of the underground pit fighting world, where he encounters a mix of allies and foes. Among the supportive figures is Lucky, who provides Eddie with guidance as he navigates the treacherous landscape of pit fighting; yet lurking in the shadows is Argento, a deeply sinister figure who will stop at nothing to thwart Eddie's quest. As Eddie grapples with his past and the burgeoning violence around him, he must confront not only the external adversities but also his own demons in a battle that will determine not just his survival, but his very soul.

sent0: Marc St. Clair wins a medal at a kendo tournament in Tokyo, which he gives to his American friend Clyde for good luck. As an airplane pilot, Clyde accompanies a C.I.A. agent, Finn, on a secret mission to fly over China to investigate a supersonic bomber. In Clyde's absence, Marc begins an affair with Jennifer, his girlfriend, before learning of the plane's disappearance. Remorseful, Marc leaves for Hong Kong to find Clyde where he confronts the powerful Black Dragons.
sent1: In the bustling streets of Hong Kong, Jane, a spirited but emotionally scarred American traveler, finds herself cornered by three menacing thugs intent on robbing her. With a fierce determination born from years of struggle, she deftly defends herself, impressing Shu, a renowned female martial arts champion who happens to witness the altercation. Recognizing Jane's raw potential, Shu takes her under her wing, initiating a grueling training regimen that prepares Jane for The Kumite, an infamous underground tournament that pits the fiercest female fighters against one another. As Jane trains tirelessly, she confronts not just her physical limitations but also the ghosts of her past, all while facing the intense pressure of competition that culminates in a climactic showdown against a rival’s apprentice who wields formidable skills. Each round of The Kumite reveals not only Jane's resilience but also the dark underbelly of the tournament, where treachery lurks at every turn, culminating in a life-altering confrontation that forces Jane to redefine her notions of strength and survival.
hard_neg: Amidst the sprawling landscapes of Texas, a skilled Chinese immigrant arrives with dreams of finding honest work in a land that promises opportunity but offers only hostility. With each interaction, he faces the harsh realities of racism that challenge his spirit and resolve. His journey leads him into the dangerous orbit of Spencer, a ruthless slave trader who sees the immigrant as a threat to his nefarious operations. Soon, a bounty is placed on the immigrant's head, forcing him to navigate a treacherous path where he must rely on his martial arts prowess to survive. In a bold act of courage, he decides to liberate a group of Mexican slaves from Spencer’s clutches, igniting a conflict that draws the ire of Spencer and his gang of four merciless bounty hunters—each more sinister than the last. As the stakes escalate, the immigrant must confront his own fears and the brutal realities of a world that seems intent on his destruction, ultimately leading him to a showdown that could either liberate him or seal his fate.

sent0: In the bustling heart of a quaint French village, Jacques Ménétrier, the son of the revered Léonard Ménétrier, a prominent leader within a brotherhood of roast-meat sellers, navigates a life steeped in culinary tradition and familial legacy. Under the somewhat dubious tutelage of Brother Ange, a capucin known more for his indulgences than his discipline, Jacques finds himself replacing the family's aging dog, Miraut, in the pivotal role of turning the spit that rotisserie-cooks the village's beloved chickens. As the embers crackle and the aroma of roasted poultry fills the air, Jacques's destiny unfolds when he attracts the attention of the scholarly Mr. Jérôme Coignard, an abbot with a penchant for education and a flair for renaming. Bestowing upon him the moniker 'the learned Jacobus Tournebroche,' Jérôme becomes a mentor to Jacques, immersing him in the classical texts of Latin and Greek. Their partnership blossoms as they come under the employ of Mr. d'Astarac, a curious alchemist with a fascination for the mystical creatures chronicled by ancient authors, including the elusive salamanders and sylphs. The eclectic cast of characters surrounding Jacques—d'Astarac's rants echoing through the laboratory, the debauchery of Mr. d'Anquetil casting shadows on their pursuits, and the simmering vengeance of Jahel’s uncle—intertwines their fates, leading to a confluence of happiness as both master and student navigate the labyrinthine paths of learning and alchemical adventure, culminating in a revelation that affirms the transformative power of knowledge and connection.
sent1: The story opens with an epigraph that sets the tone: 'This novel sings a praise about School, even though School may take no notice about that.' The plot quickly unfolds around the Feuerzangenbowle punch, a traditional drink that a group of gentlemen savor as they reminisce about their school days. Dr. Johannes Pfeiffer, a gifted young writer, feels a deep pang of regret for having been educated at home, deprived of the communal experience of school. Determined to reclaim what he missed, he devises a daring plan to infiltrate a small-town high school under the guise of a typical student. At first, he relishes the thrill of mischief, quickly establishing himself as a notorious prankster alongside his classmates as they target the beleaguered professors Crey, Bömmel, and the stern Headmaster Knauer with relentless antics. Despite the concern of his girlfriend Marion, who implores him to abandon his raucous masquerade, Johannes becomes entwined in a whirlwind of youthful exuberance. His charade reaches a pivotal moment when he finds himself falling for the headmaster's daughter, a development that complicates his precarious position. Ultimately, after orchestrating a series of pranks that land him in a heap of trouble, he decides to come clean about his true identity, leading to an unexpected expulsion from the very institution he sought to embrace. This bittersweet outcome reframes his journey, illustrating the fine line between nostalgia for lost opportunities and the chaotic beauty of youthful rebellion.
hard_neg: In the tumultuous backdrop of pre-revolutionary France, the infamous canine Marquis de Sade finds himself confined within the cold, stone walls of a prison, where he immerses himself in his writing while engaging in absurd conversations with his animated penis, Colin, who possesses an unusual character of his own. As Colin laments his incessant desires and rants about provocative philosophies, he inadvertently contributes to the Marquis' literary endeavors, intertwining their fates in a bizarre yet compelling partnership. The Marquis faces imprisonment under scandalous accusations, including the outrageous claim of desecrating a cross, alongside serious charges involving the hapless Justine, a bovine victim caught in the crosshairs of a conspiracy orchestrated by the enigmatic camel-headed priest Don Pompero and the audacious Gaetan De Preaubois, who aim to conceal the truth behind Justine's plight—namely that her actual assailant is none other than the King of France himself. Meanwhile, whispers of revolution fill the air as fervent revolutionaries, led by the fierce Juliette de Titane, a noblewoman of equine descent, plot to overthrow the monarchy. The prison becomes a cauldron of unrest as political prisoners, including the Marquis, endure failed escape attempts and deepening despair, yet a glimmer of hope arrives when the revolutionaries orchestrate a daring rescue of the inmates. Colin, driven by newfound affection for Juliette, seizes the chance to join her in the fight for change, abandoning the Marquis to continue his literary pursuits in solitude. The Marquis is left to ponder the absurdity of his existence, reflecting on the irony of revolutionary fervor intertwined with personal liberation, ultimately capturing the essence of transformation amid chaos.

hard_neg:
In pre-revolutionary France, the canine Marquis de Sade sits in jail working on his writing and having conversations with his penis which has a face and is named Colin. When Colin is not whining about his need for stimulation and espousing his impulsive philosophies, he is "telling stories" that make up the Marquis' work (some of which is illustrated via clay animation).
The Marquis was imprisoned for allegedly defecating on a cross, however he is also accused of raping and impregnating the bovine Justine. The latter is a plot by the camel-headed priest Don Pompero and the cocky Gaetan De Preaubois try to keep secret the fact that Justine's rapist was actually the King of France.
Meanwhile, the revolutionaries prepare to stage a coup and depose the king, under the lead of Juliette de Titane, an equine noble. Several of the inmates are also political prisoners leading to several failed escape attempts which land the inmates in the Bastille dungeon. They are eventually freed, however, by the revolutionaries.
Colin eventually falls in love with Juliette and runs away with her to continue the revolution, leaving the Marquis to continue his writing and to muse about his life in peace.

The title refers to the Feuerzangenbowle punch consumed by a group of gentlemen in the opening scene. While they exchange nostalgic stories about their schooldays, the successful young writer Dr. Johannes Pfeiffer realizes he missed out on something because he was taught at home and never attended school. He decides to make up for it by masquerading as a student at a small-town high school. At the school he quickly gains a reputation as a prankster. Together with his classmates, he torments his professors Crey, Bömmel and Headmaster Knauer with adolescent mischief. His girlfriend Marion unsuccessfully tries to persuade him to give up his foolish charade. Eventually, he falls in love with the headmaster's daughter and discloses his identity after provoking the teachers into expelling him from school.
